# commentable-html feature specification

commentable-html turns a standalone HTML artifact into a self-contained code-review surface. A reader can select text, code, diff lines, mermaid nodes or whole diagrams, headings, images, and charts, leave comments in the page, copy a machine-readable review bundle back to an agent, and let the agent mark processed comments handled in the same HTML file.

The core invariant is self-contained operation. Standalone mode embeds the CSS, state blocks, UI, and runtime in one file. NonPortable mode keeps the document-owned state inline but loads CSS and JS from local companion files in `dist/`; its export action rebuilds one portable file so the review can travel without companions. The review layer is bundled into the document. Optional host features, such as mermaid rendering, Chart.js, tooltip libraries, and Run in Azure Data Explorer links, can load from a CDN and should be vendored, inlined, or captured with Export Offline when single-file zero-network portability is required.

Current UI label note: the authoritative action label is `Export as Portable`. Some historical test names still say `Save comments`, `Save in HTML`, or `Export with embedded comments`.

Coverage notation: each row lists the strongest automated coverage or source citation I found. `manual/validator` means the behavior is a documented convention or a static validator invariant rather than a browser E2E behavior test.

| CMH-CODE-06 | A code/KQL block's `.cmh-code-gutter` stays vertically aligned to its source lines when a collapsed `<section>` containing it is revealed. The gutter lays out each `.cmh-code-line` at `N * lineHeight` where `lineHeight` comes from `getComputedStyle(target).lineHeight`; unlike a canvas chart's `clientWidth` (which is 0 while `display:none`, see CMH-CHART-09), the browser resolves a numeric line-height to a px value even for a hidden block, so the gutter is measured correctly at load and needs no reveal recompute - the offsets that were laid down while collapsed already match the visible line-height after reveal, and the gutter's total height matches the block's actually-rendered text height. Without this pin, a report/deck/theme whose ambient `line-height` is the keyword `normal` - or a direct `code { line-height: normal }` theme reset on the very `<code>` the gutter measures - would leave the computed value `"normal"`, `ensureCodeLineGutter()` would `parseFloat` it to `NaN` and fall back to a hardcoded `20px`, and the gutter numbers would sit above the text and drift down a tall block. The pin is scoped off the non-gutter `mermaid`/`diff` `<pre>` (their typography is untouched) and covers the `<code>` (as a descendant, matching the `pre.querySelector("code")` target even when the `<code>` is nested below the `<pre>`) at a specificity above a bare `code { line-height }` rule, so the computed line-height is never `normal` and the gutter's per-line step and cumulative bottom track the actually-rendered text line height and stay aligned. | `tests/24-copy-sort.spec.js` - `code and KQL line gutters stay aligned when the ambient line-height is 'normal' (CMH-CODE-07)`; source: `assets/css/70-kql.css` - `#commentRoot pre:not(.mermaid):not(.cmh-diff):not(.cmh-diff-raw)` line-height pin, `assets/js/` - `ensureCodeLineGutter()` |

The `Comment on diagram` button is clip-aware (a pure `positionMermaidWhole` positioner shares the `_clipAwareRect`/`_floatingBounds` path with the node add button, and the show wrapper owns the state/timer side effects): inside a bounded overflow container (for example a `.cmh-diagram-gallery` card, which clips with `overflow:hidden`) the button stays clamped to the card rather than clamping to the viewport. | `tests/26-ui-batch2.spec.js` - `whole-diagram comment: hover empty area, create, and it survives reload (http)`; `tests/76-diagram-gallery.spec.js` - `a gallery diagram is commentable and the whole-diagram button stays inside its card (CMH-CONTENT-19 / CMH-MMD-06)`; `tests/25-example.spec.js` - `the chart is commentable and mermaid gantt/flowchart nodes are commentable (served over http)`; source: `assets/js/` - `positionMermaidWhole()`, `showMermaidWholeFor()`, `MERMAID_RENDERED_SEL` |
| CMH-MMD-07 | A mermaid diagram authored inside a collapsible `<section>` that is collapsed (`display:none`) at load is NOT drawn in place by `mermaid.run()` - an in-place render measures a zero-size layout and produces a tiny, broken SVG (a degenerate `~16px` viewBox with the nodes collapsed on top of each other) that never re-measures. The live loader AND the Export Offline vendored-inline re-init render each visible diagram with `mermaid.run()` and each hidden diagram by running `mermaid.run()` on an OFF-SCREEN CLONE (a `position:fixed` `1000px` sandbox) and moving the rendered SVG into the real element - reusing mermaid's own source extraction (so `<br/>` labels, entities and indentation match a visible diagram) and error handling (a malformed diagram leaves no stray SVG in `<body>`). Every diagram is laid out correctly AT LOAD - correct the moment its section is revealed and also correct if the collapsed section is printed. Renders are serialized (mermaid shares internal state) so rendering many diagrams at once does not corrupt any of them, and one malformed diagram does not starve its siblings. Deck slides (hidden by `visibility:hidden`, not `display:none`) keep their existing eager `run()`. On reveal the layer recomputes a diagram's wide/scroll-fade class against its now-real container width (it was computed against a window-fallback while collapsed), and the loader exposes `window.__cmhMermaidReady` (a promise that settles once all renders finish) for print/screenshot automation. | `tests/04-comments-code-mermaid.spec.js` - `CMH-MMD-07: a report diagram in a collapsed section is rendered correctly at load, before and after reveal`, `CMH-MMD-07: many diagrams in collapsed sections all render correctly at load (serialized, no corruption)`, `CMH-MMD-07: a malformed diagram in a collapsed section leaves no stray render node and does not break siblings`, `CMH-MMD-07: a revealed collapsed diagram is commentable and its markdown source is preserved`, `CMH-MMD-07: a collapsed-section diagram is already rendered under print media (print-safe)`, `CMH-MMD-07: a wide diagram revealed from a collapsed section is classified like a visible twin`; `tests/49-offline-export.spec.js` - `CMH-MMD-07: Export Offline renders a collapsed-section diagram correctly at load with zero network`; source: `assets/template.shell.html` - mermaid loader; `assets/js/68-export-offline.js` - offline re-init (`renderHidden`, serialized `chain`); `assets/js/20-mermaid.js` - `updateMermaidWidthClass` reveal `ResizeObserver` |
| CMH-GANTT-01 | Gantt task labels and task bars are individually commentable via the mermaid hover layer; hovering a `.taskText` element shows the `Add Comment` button, saving creates an `anchorType: "mermaid"` comment with the label text, and the task is ringed. (Refinement of CMH-MMD-05 for the gantt diagram type with explicit E2E coverage.) | `tests/23-coverage.spec.js` - `CMH-GANTT-01: gantt task labels are commentable` |
| CMH-MMD-08 | On a deck (a `.deck-stage` document, whose slides render inside a CSS-scaled 1920x1080 stage), the mermaid loader initializes with `htmlLabels: false` so node/edge labels are SVG `<text>` rather than HTML in a `<foreignObject>`. HTML labels are re-laid-out by the browser against the stage's `transform: scale()` context, so a node box sized from the unscaled label measurement is too small and wider labels (for example `You comment on the exact spot`) clip mid-word; SVG `<text>` labels scale with the diagram and never re-flow, so every label stays fully visible inside its node box at the deck's scaled size. Non-deck documents (reports) keep the richer HTML labels (line breaks, entities). The same `!document.querySelector('.deck-stage')` choice is applied by the live loader and the Export Offline re-init, so it also holds after Export Offline and for a diagram slide hidden at load then revealed. Because mermaid splits a wrapped SVG label into per-line `tspan.text-outer-tspan` rows with no separator, `mermaidNodeLabel()` rejoins the rows with a space so the anchor key, the comment quote, and Copy all keep the wrap-point space (`exact spot`, not `exactspot`); and `findMermaidNode()` matches a `label:` anchor whitespace-insensitively so a comment saved before the HTML-to-SVG label change still re-attaches (keeps its ring/jump). | `tests/62-deck-regressions.spec.js` - `CMH-MMD-08: deck Mermaid node labels are fully visible inside their node box` (also asserts nodes and the labeled edge use SVG `<text>`, no `foreignObject`), `CMH-MMD-08: deck mermaid comment labels preserve spaces across SVG-text wrapping`, `CMH-MMD-08: a legacy deck mermaid anchor whose spacing differs re-attaches after the label mode change`; `tests/65-deck-exports.spec.js` - `CMH-DECK-EXPORT-01: Export Offline keeps the deck mermaid + chart live, validates strict, and reopens with zero network` (offline label-fit + SVG-label assertion for nodes and edges); source: `assets/template.shell.html` - mermaid loader (`htmlLabels` deck gate); `assets/js/68-export-offline.js` - offline re-init; `assets/js/20-mermaid.js` - `mermaidNodeLabel()` row rejoin, `findMermaidNode()` whitespace-insensitive fallback |
| CMH-MMD-09 | The mermaid loader bootstrap that CMH-MMD-08 changed is baked into the document `<head>` from `assets/template.shell.html` at scaffold time, OUTSIDE the swappable CSS / COMMENT UI / JS regions, so a region swap alone never reaches it. `tools/authoring/upgrade.py` therefore re-emits the shell-baked mermaid loader block (the `<head>` `<script type="module">` that boots mermaid via a dynamic mermaid `import(...)`, plus its immediately-preceding `<!-- Mermaid loader -->` comment) from the template, so a document generated before CMH-MMD-08 gains the `.deck-stage` / `htmlLabels: false` deck gate (and any future shell-bootstrap change) on upgrade. The lookup is scoped to `<head>` so an authored `<script type="module">` in the document body/CONTENT can never be mistaken for the loader (which would crash the upgrade or replace authored content), and it identifies the loader by a mermaid `import("...mermaid...")` in its body (not a specific selector string or a comment mention), so loaders from versions predating the `pre.mermaid, div.mermaid` guard are matched too while an authored head module that only mentions mermaid is not. The swap is document-kind-agnostic (the gate is a runtime `!document.querySelector('.deck-stage')` check, so a non-deck report keeps HTML labels) and idempotent (no bootstrap change is reported when it already matches the template), and `--check` flags a stale bootstrap. A hand-vendored offline loader (any mermaid `import(...)` with a local/relative specifier, per the SKILL.md vendoring recipe; a scheme-bearing or protocol-relative `//host` specifier is treated as remote) is left untouched so the swap never silently re-points mermaid back to the CDN. The Export Offline re-init is NOT shell-baked - it lives in `assets/js/68-export-offline.js` inside the swapped JS region - so it is refreshed by the existing JS region swap. The SHIPPED EXAMPLES have the same drift risk (their loader is in `<head>`, outside the swapped regions), so `build.py`'s `regen_example` ALSO re-emits the canonical loader from the freshly built `PORTABLE.html` into every example - the same `<head>`-scoped, mermaid-`import(...)`-keyed lookup - so an example single-sources the loader and can never ship a stale pre-CMH-MMD-07 loader (the old naive in-place `mermaid.run()`) that renders a collapsed-at-load diagram as a degenerate `~16px` SVG. That example re-emit is a hand-maintained MIRROR of the upgrade.py matcher (build's `_mermaid_loader_span()`/`_mermaid_loader_is_vendored()` mirror upgrade's `_mermaid_bootstrap_span()`/`_mermaid_loader_is_vendored()`), so a cross-implementation differential test asserts the two return identical spans and vendored classification on an ambiguous/vendored/decoy corpus (and both reject an ambiguous head, each with its own exception type), pinning the mirror so it can never diverge. The `dev/examples/src/*.html` loader blocks are BUILD-OWNED placeholders (`regen_example` overwrites them from the shell template on every build) rather than stale hand-authored loaders, so editing one there has no effect on the built example. Both matchers locate the `<head>` and the module-script candidates on a COMMENT-MASKED copy of the document (HTML comment interiors are blanked with offsets preserved), so a `<head>` or `<script>` inside an HTML comment before or around the real head is never mis-scoped as the head or the loader; the real loader's immediately-preceding `<!-- Mermaid loader -->` comment is still matched on the original text and stays part of the swapped span. An unterminated `<!--` (no closing `-->`) is masked through end-of-string, matching how an HTML parser treats it, so a loader inside an unclosed comment is inert too. The masking is HTML-state-aware: `<!--` opens a comment only in DATA state, so a `<!--` inside a tag (e.g. a quoted attribute value like `<meta content="&lt;!--"&gt;`) or inside a raw-text element body (`<script>`/`<style>`/`<textarea>`/`<title>`) is left intact, so it never blanks the real `</head>` or a real loader. | `tests/test_upgrade.py` - `test_upgrade_reemits_shell_mermaid_bootstrap_cmh_mmd_09`, `test_mermaid_bootstrap_noop_when_already_current`, `test_bootstrap_swap_is_scoped_to_head_cmh_mmd_09`, `test_bootstrap_swap_matches_legacy_loader_without_diagram_guard_cmh_mmd_09`, `test_bootstrap_swap_preserves_vendored_offline_loader_cmh_mmd_09`, `test_mermaid_loader_vendored_classification_cmh_mmd_09`, `test_bootstrap_swap_matches_loader_with_decoy_local_import_cmh_mmd_09`, `test_bootstrap_lookup_ignores_head_module_without_mermaid_import_cmh_mmd_09`, `test_bootstrap_ignores_commented_head_before_real_head_cmh_mmd_09`, `test_bootstrap_comment_scan_is_html_state_aware_cmh_mmd_09`, `test_corpus_snapshots_gain_deck_gate_after_upgrade_cmh_mmd_09`, `test_template_has_a_recognizable_mermaid_bootstrap_cmh_mmd_09`, `test_upgrade_reports_stale_bootstrap_in_check_mode`; `tests/test_examples.py` - `test_examples_single_source_the_canonical_mermaid_loader`, `test_span_ignores_head_module_without_mermaid_import_cmh_mmd_09`, `test_span_ignores_mermaid_import_in_attribute_cmh_mmd_09`, `test_span_ambiguous_multiple_head_loaders_raise_cmh_mmd_09`, `test_span_disambiguates_by_loader_comment_cmh_mmd_09`, `test_span_is_scoped_to_head_cmh_mmd_09`, `test_span_head_match_ignores_pre_head_header_comment_cmh_mmd_09`, `test_span_ignores_commented_head_before_real_head_cmh_mmd_09`, `test_span_ignores_unterminated_comment_head_cmh_mmd_09`, `test_span_ignores_comment_marker_in_tag_attribute_cmh_mmd_09`, `test_span_ignores_commented_module_inside_real_head_cmh_mmd_09`, `test_span_matches_legacy_loader_without_diagram_guard_cmh_mmd_09`, `test_vendored_classification_cmh_mmd_09`, `test_stamp_preserves_vendored_offline_loader_cmh_mmd_09`, `test_stamp_reemits_over_loader_with_decoy_local_import_cmh_mmd_09`, `test_stamp_reemits_canonical_over_build_owned_placeholder_cmh_mmd_09`, `test_src_example_loaders_are_build_owned_cmh_mmd_09`, `test_span_matchers_agree_on_corpus_cmh_mmd_09`, `test_vendored_classifiers_agree_on_corpus_cmh_mmd_09`, `test_both_reject_ambiguous_head_loaders_cmh_mmd_09`; source: `tools/authoring/upgrade.py` - `_mermaid_bootstrap_span()`, `_mask_html_comments()`, `_mermaid_loader_is_vendored()`, bootstrap re-emit in `upgrade()`; `tools/build_parts/30-examples.py` - `_mermaid_loader_span()`, `_mask_html_comments()`, `_mermaid_loader_is_vendored()`, `_stamp_mermaid_loader()` (a hand-maintained mirror of upgrade.py); `dev/examples/src/*.html` - build-owned placeholder loaders; `assets/template.shell.html` - mermaid loader |
| CMH-MMD-10 | In a non-deck report, a mermaid diagram whose natural width is well under the content column is marked `cmh-diagram-narrow` and scaled UP toward the column: `updateMermaidWidthClass` classifies with HYSTERESIS (enter when `natural/container < NARROW_ENTER` 0.82, stay until it exceeds `NARROW_EXIT` 0.90) so that scaling a diagram taller - which can toggle a document scrollbar and shrink the container on the reveal/resize `ResizeObserver` - cannot flip a diagram near the boundary back and forth, and exposes a capped target width `--cmh-diagram-cap` (`natural * NARROW_CAP` 1.4). The CSS (in `30-mermaid.css`, covering both `pre.mermaid` and `div.mermaid` hosts via `.cm-mermaid-host`) grows the SVG to `min(100%, var(--cmh-diagram-cap))` (centered), so the diagram uses the column while a tiny one never balloons and never overflows. A `wide` diagram (which scrolls) and deck diagrams (their own contain-fit) are excluded. This runtime scale-up fixes an intrinsically narrow diagram (Pattern A); a SPARSE layout whose SVG already fills the width but whose nodes are small with large internal gaps (Pattern B) cannot be scaled (the emptiness is inside the viewBox), so `references/mermaid-diagrams.md` documents the authoring fix (prefer `flowchart LR` for linear pipelines, keep architecture subgraphs dense with `LR` + `direction TB`, avoid long cross-subgraph edges and isolated nodes). | `tests/04-comments-code-mermaid.spec.js` - `CMH-MMD-10: a narrow report diagram scales up toward the content column, capped (not marooned)`, `CMH-MMD-10: a wide diagram is not classified narrow (no scale-up)`; `tests/test_docs_diagrams.py` - `MermaidLayoutGuidanceTests.test_mermaid_reference_documents_dense_layout_guidance`; source: `assets/js/20-mermaid.js` - `updateMermaidWidthClass` (`NARROW_ENTER`/`NARROW_EXIT`/`NARROW_CAP`), `assets/css/30-mermaid.css`, `skill/references/mermaid-diagrams.md` |

| CMH-DOCCMT-02 | A real desktop right-click (mousedown, mouseup, then contextmenu) on empty space keeps the document-comment menu open: neither a right-button `mouseup` (button 2) nor a macOS Ctrl-click gesture (a primary-button `mouseup` with `ctrlKey` held) runs the text-selection cleanup that would queue a `hideMenu()` and make the menu flicker open then vanish. | `tests/42-doc-comment.spec.js` - `a real right-click (mousedown, mouseup, then contextmenu) keeps the doc-comment menu open (CMH-DOCCMT regression)`, `a macOS Ctrl-click (mouseup button:0 ctrlKey, then contextmenu) keeps the doc-comment menu open (CMH-DOCCMT regression)`; source: `assets/js/` |
| CMH-ANCHOR-01 | The structural-anchor layers (image, mermaid, diff, link, widget, heading) each own a floating `Add Comment` button but share ONE active-affordance sentinel: every layer reveals its button through `setActiveAdd()`, which hides and clears whichever OTHER layer's button was showing, so overlapping targets never leave two buttons up at once (single active affordance at a time). For a NESTED target - the common clickable-thumbnail/logo `<a><img></a>`, where the image layer's `<img>` lives inside the link layer's `<a>` and hovering fires both - the INNERMOST element owns the affordance (the image wins over the wrapping link), deterministically and independent of hover-event order, so the reader sees exactly one button; the image's own commenting (ring, card, reload) is unaffected. The same rule holds for any cross-layer overlap (for example a link inside a heading -> the inner link wins). For a non-nested (sibling) overlap where neither target contains the other, the last affordance shown wins. The innermost-wins tie-break only defers to an inner affordance that is STILL SHOWING: each layer's hide timer clears the shared sentinel via `clearActiveAdd()` when it hides its own button, so a dismissed inner affordance never keeps suppressing the enclosing layer (hovering the enclosing target afterward re-shows its button). A build-time seam guard forbids any layer from assigning `_activeAdd` directly (it must route through `setActiveAdd()`), so a future anchor layer cannot silently reintroduce the two-buttons bug. Known limitation: because each layer reveals on a one-shot hover/focus, if the pointer leaves a nested inner target and lands on the enclosing outer target's own (non-inner) area, or re-enters the outer within the inner's brief hide grace window, the outer button may not re-arm until the next hover; this never shows two buttons and resolves on the next hover (the same single-active tradeoff as a mouse hover preempting a keyboard-focused affordance). | `tests/75-add-affordance.spec.js` - `a nested <a><img></a> shows exactly one affordance - the image, when the image fires first (CMH-ANCHOR-01)`, `a nested <a><img></a> shows exactly one affordance - the image, when the link fires first (CMH-ANCHOR-01)`, `showing one layer's add button hides another layer's (single active affordance) (CMH-ANCHOR-01)`, `the image affordance on a nested <a><img></a> still saves an image comment, not a link comment (CMH-ANCHOR-01)`, `a cross-layer overlap (a link inside a heading) also resolves to one affordance - the inner link (CMH-ANCHOR-01)`, `the outer affordance recovers after the inner one is dismissed - no stale-ghost suppression (CMH-ANCHOR-01)`; `tests/13-diff.spec.js` - `selecting sub-line text does not re-show the whole-line add button on a later mousemove (CMH-ANCHOR-01)`; `tests/test_active_add_seam.py` - `test_no_direct_active_add_object_assignment`, `test_only_owner_modules_assign_the_sentinel`, `test_every_revealing_layer_clears_on_hide`, `test_shared_helpers_exist`; source: `assets/js/20-mermaid.js` - `setActiveAdd()`, `clearActiveAdd()`; `assets/js/26-highlight.js`, `assets/js/30-images.js`, `assets/js/31-links.js`, `assets/js/35-widgets.js`, `assets/js/82-toc.js` - call sites and hide-path clears |

## Layered checklist

A `data-cmh-checklist` container is a hierarchical checklist. Any descendant carrying `data-cmh-state` or `data-cmh-item` is an item; an item with child items (nested lists, or table rows that name a parent via `data-cmh-parent`) is a branch that aggregates over its DIRECT children, otherwise a leaf. Leaf state cycles `blank -> check -> cross -> question -> blank`; a branch click propagates its next state to every descendant leaf. Only leaves whose state differs from their authored `data-cmh-state` baseline are stored, as one-character codes under `COMMENT_KEY + "::cl"`. Changes surface as one per-list card (jump + reset) placed by document order, a Copy-all section with `CHECKLIST_STATE_JSON`, and an export bake into `data-cmh-state`. Item identity is `data-cmh-item` when present, else the 1-based document-order position within the checklist; identity is never rendered (no visible numeration), so a checklist can live in a sortable table.

| Feature id | Behavior | Covering tests |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CMH-CHECK-01 | An authored checklist renders one injected `cm-skip` state control per item; the container gets `cmh-checklist-ready`, and each item is tagged `data-cmh-check-role="leaf"` or `"branch"` (a branch is an item with child items). | `tests/59-checklist.spec.js` - `CMH-CHECK-01: authored checklist renders controls; leaf vs branch detected` |
| CMH-CHECK-02 | Clicking a leaf control cycles its state `blank -> check -> cross -> question -> blank`. | `tests/59-checklist.spec.js` - `CMH-CHECK-02: a leaf cycles blank -> check -> cross -> question -> blank` |
| CMH-CHECK-03 | A branch's state derives from its direct children: all-same shows that state, any disagreement shows `mixed`; authored `data-cmh-state` on a branch is ignored. | `tests/59-checklist.spec.js` - `CMH-CHECK-03: a branch aggregates over its direct children (all-same, else mixed)` |
| CMH-CHECK-04 | Clicking a branch cycles it to its next state (from `mixed`, to `check`) and sets every descendant leaf to that state. | `tests/59-checklist.spec.js` - `CMH-CHECK-04: clicking a branch propagates its next state to all descendant leaves` |
| CMH-CHECK-05 | Only leaves whose state differs from the authored baseline are stored (minimal one-character-code deltas under `::cl`); cycling a leaf back to baseline prunes it, and states restore on reload. | `tests/59-checklist.spec.js` - `CMH-CHECK-05: only changed leaves persist (minimal delta), restored on reload` |
| CMH-CHECK-06 | Each checklist with changes renders one non-comment card with a Reset button that reverts that checklist to its authored baseline; the changed checklist is counted in the comment-count badge so a checklist-only change is not mistaken for no change (issue #643). | `tests/59-checklist.spec.js` - `CMH-CHECK-06: one card per changed list; Reset reverts that list to baseline` |
| CMH-CHECK-07 | A checklist change card is placed by document order among the comment cards (not pinned on top), using the checklist container's text position. | `tests/59-checklist.spec.js` - `CMH-CHECK-07: the checklist card is placed by document order among comments` |
| CMH-CHECK-08 | Copy all emits a `## Checklist "<id>"` section listing each changed item's `from -> to`, plus a machine-readable `CHECKLIST_STATE_JSON: {...}` line for the cementing tool. | `tests/59-checklist.spec.js` - `CMH-CHECK-08: Copy all includes a checklist section and CHECKLIST_STATE_JSON` |
| CMH-CHECK-09 | Exporting bakes each leaf's current state into its `data-cmh-state` attribute so the exported file reflects the new states. | `tests/59-checklist.spec.js` - `CMH-CHECK-09: export bakes current states into data-cmh-state` |
| CMH-CHECK-10 | A table-shape checklist builds its tree from `data-cmh-parent` references (rows cannot nest), and aggregation and top-down propagation work across those links. | `tests/59-checklist.spec.js` - `CMH-CHECK-10: table-shape parent links build the tree; aggregation and propagation work` |
| CMH-CHECK-11 | An item's label stays ordinary commentable content (a text comment on it creates a highlight); only the injected icon control carries `cm-skip`. | `tests/59-checklist.spec.js` - `CMH-CHECK-11: item labels stay text-commentable; the control is cm-skip` |
| CMH-CHECK-12 | The control is keyboard-operable (Enter/Space cycle it) and its `aria-label` announces the item label and current state. | `tests/59-checklist.spec.js` - `CMH-CHECK-12: keyboard cycles the control and ARIA announces the state` |
| CMH-CHECK-13 | `tools/checklist/checklist_apply.py` reads a `CHECKLIST_STATE_JSON` map (from a bundle or `--state-json`) and rewrites `data-cmh-state` on each named item (by `data-cmh-item` id or positional key) deterministically and idempotently, inserting the attribute when absent and skipping invalid tokens. | `tests/test_checklist_apply.py` - `ChecklistApplyTests` |
| CMH-CHECK-14 | `tools/checklist/checklist_scaffold.py` turns an indented outline into validator-clean list or table markup with stable slug ids, `data-cmh-parent` links for the table shape, per-item baseline states, and no `data-cmh-state` on branches. | `tests/test_checklist_scaffold.py` - `ChecklistScaffoldTests` |
| CMH-CHECK-15 | `validate.py` flags checklist authoring mistakes as strict-escalated warnings (duplicate container ids, invalid state tokens, an empty checklist, duplicate item ids, unresolved `data-cmh-parent`), and is a no-op for a checklist-free document. | `tests/test_validate_checklist.py` - `ChecklistValidateTests` |
| CMH-CHECK-BADGE | An unsaved checklist state change flips the document badge to Not portable with a reason naming the checklist, until the file is re-exported. | `tests/59-checklist.spec.js` - `CMH-CHECK-BADGE: an unsaved checklist change flips the badge to Not portable` |
| CMH-CHECK-16 | A table-row item's label is taken from its first content cell (the cell after the state cell), not a concatenation of every remaining cell, so extra columns like an Owner do not leak into the change record or the Copy-all bundle. | `tests/59-checklist.spec.js` - `CMH-CHECK-16: a table item's label is its first content cell, not every other cell` |
| CMH-CHECK-17 | A document that loads already having a persisted checklist change opens the comments sidebar at startup (not only on the live 0 -> >0 change transition), so the per-list change card is visible instead of hidden in a closed panel. | `tests/59-checklist.spec.js` - `CMH-CHECK-17: a checklist that loads with a persisted change opens the sidebar so the card is seen` |
| CMH-CHECK-18 | Clear restores every changed checklist item to its authored `data-cmh-state` baseline, removes the checklist change card, and prunes the persisted `::cl` state. | `tests/59-checklist.spec.js` - `CMH-CHECK-18: Clear restores checklist state changes to the authored baseline`; source: `assets/js/` - `resetAllChecklists()`, Clear handler |

## Editable notes fields

A `data-cmh-note="<id>"` element is a single editable free-text field. The runtime replaces its
authored text with a `<textarea>` (with a single/multi-line toggle) whose baseline is the authored,
canonically-normalized `textContent`. Only a note whose current text differs from baseline is stored,
as `{id:text}` under `COMMENT_KEY + "::note"`; edits surface as one per-note change card (jump + reset)
placed by document order, a Copy-all section with `NOTES_STATE_JSON`, and an export bake into the
element's text. The field is `cm-skip` (editing never creates a highlight) and is set up before offset
restoration so its excluded text does not shift comment offsets. The one canonical normalizer (LF line
endings, trimmed outer whitespace) is implemented identically in the runtime and in
`tools/notes/notes_apply.py` so the browser and the cementing tool agree.

| Feature | Behavior | Test |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CMH-NOTE-01 | An authored `data-cmh-note` element upgrades to an editable `<textarea>`; the container gets `cm-skip` and `cmh-note-ready`, seeded with the authored text, alongside a label chip and a single/multi-line toggle. | `tests/62-notes.spec.js` - `CMH-NOTE-01: authored note upgrades to an editable textarea (cm-skip, ready, toggle)` |
| CMH-NOTE-02 | An edit stores a minimal delta (only when the normalized text differs from baseline) under `::note`; it restores on reload, and editing back to the exact baseline prunes the entry. | `tests/62-notes.spec.js` - `CMH-NOTE-02: an edit persists a minimal delta, restores on reload, and prunes at baseline` |
| CMH-NOTE-03 | A per-note toggle switches the field between single-line and multi-line (row count). | `tests/62-notes.spec.js` - `CMH-NOTE-03: the single/multi-line toggle switches the field height` |
| CMH-NOTE-04 | Each changed note renders one non-comment card, placed by document order among the comment cards, and is counted in the comment-count badge (a changed note alone shows 1) so a note-only change is not mistaken for no change (issue #643). | `tests/62-notes.spec.js` - `CMH-NOTE-04: a changed note renders one non-comment card by document order and is counted` |
| CMH-NOTE-05 | The card's reset reverts the field to its authored baseline and prunes the delta. | `tests/62-notes.spec.js` - `CMH-NOTE-05: reset reverts the note to its authored baseline` |
| CMH-NOTE-06 | The global Clear all comments also reverts every changed note to its authored baseline and prunes the `::note` state, and it does so even when a note edit is the only pending change (no comment, checklist, or widget change present) - the Clear all guard treats a note-only change as clearable, and the confirm names the note/checklist/widget resets. | `tests/62-notes.spec.js` - `CMH-NOTE-06: Clear all comments also reverts note edits to baseline`, `CMH-NOTE-06: Clear all reverts a note-only change even with no comment present` |
| CMH-NOTE-07 | An unsaved note edit flips the document badge to Not portable with a reason naming the note, until the file is re-exported. | `tests/62-notes.spec.js` - `CMH-NOTE-07: an unsaved note edit flips the badge to Not portable, naming the note` |
| CMH-NOTE-08 | Copy all emits a `## Note "<id>"` section with the from/to text plus a machine-readable `NOTES_STATE_JSON: {...}` line. | `tests/62-notes.spec.js` - `CMH-NOTE-08: Copy all includes a Note section and NOTES_STATE_JSON` |
| CMH-NOTE-09 | Every export bakes the current note text into the element (via `textContent`, never `innerHTML`) with no runtime editing attributes, so the exported source carries the edit safely. | `tests/62-notes.spec.js` - `CMH-NOTE-09: export bakes note text into the source element with no editing attributes` |
| CMH-NOTE-10 | A changed note is searchable: its change card filters by the note label and text like a comment card (rather than being hidden while a query is active). | `tests/62-notes.spec.js` - `CMH-NOTE-10: a changed note is searchable in the sidebar` |
| CMH-NOTE-11 | A document that loads already having a persisted note edit opens the comments sidebar at startup so the change card is visible. | `tests/62-notes.spec.js` - `CMH-NOTE-11: a note that loads with a persisted edit opens the sidebar` |
| CMH-NOTE-12 | The note field is `cm-skip`: selecting or typing in it never creates a highlight comment. | `tests/62-notes.spec.js` - `CMH-NOTE-12: the note field is cm-skip; editing it never creates a highlight` |
| CMH-NOTE-E2E | The full reviewer-edit round-trip: an in-browser edit produces a Copy-all bundle whose `NOTES_STATE_JSON` `tools/notes/notes_apply.py` cements into the source file, and reopening the cemented file (keeping the stale storage override) shows the baked text, no pending change, and a Portable badge. | `tests/62-notes.spec.js` - `CMH-NOTE-E2E: reviewer edit -> Copy all -> notes_apply.py -> reopen shows the cemented source` |
| CMH-NOTE-13 | `tools/notes/notes_apply.py` cements each `NOTES_STATE_JSON` value into its `data-cmh-note` element's inner content (HTML-escaped, newline-style preserved) deterministically and idempotently, from a bundle or `--state-json`, filling an empty element and skipping unknown ids. | `tests/test_notes_apply.py` - `NotesApplyTests` |
| CMH-NOTE-14 | `tools/notes/notes_scaffold.py` emits validator-clean `data-cmh-note` markup with a stable id, an optional label, escaped text, an optional multi-line default, and an optional foldable flag. | `tests/test_notes_scaffold.py` - `NotesScaffoldTests` |
| CMH-NOTE-15 | `validate.py` flags notes authoring mistakes as strict-escalated warnings (duplicate id, empty id, a nested note, a void-element note, a note with child elements, a note inside a checklist/diff/widget substrate), and is a no-op for a notes-free document. A note anywhere on a deck slide (or `.deck-stage`) is NOT flagged - live notes on a slide are a supported pattern (used by the showcase deck, where the notes sit inside slide layout wrappers) - because a slide, unlike a checklist/diff/widget, does not mark its descendant text `cm-skip`; a note nested inside a checklist/diff/widget that sits on a slide is still flagged. | `tests/test_notes_validate.py` - `NotesValidateTests` |
| CMH-NOTE-16 | A `data-cmh-note-foldable` note is a disclosure: a `+`/`-` toggle on the header line reveals or hides the field on the line below. It starts collapsed only when empty (a note with content, authored or a persisted edit, starts expanded); the fold state is session-only, evaluated once at load, so a manual collapse sticks. The toggle is an accessible `<button>` (`aria-expanded`, `aria-controls`), a collapsed note that still holds content is badged, `jumpToNote` expands a collapsed note before focusing it, and the collapse never reaches storage or the exported source. | `tests/62-notes.spec.js` - `CMH-NOTE-16: a foldable note starts collapsed only when empty and toggles with the +/- control`, `CMH-NOTE-16: keyboard toggles the fold, and a collapsed note with content shows a badge`, `CMH-NOTE-16: a foldable note with a persisted edit auto-expands on reload; jump expands a collapsed note`, `CMH-NOTE-16: fold state is presentation only - the export keeps data-cmh-note-foldable but no runtime collapse` |
| CMH-NOTE-17 | Typing in a note field coalesces the sidebar re-render: because `renderComments()` runs full-document text-node and section scans, a burst of keystrokes schedules a single debounced render (a note's document position does not move while its text is edited) instead of one scan per keystroke, so typing does not freeze a large document. The edit is persisted synchronously on every keystroke and the lightweight UI (portability badge, Copy-all affordance, first-change sidebar auto-open) updates synchronously on the dirty-state transition (never per keystroke, so a burst does no per-key document scan), so an export or Copy all fired mid-burst reflects the latest text; only the sidebar-list card is deferred, rendering once the reviewer pauses. Programmatic reset / clear-all still render immediately. | `tests/62-notes.spec.js` - `CMH-NOTE-17: typing coalesces the sidebar re-render (one debounced render per burst, not one per keystroke)`, `CMH-NOTE-17: a Copy all during the debounce window reflects the latest text and the badge updates synchronously` |

## Copy-all bundle and handled-id contracts

| Feature id | Behavior | Covering tests |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CMH-COPY-01 | Copy all emits a Markdown bundle with document label, `Source`, ordered comments, agent instructions, and a final `HANDLED_IDS_JSON` array. | `tests/05-copy-handled.spec.js` - `Copy all emits the markdown bundle with an exact HANDLED_IDS_JSON line`; `tests/12-features.spec.js` - `the bundle carries the full documented contract for a text comment` |
| CMH-COPY-02 | Text-comment bundle entries include `Where`, `Pinpoint`, offsets, quoted text, optional context, containing block, and the user's note. | `tests/12-features.spec.js` - `the bundle carries the full documented contract for a text comment`, `a comment under headings surfaces Where / Pinpoint / In context metadata` |
| CMH-COPY-03 | Copy all uses the same bundle from toolbar and sidebar, and it has anchor-specific payloads for code, mermaid, diff, and image comments. | `tests/12-features.spec.js` - `the top toolbar Copy all copies the same bundle (positive path)`, `a code comment emits a fenced block and omits the prose-only context fields`, `a mermaid comment produces a mermaid Copy-all payload and its ring activates the card`; `tests/13-diff.spec.js` - `Copy all for a region comment quotes only the selected substring, not the whole line`, `Copy all emits the diff anchor and the quoted diff line`, `Copy all for a diff comment includes the HANDLED_IDS_JSON contract and 'added line N'`; `tests/14-image.spec.js` - `Copy all emits an image anchor with the alt text` |
| CMH-COPY-04 | Copy all with no copyable comments or tracked state changes is disabled, copies nothing, and exposes a `No comments to copy` tooltip instead of running the copy path. | `tests/12-features.spec.js` - `Copy all is disabled with zero comments and re-enabled after a comment exists (CMH-COPY-04, CMH-COPY-07)` |
| CMH-COPY-05 | Copy all clipboard writes use `navigator.clipboard` first, then `execCommand`, then a `prompt` fallback. When the user cancels the prompt the toast must not claim success - it must describe what actually happened. | `tests/12-features.spec.js` - `Copy all falls back to execCommand when the async clipboard is unavailable`, `Copy all falls back to prompt() when both clipboard paths fail`; `tests/36-a11y.spec.js` - `Copy all does NOT claim success when it falls back to a manual prompt` |
| CMH-COPY-06 | Generic per-block clipboard actions (code-block Copy buttons and `data-cmh-copy` chips) use `navigator.clipboard` then `execCommand`; if both fail they show a `Copy failed` toast and do NOT open a `prompt` (only Copy all has the prompt fallback). | `tests/38-spec-gaps.spec.js` - `per-block copy failures show Copy failed without opening prompt (CMH-COPY-06)`; source: `assets/js/` - code-block copy and `data-cmh-copy` handlers |
| CMH-COPY-07 | Copy all controls expose `aria-disabled="true"` and the tooltip `No comments to copy` when there is no copyable review state, stay keyboard-focusable for the same tooltip, re-enable once a comment exists, and return to disabled after Clear removes the last comment. | `tests/12-features.spec.js` - `Copy all is disabled with zero comments and re-enabled after a comment exists (CMH-COPY-04, CMH-COPY-07)`; source: `assets/js/` - `updateCopyAllState()` |
| CMH-COPY-08 | Copy all treats reviewer notes, document text, the document label, and the document source as untrusted: it strips Unicode bidi/format controls (`U+202A-U+202E`, `U+2066-U+2069`, `U+200E/U+200F`) from human-readable payload text, and escapes those controls in machine-readable JSON identifiers so apply tools still parse the original ids without raw invisible controls in the bundle. Each free-text note is otherwise wrapped in a dynamic, tilde-sized `UNTRUSTED REVIEWER NOTE` fence (longer than any tilde run in the note), a read-first AGENT INSTRUCTIONS block frames notes as document-scoped change requests (data, not agent/system instructions), and `data-doc-source` is emitted single-line with backticks neutralized so an embedded newline cannot forge a standalone machine line and a backtick cannot close the Markdown code span it sits in. | `tests/05-copy-handled.spec.js` - `forged trailer/instruction lines in a note or doc-source are neutralized (CMH-COPY-08/09)`; `tests/05-copy-handled.spec.js` - `bidi formatting controls in notes, document text, and metadata are stripped from Copy all (CMH-COPY-08)`; `tests/05-copy-handled.spec.js` - `the untrusted-note fence tilde run outgrows a long tilde run in the note (CMH-COPY-08)`; `tests/12-features.spec.js` - `the bundle carries the full documented contract for a text comment` |
| CMH-COPY-09 | All machine-readable JSON (`HANDLED_IDS_JSON`, `NOTES_STATE_JSON`, `CHECKLIST_STATE_JSON`) is emitted only inside ONE unconditional final `=== CMH MACHINE TRAILER (do not edit) ===` block (canonical empty values when there are no changes); the three Python apply tools read JSON only from within that fenced trailer (last open marker) and FAIL CLOSED when the opening marker has no matching END marker, so a forged trailer/state line in a note body or doc-source is ignored. | `tests/05-copy-handled.spec.js` - `forged trailer/instruction lines in a note or doc-source are neutralized (CMH-COPY-08/09)`; `tests/test_notes_apply.py` - `test_from_bundle_reads_only_the_machine_trailer`, `test_from_bundle_ignores_forged_line_when_no_changes`, `test_from_bundle_fails_closed_on_unclosed_trailer`; `tests/test_checklist_apply.py` - `test_from_bundle_ignores_forged_line_outside_trailer`, `test_from_bundle_ignores_forged_line_when_no_changes`, `test_from_bundle_fails_closed_on_unclosed_trailer`; `tests/test_mark_handled.py` - `test_bundle_parsing_uses_last_authoritative_contract`, `test_bundle_parsing_fails_closed_on_unclosed_trailer` |
| CMH-HANDLED-01 | The agent treats the `HANDLED_IDS_JSON` line inside the final `=== CMH MACHINE TRAILER (do not edit) ===` block as the machine-readable contract and appends those ids to `<script id="handledCommentIds">`; a forged `HANDLED_IDS_JSON` line elsewhere in the bundle is ignored. | `tests/test_mark_handled.py` - `test_bundle_parsing`, `test_bundle_parsing_uses_last_authoritative_contract`, `test_cli_from_bundle_stdin` |
| CMH-HANDLED-02 | On load, ids in `handledCommentIds` are pruned from memory and `localStorage`, and matching highlights or rings disappear. | `tests/05-copy-handled.spec.js` - `marking an id handled prunes it on reload and from Copy all (via mark_handled.py)`; `tests/04-comments-code-mermaid.spec.js` - `a mermaid comment prunes on reload once marked handled` |
| CMH-HANDLED-03 | Copy all filters handled comments even if storage still contains them, so handled ids never reappear in the bundle or count. | `tests/05-copy-handled.spec.js` - `marking an id handled prunes it on reload and from Copy all (via mark_handled.py)`; `tests/12-features.spec.js` - `a handled mermaid comment is excluded from Copy all after reload` |
| CMH-HANDLED-04 | `mark_handled.py` edits only the handled-id JSON block, dedupes ids, validates id shape, preserves order and newline style, and refuses ambiguous duplicate blocks. | `tests/test_mark_handled.py` - `test_only_the_block_changes`, `test_dedupes_and_preserves_order`, `test_rejects_unsafe_id_without_writing`, `test_preserves_crlf`, `test_rejects_multiple_real_blocks`, `test_cli_appends_and_is_idempotent` |

## Export, embedded comments, and output modes

| Feature id | Behavior | Covering tests |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CMH-EXP-01 | `Export as Portable` downloads `<stem>-portable.html`, strips any existing `-comments` or `-portable` suffix before adding it, and writes current comments into `<script id="embeddedComments">` with `<` escaped as `\u003c`. | `tests/06-save-export.spec.js` - `a comment note with a closing-script tag is escaped and round-trips decoded`, `Save comments embeds the comment into the downloaded copy`; `tests/28-duck-fixes.spec.js` - `Export as Portable does not stack suffixes on a *-comments.html file`; source: `assets/js/` - `_suggestedFilename()` |
| CMH-EXP-02 | A downloaded copy with embedded comments opens in a fresh browser and shows those comments without relying on existing `localStorage`. | `tests/06-save-export.spec.js` - `embedded comments travel: a shared copy shows them in a fresh browser (no localStorage)`; `tests/34-modes.spec.js` - `MODE Portable-with-comments: Save embeds comments that travel to a fresh browser and stay Portable` |
| CMH-EXP-03 | On load, embedded comments merge into localStorage by id; the item with the later `updatedAt`, or fallback `createdAt`, wins, and disjoint ids are preserved. | `tests/10-more.spec.js` - `embedded comments merge by latest updatedAt (both directions)`; source: `assets/js/` - `mergeCommentSets()`, `loadComments()` |
| CMH-EXP-04 | `Export to Plain HTML` removes handled ids, embedded comments, the real `commentableHtmlLayer` descriptor, comment UI, and runtime JS while preserving host content, shipped content styling, localStorage, and host scripts that only carry a decoy `data-id="commentableHtmlLayer"` attribute. | `tests/06-save-export.spec.js` - `Export plain strips the layer but keeps the content`; `tests/12-features.spec.js` - `Export plain unwraps highlights, drops sidebar-open, keeps content, and leaves localStorage intact`; `tests/10-more.spec.js` - forged marker no leak, `Export plain ignores a data-id commentableHtmlLayer decoy script` |
| CMH-EXP-05 | In nonportable mode, Plain HTML keeps the CSS companion link for styling and removes only the runtime companion and comment DOM. | `tests/10-more.spec.js` - `Export plain in nonportable mode strips the JS companion but keeps the CSS companion and content` |
| CMH-EXP-06 | On `file://` (where `fetch()` of the page is blocked) every export falls back to a DOM snapshot as its base. The snapshot is captured before any runtime mutation but is completed at export time with the host content parsed after the layer `<script>` (per charts-embedding.md, chart data and init scripts placed after the `END: commentable-html - JS` marker), so a chart or other tail content is never dropped from a file:// Portable or Plain export. The recovered tail excludes layer-injected chrome (footer, side-TOC, scroll progress, tooltips - all `cm-skip`) so that chrome never leaks into an export. | `tests/45-file-export-tail.spec.js` - `Export as Portable keeps a chart placed after the JS marker`, `Export as Plain keeps a chart placed after the JS marker`; `tests/20-ui-chrome.spec.js` - `the TOC side menu and Help modal never leak into a Plain HTML export`, `the TOC side menu and Help modal never leak into an Export with embedded comments`; `tests/22-footer-show.spec.js` - `the footer does not leak into a Plain HTML export`, `the Report an issue link does not leak into a Plain HTML export (CMH-FOOT-05)`, `the brand site link does not leak into a Plain HTML export`, `the copy-session control does not leak into a Plain HTML export (CMH-FOOT-04)`; source: `assets/js/` - `_snapshotWithTail()`, `CMH_LAYER_SCRIPT` |
| CMH-EXP-07 | The embedded-comments block is located by a real, whitespace-delimited `id="embeddedComments"` attribute regardless of the remaining attribute order or spacing, so a document authored or re-saved as `<script id="embeddedComments" type="application/json">` still receives the current comments on export. A decoy `data-id="embeddedComments"` or `aria-id="embeddedComments"` on another script is never mistaken for the real block, so the export cannot update the wrong script and leave real comments stale. | `tests/47-hardening.spec.js` - `embedded-comments script is found regardless of attribute order (CMH-EXP-07)`, `export updates the real embeddedComments block, not a decoy data-id script (CMH-EXP-07)`; source: `assets/js/` - `_buildSavedHtml()` |
| CMH-EXP-08 | Save, Portable, and Offline exports filter the canonical export comment set through `handledCommentIds`, matching Copy all: a comment already marked handled never reappears in embedded export JSON even if it is still present in live memory. | `tests/06-save-export.spec.js` - `Save, Portable, and Offline exports exclude comments already listed as handled`; source: `assets/js/` - `_exportableComments()` |
| CMH-EXP-09 | Every export path (Save, Portable, Offline, and Plain) normalizes the base HTML by stripping the transient runtime body-state classes (`sidebar-open`, `cm-sidebar-resizing`, `cm-widget-dragging`) from the `<body>` open tag, so a stale or open-sidebar source never bakes that state into a saved or exported file (a persisted `sidebar-open` would render the content full width with an empty sidebar gutter via the `body.sidebar-open .app` layout rule). The normalizer touches only the FIRST `<body>` open tag (a `<body class=...>` literal inside a later script/content is left alone), handles double-quoted, single-quoted, and unquoted class values, matches whole class tokens (a superstring like `x-sidebar-open` is preserved), and removes an emptied class attribute. Non-transient body classes are preserved; the live layer re-derives the sidebar state on load. | `tests/54-export-body-normalize.spec.js` - `Save/Portable export drops sidebar-open and other transient body classes (CMH-EXP-09)`, `Offline export drops sidebar-open and other transient body classes (CMH-EXP-09)`, `Plain export drops sidebar-open and other transient body classes (CMH-EXP-09)`, `Portable export from a nonportable file (snapshot fallback) drops transient body classes (CMH-EXP-09)`, and the `Body-class normalizer unit (CMH-EXP-09)` suite (`single-quoted transient class is stripped to the kept classes (CMH-EXP-09)`, `a <body class> literal after the real body (in a script) is not mutated (CMH-EXP-09)`, `a superstring host class like x-sidebar-open is preserved (CMH-EXP-09)`, `the existing double-quoted case still strips only the transient token (CMH-EXP-09)`, `an emptied class attribute is removed and unquoted values are handled (CMH-EXP-09)`); source: `assets/js/` - `_stripTransientBodyClasses()`, `_getBaseHtml()`, `_buildPlainHtml()` |
| CMH-EXP-10 | `Export as Portable` preserves a comment note verbatim even when it contains a `$`-replacement pattern (`$&`, `$1`, `$$`, and the dollar-backtick and dollar-quote forms): the saved-HTML builder splices the embedded-comments region with a function replacer, so `String.replace` never expands those patterns from the note text and the stored JSON round-trips exactly on reload. | `tests/55-export-dollar-and-markers.spec.js` - `Export as Portable preserves a comment note containing $-replacement patterns (F2)`; source: `assets/js/` - `_buildSavedHtml()` |
| CMH-EXP-11 | `Export to Plain HTML` strips a comment-data region whose banner uses the compact single-line marker form (no `===` decoration) that the validator's `=*` marker grammar also accepts, so a compact-markered document exports to plain cleanly without tripping the leak-guard safety net; the leak guard itself matches `id="handledCommentIds"`/`id="embeddedComments"` regardless of quote style or surrounding whitespace. | `tests/55-export-dollar-and-markers.spec.js` - `Export as Plain strips a compact-markered EMBEDDED COMMENTS region (F3)`; source: `assets/js/` - `_buildPlainHtml()` |
| CMH-EXP-12 | The export path (a proven bug hot spot) is guarded by a property/fuzz test and a cross-implementation grammar test: a batch of adversarial comment notes (every `$`-replacement pattern, escaped `<`, `<!--`, `</script`, unicode, backticks, and literal region-marker phrases) each round-trips byte-exact through Export as Portable and `JSON.parse` with no expansion, escaping loss, truncation, or forged region boundary; and Export to Plain strips compact-markered comment-data regions across all of HANDLED IDS, EMBEDDED COMMENTS, and COMMENT UI (not just one), matching the validator's `=*` grammar. | `tests/56-export-fuzz.spec.js` - `Export as Portable round-trips a batch of adversarial comment notes byte-exact (GH-REGRESS-EXPORT-HOTSPOT)`, `Export as Plain strips compact-markered comment-data regions across all of them (GH-REGRESS-EXPORT-HOTSPOT)`; source: `assets/js/` - `_buildSavedHtml()`, `_buildPlainHtml()` |
| CMH-EXP-13 | The comments sidebar exposes a single `Export` disclosure instead of separate always-visible Portable, Offline, Markdown, and Plain HTML buttons. Opening the disclosure toggles `aria-expanded` and reveals ordinary buttons (no ARIA menu/menuitem contract) with the same ids and handlers; Escape closes only that disclosure before any composer draft behind it. | `tests/06-save-export.spec.js` - `sidebar export actions live in a single disclosure and Portable still downloads (CMH-EXP-13)`, `Escape closes only the sidebar export disclosure before a composer draft (CMH-EXP-13)`; source: `assets/template.shell.html`, `assets/js/55-toolbar-menu.js`, `assets/css/20-chrome.css` |

## Export security invariants

| Feature id | Behavior | Covering tests |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CMH-EXP-14 | The whole-document `DOMParser -> doc.documentElement.outerHTML` round-trip used in the four export/apply paths (`36-checklist.js`, `37-notes.js`, `65-export-portable.js`, `68-export-offline.js`) is safe only because comment/state data never enters it as markup and host HTML is a trusted, unsanitized boundary (CMH-SEC-01). Every `parseFromString()` call site feeds only the trusted document `html` string, never a comment `note`, `quote`, `author`, or any other user-supplied field. If host-HTML sanitization is ever added upstream, this round-trip must be re-audited for serialization-differential stored XSS before landing. | `tests/test_export_domparser_guard.py` - `test_domparser_call_sites_never_take_comment_fields`, `test_domparser_call_sites_use_the_trusted_html_variable`, `test_domparser_present_only_in_expected_partials`; source: `assets/js/36-checklist.js` - `_applyChecklistStateToHtml()`, `assets/js/37-notes.js` - `_applyNoteStateToHtml()`, `assets/js/65-export-portable.js` - `_applyWidgetLayoutToHtml()`, `assets/js/68-export-offline.js` - `_offlineDocFromHtml()` |

## Export to Markdown

| Feature id | Behavior | Covering tests |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CMH-MD-01 | `Export to Markdown` deterministically converts `#commentRoot` content to GFM (headings, tables, fenced code with language, mermaid source not SVG, lists, blockquotes, image figures, KQL kusto fences with the run link), appends live comments as a `Review comments` section (thread-aware: each thread is one heading whose count is per-thread, carrying the root author, then its reply refinements labelled `_Reply k - by NAME:_` oldest-first, with Unicode line separators folded inside a note), and downloads a `.md` file (download only, never writing the clipboard), and is reachable from the sidebar and the overflow menu. | `tests/43-export-markdown.spec.js` - kitchen-sink structure, determinism, callout/chart/diff forms, mermaid source, comment appendix, `the Review comments appendix is thread-aware: root author, ordered reply refinements, and a per-thread count (CMH-MD-01)`, `Export to Markdown downloads a .md file and does NOT write the clipboard`, sidebar/overflow availability; source: `assets/js/` - `htmlToMarkdown()`, `buildMarkdownDoc()`, `exportMarkdown()` |
| CMH-MD-02 | Markdown export is injection-safe: prose, comment notes, and attribute-derived labels cannot forge headings, blockquotes, lists, thematic rules, setext underlines, GFM tables, or raw HTML; link/image labels and URLs are escaped; `javascript:`/control-char URLs are neutralized; code spans and fences stay intact against backtick and pipe tricks. | `tests/43-export-markdown.spec.js` - `plain prose cannot forge headings, blockquotes, lists, or rules`, `a comment note cannot forge a setext heading`, `a comment note cannot forge a GFM table`, `a backslash in link text cannot hijack the destination`, `a link URL with a leading control char cannot smuggle an executable scheme`, `pipes inside code spans in a table cell stay escaped, not column boundaries`, `a code language class with a backtick cannot void the fence`, `a comment note cannot forge headings/fences or inject HTML in the appendix`, `attribute-derived labels (image alt) escape HTML and markup`; source: `assets/js/` - `_mdText()`, `_mdEscapeLeading()`, `_mdEscapePipes()`, `_mdUrl()` |
| CMH-MD-03 | List items serialize inline `<strong>`/`<em>`/`<a>`/`<code>`/`<img>` that are DIRECT children of the `<li>` (not only inside a nested block), so a bullet mixing `**bold**`, an inline `<a>` link, and `code` keeps its emphasis, link, and code span in the exported Markdown. | `tests/47-hardening.spec.js` - `Markdown export serializes strong/link/code that are direct list-item children (CMH-MD-03)`; source: `assets/js/` - `_mdInlineOne()`, `_mdAppendInline()`, `_mdList()` |
| CMH-MD-04 | The Markdown URL allowlist keeps only image data URLs: a bare `data:` destination (for example `data:text/html`) is neutralized to `about:blank`, while a `data:image/...` source is preserved so inline images still export. | `tests/47-hardening.spec.js` - `Markdown export drops a bare data: URL but keeps a data:image URL (CMH-MD-04)`; source: `assets/js/` - `_mdUrl()` |
| CMH-MD-05 | Markdown export serializes `data-cm-widget` content even when the widget is `cm-skip`: slotted boards export as a `_[Widget: ...]_` note plus a GFM table whose columns are `data-cm-slot` values and whose cells are `data-cm-part-label` values; unslotted widgets export as a bullet list. | `tests/43-export-markdown.spec.js` - `expanded demo item kinds convert to Markdown`; source: `assets/js/` - `_mdWidget()` |
| CMH-MD-06 | Markdown export deliberately omits `data-cm-offline-chart` image payloads to avoid base64 bloat, emitting only the offline chart snapshot's caption or alt text as a chart-snapshot placeholder. | `tests/43-export-markdown.spec.js` - `offline chart snapshots export label text without embedding the data image (CMH-MD-06)`; source: `assets/js/` - `_mdFigure()` |
| CMH-MD-07 | The Markdown review-comments appendix carries an agent-facing untrusted-data boundary: read-first instructions frame every reviewer note as a document-scoped request rather than an agent or system instruction, and each root/reply note is individually enclosed in a dynamic tilde-sized `BEGIN/END UNTRUSTED REVIEWER NOTE` fence whose delimiter exceeds every tilde run in that note. Bidi/invisible formatting controls are stripped before note text enters a fence. | `tests/43-export-markdown.spec.js` - `the Markdown appendix marks reviewer notes as untrusted fenced data (CMH-MD-07)`, `the Markdown appendix strips bidi controls from reviewer notes (CMH-MD-07)`; source: `assets/js/60-export-markdown.js` - `_mdCommentsAppendix()` |
| CMH-MD-08 | A `<blockquote>` serializes its block children recursively (paragraphs, lists, code fences, nested blockquotes) so every output line is prefixed with `> `, preserving the multi-block structure. The old `_mdCollapse(_mdInlineText(el))` path that flattened all children to one line is not used. | `tests/43-export-markdown.spec.js` - `a blockquote with nested block children preserves each child on its own > prefixed lines (CMH-MD-08)`; source: `assets/js/` - `_mdBlock()` BLOCKQUOTE branch |

A shown/total count (for example `2 / 3`) sits beside the field, and a clear (X) button appears while the field is non-empty; clicking it empties the field, restores every card, and refocuses the field. | CMH-TOC-09 | The side menu carries a `cm-skip` filter box that searches AS a filter over the sections (not only the list): a query hides each matching entry's `<section>` (for section-wrapped content) and always its menu entry, Escape clears the filter, and a deep link OR a comment/widget jump to a filtered-out section reveals AND expands it instead of scrolling to nothing. The filter box hides when the menu is collapsed and clears when the viewport narrows below the menu breakpoint. Filtered sections use `display:none` (offset-safe), and scroll-spy never marks a filtered entry `aria-current` - including a flat heading with no `<section>` wrapper (whose menu row still filters). | `tests/65-toc-search.spec.js` - `the search box filters visible sections by heading and body text, Escape clears (CMH-TOC-09)`, `navigating to a filtered-out section reveals it (CMH-TOC-09)`, `the filter box hides when the side menu is collapsed (CMH-TOC-09)`, `jumping to a comment inside a filtered-out section reveals it (CMH-TOC-09)`, `a filtered-out flat heading entry never receives aria-current (CMH-TOC-09)`; source: `assets/js/` - `setupSideToc()` `applyTocFilter()` `onScroll()`, `50-sidebar.js` `expandCollapsedAncestors()`; `assets/css/` - `.cm-side-toc-search`, `section.cm-toc-filtered` |
