## Rich-text comment notes

Review comment notes are stored as a plain-text markdown-ish SOURCE string and rendered to safe HTML
at display time (sidebar card, inline popover, print appendix). A composer formatting toolbar and
Ctrl/Cmd shortcuts make the markers discoverable, mimicking Office-style comments. Rendering is
presentation-only: the stored note is unchanged, so old notes stay compatible and Copy all preserves
the raw source.

| Feature id | Behavior | Covering tests |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CMH-RICH-01 | `renderRichNote` renders `**bold**`, `*italic*`, `__underline__`, `~~strike~~`, and inline-code markers as `<strong>`/`<em>`/`<u>`/`<s>`/`<code>` in the reviewer's sidebar card note (`.note.cmh-rich`). | `tests/33-rich-text.spec.js` - `renders bold, italic, underline, strikethrough, and inline code from markers in the sidebar card (CMH-RICH-01)` |
| CMH-RICH-02 | Lines beginning `- ` render as a `<ul class="cmh-rich-list">` bullet list, and the stored `comment.note` keeps the raw source (rendering is presentation-only). | `tests/33-rich-text.spec.js` - `renders '- ' lines as a bullet list and stores the note as raw source (CMH-RICH-02)` |
| CMH-RICH-03 | Markdown-style links (a label in square brackets followed by a URL in parentheses, including balanced parentheses in the URL) and bare `http(s)://` URLs (trailing sentence punctuation stripped) render as `<a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow">`; clicking a rendered note link navigates without firing the card's jump/flash handler. | `tests/33-rich-text.spec.js` - `renders markdown links and bare-URL auto-links as safe new-tab anchors; a note link does not trigger the card jump (CMH-RICH-03)` |
| CMH-RICH-04 | The renderer escapes all HTML first and only emits its own fixed tags, so raw HTML in a note is inert; a link whose scheme is not `http`/`https`/`mailto` (for example `javascript:` or `data:`) is left as literal text, not an anchor; unbalanced/unclosed and whitespace-flanked markers render literally; and C0 control characters (including carriage return) are stripped so a link URL cannot be obfuscated. A null note renders as an empty card. | `tests/33-rich-text.spec.js` - `escapes HTML and rejects javascript/data link schemes; malformed markers stay literal (CMH-RICH-04)`; `tests/33-rich-text.spec.js` - `renders hostile long input fast without hanging (CMH-RICH-04-dos)`; `tests/33-rich-text.spec.js` - `strips control chars so a link URL cannot be CR-obfuscated, and a null note renders empty (CMH-RICH-04-ctl)` |
| CMH-RICH-05 | The composer has a `role="group"` formatting toolbar (`.cm-format-bar`) of labelled `type="button"` controls (bold, italic, underline, strikethrough, code, link, list) that wrap the textarea's current selection with the matching marker; the link button wraps the selection in link syntax and selects the URL placeholder to type over. | `tests/33-rich-text.spec.js` - `composer toolbar buttons wrap the textarea selection for each format (CMH-RICH-05)` |
| CMH-RICH-06 | In the composer textarea, Ctrl/Cmd+B/I/U wrap bold/italic/underline and Ctrl/Cmd+K wraps a link around the selection; the existing Ctrl/Cmd+Enter (save) and Esc (cancel) still work. | `tests/33-rich-text.spec.js` - `Ctrl/Cmd+B/I/U/K shortcuts wrap the selection and Ctrl+Enter still saves (CMH-RICH-06)` |
| CMH-RICH-07 | The inline comment popover renders the note as rich HTML (`.cm-comment-popover-note.cmh-rich`) via `renderRichNote`. | `tests/33-rich-text.spec.js` - `the inline comment popover renders the note rich (CMH-RICH-07)` |
| CMH-RICH-08 | Copy all emits the byte-exact raw note source (the agent round-trip), while the Markdown review-comments appendix preserves that source inside a non-forgeable adaptive tilde fence, making its markers literal data rather than rendered markup or document structure. | `tests/33-rich-text.spec.js` - `Copy all keeps the raw note source; Markdown export fences it as literal data (CMH-RICH-08)` |
| CMH-RICH-09 | Comment search still matches a note's markdown markers and link URLs: each comment card carries a hidden `.cmh-note-raw` element with the raw source, which the search haystack reads (the visible rich note drops markers and link URLs). | `tests/33-rich-text.spec.js` - `comment search matches note markers and link URLs via the hidden raw source (CMH-RICH-09)` |
| CMH-RICH-10 | The Help panel documents the formatting markers and the Ctrl/Cmd shortcuts in a "Formatting your comment" topic. | `tests/33-rich-text.spec.js` - `Help documents the formatting markers and shortcuts (CMH-RICH-10)` |
| CMH-RICH-11 | The add-comment selection menu renders above any open composer (its z-index is set above the current composer stack in `showMenu`), so a reviewer can always start another comment on a fresh selection even when a taller composer overlaps it. | `tests/33-rich-text.spec.js` - `the add-comment selection menu renders above an open composer (CMH-RICH-11)` |
| CMH-RICH-12 | The print/PDF comment appendix renders each note rich via `renderRichNote` inside a `<div class="cmh-print-note cmh-rich">` (so a bullet list is valid markup), not as escaped plain text. | `tests/33-rich-text.spec.js` - `the print appendix renders the note rich (CMH-RICH-12)` |
| CMH-RICH-13 | A threaded reply's note also renders rich (in the sidebar and print) and carries its own hidden raw-source element, so a reply's markers and link URLs stay searchable alongside the root. | `tests/33-rich-text.spec.js` - `a threaded reply also renders its note rich and stays searchable (CMH-RICH-13)` |
| CMH-RICH-14 | `docs/TUTORIAL.md` documents the formatting feature (the "Format your comment" section with the markers and shortcuts) and embeds both the composer-toolbar and rendered-rich-card screenshots. | `tests/test_tutorial_rich.py` - `test_tutorial_documents_formatting_and_embeds_both_shots` |
## Storage management

| Feature id | Behavior | Covering tests |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CMH-STORE-01 | The comment array is persisted in a modern slot `COMMENT_KEY + "::z"` holding either a compressed (lz-string, `\u0001z`-framed) payload or plain JSON, whichever is smaller in UTF-16 length; `COMMENT_KEY` is read only as a legacy fallback and never written by the current runtime. | `tests/72-storage.spec.js` - `codec round-trips arbitrary Unicode and stores the smaller of plain/framed (CMH-STORE-01, CMH-STORE-03)`, `large comment sets persist compressed in the ::z slot and reload intact (CMH-STORE-01, CMH-STORE-02)` |
| CMH-STORE-02 | Large comment sets are stored compressed and round-trip losslessly across a reload; a legacy plain-JSON `COMMENT_KEY` value still loads and is migrated to the `::z` slot on the next save. | `tests/72-storage.spec.js` - `large comment sets persist compressed in the ::z slot and reload intact (CMH-STORE-01, CMH-STORE-02)`, `a legacy plain value still loads and migrates to the ::z slot (CMH-STORE-02)` |
| CMH-STORE-03 | A present-but-unreadable value in the modern `::z` slot (a corrupt/newer-format frame OR a corrupt/non-array PLAIN-JSON value, since the slot stores the smaller of the two) is left untouched (not overwritten) and surfaces a recovery notice; a corrupt LEGACY base-key value still degrades silently to empty. The codec round-trips arbitrary Unicode (astral, lone surrogates, U+2028/9, control chars). | `tests/72-storage.spec.js` - `codec round-trips arbitrary Unicode and stores the smaller of plain/framed (CMH-STORE-01, CMH-STORE-03)`, `an unreadable (corrupt/newer-format) stored value is left untouched and surfaces a notice (CMH-STORE-03)`, `a startup context backfill does not overwrite an unreadable store (CMH-STORE-03)`, `a corrupt PLAIN-JSON value in the modern slot is protected, not overwritten (CMH-STORE-03)`; `tests/test_storage.py` - `test_declared_suffixes_include_the_modern_slot` |
| CMH-STORE-04 | The Manage storage dialog lists every commentable-html document's stored data across this browser origin with a human-readable size and comment count, the current document marked "This document", sorted by size. | `tests/72-storage.spec.js` - `the manager lists every document with size and count, current marked (CMH-STORE-04)` |
| CMH-STORE-05 | Deleting another document's row removes exactly that document's owned keys (base + known subkey suffixes + its dismissed-banner keys) and updates the totals; deletion authority is the owned-key shape plus ownership proof, tested with a prototype-free membership set, so a non-commentable-html key (including one literally named `constructor`/`toString`/`__proto__`) is never removed. | `tests/72-storage.spec.js` - `deleting another document's row frees its keys but never touches non-CMH data (CMH-STORE-05)`, `a registered document whose custom key ends in a reserved suffix groups and deletes whole (CMH-STORE-05)`, `foreign keys named like Object.prototype members are never listed or deleted (CMH-STORE-05)`; `tests/test_storage.py` - `test_every_comment_key_subkey_suffix_is_registered` |
| CMH-STORE-06 | The dialog is a near-full-screen overlay; clicking the backdrop or pressing Escape closes it, focus is trapped (Tab and Shift+Tab stay inside) while open and restored to the opener on close, and deleting a row keeps focus in the dialog. | `tests/72-storage.spec.js` - `the dialog is near-full-screen, closes on backdrop and Escape, and restores focus (CMH-STORE-06)`, `Tab stays within the dialog and deleting a row keeps focus inside it (CMH-STORE-06)` |
| CMH-STORE-07 | When a comment save fails because storage is full, the Manage storage dialog opens automatically; freeing space retries the exact failed write and the comment persists. Secondary writers (notes/checklist/section-review) instead surface a "Manage storage" toast action, whose button is removed once the toast is dismissed so it cannot linger clickable or focusable. Closing the dialog while any write (comment or secondary) is still pending re-offers the recovery action so nothing unsaved is lost silently. | `tests/72-storage.spec.js` - `a quota failure on a comment save opens the manager and retrying after freeing space persists (CMH-STORE-07)`, `winner-first write order: a ::z quota failure leaves the legacy value recoverable (CMH-STORE-07)`, `a secondary writer (note) surfaces a Manage storage action and freeing space retries + persists it (CMH-STORE-07)`, `a checklist quota failure surfaces a Manage storage action (CMH-STORE-07)`, `a section-review quota failure surfaces a Manage storage action (CMH-STORE-07)`, `closing the manager while a secondary write (note) is still pending re-offers recovery (no silent loss) (CMH-STORE-07)`, `closing the manager with a comment still unsaved warns (no silent loss) (CMH-STORE-07)`, `the storage toast action is removed once dismissed so it cannot linger clickable or focusable (CMH-STORE-07)` |
| CMH-STORE-08 | The current-document row disables Delete and offers "Clear all comments" (reusing the confirmed clear-all flow); the empty/quota state offers Export and Clear shortcuts inside the dialog even when only shared preferences remain (no other document to delete). | `tests/72-storage.spec.js` - `the current-document row disables Delete and offers Clear all comments (CMH-STORE-08)`, `the empty/quota state offers Export and Clear shortcuts even when shared preferences remain (CMH-STORE-08)` |
| CMH-STORE-09 | Every per-document `COMMENT_KEY + "::"` subkey suffix written by the runtime is listed in the single `CMH_SUBKEY_SUFFIXES` constant the manager uses to compute and reclaim a document's owned keys. | `tests/test_storage.py` - `test_every_comment_key_subkey_suffix_is_registered`, `test_declared_suffixes_include_the_modern_slot` |
| CMH-STORE-10 | A document that registered itself in the shared `commentable-html::index` is listed by name (and reclaimable) even when it holds only non-comment data (a custom key with cleared comments); a bare foreign array or an unrelated app's key is never listed or deleted. The registry index itself is never listed or deletable, and clearing the current document's comments keeps its index entry, so residual sidecar/banner keys stay owned and reclaimable. | `tests/72-storage.spec.js` - `foreign same-origin keys (bare arrays, non-CMH, lookalike prefix) are never listed or deletable (CMH-STORE-10)`, `a custom-key document with only non-comment data is listed and reclaimable via the registry (CMH-STORE-10)`, `the shared registry index is never listed or deletable, so custom-key docs stay reclaimable (CMH-STORE-10)`, `clearing the current document's comments keeps its index entry so residual keys stay reclaimable (CMH-STORE-10)` |
| CMH-STORE-11 | The quota auto-open re-arms after each resolved episode, so a later independent full-then-free-then-full cycle opens the manager again; when the manager cannot open (a prior episode is unresolved) the comment save surfaces a recovery toast rather than failing silently. | `tests/72-storage.spec.js` - `the quota auto-open re-arms after a successful save (repeated episodes) (CMH-STORE-11)`, `when the manager cannot open, the comment save surfaces a recovery toast instead of failing silently (CMH-STORE-11)` |
| CMH-STORE-12 | Deleting a document reclaims its dismissed asset-version banner keys, matched by exact base segment (longest owned base first) so an overlapping base cannot claim another's banner. | `tests/72-storage.spec.js` - `deleting a document reclaims its dismissed-banner keys (CMH-STORE-12)`, `dismissed-banner keys attribute to the longest owned base when two bases overlap (CMH-STORE-12)` |
| CMH-STORE-13 | The dialog shows storage consumption ONLY as a four-slice pie chart (an inline SVG) with a short bullet legend beside it - the prior prose usage summary AND the "About X used across N documents" total line are gone. The slices are This document, Other commentable-html documents (only other real documents), Other (all remaining same-origin data - non-commentable-html apps plus commentable-html's shared registry index and preferences), and Free (remaining headroom in the assumed ~5 MB budget). The four slices sum to the whole disc; when usage exceeds the assumed budget, Free is 0 and the disc equals the actual usage. Each legend bullet names its slice with a human size and percentage (the accessible text alternative; the SVG carries a brief role/aria-label and a per-slice `<title>` as a non-color cue), and each drawn slice carries a `data-slice`/`data-bytes` marker. A non-commentable-html key and the shared registry index both land in the Other slice; another commentable-html document lands in the Other commentable-html documents slice. | `tests/72-storage.spec.js` - `the storage breakdown is a four-slice pie with a bullet legend (CMH-STORE-13)`, `the storage breakdown caps Free at 0 and fills the disc when usage exceeds the budget (CMH-STORE-13)`, `the storage breakdown counts the shared registry index in the Other slice (CMH-STORE-13)` |
| CMH-STORE-14 | Documents are listed in a column-headed table (Document, Comments, Size, Share, Actions); each row's Share column shows its percentage of commentable-html storage, so a larger document reads as a larger share. | `tests/72-storage.spec.js` - `documents are shown in a table with a column-headed Share of commentable-html storage (CMH-STORE-14)` |
| CMH-STORE-15 | Each document row has a lazy "Show comments" toggle that reveals a per-comment list (quote, note, author, and an approximate per-comment size) only on click; each comment is deletable - deleting a comment in the current document routes through the live delete path (tombstoning embedded ids and re-rendering the sidebar), and deleting another document's comment rewrites that document's stored `::z` slot and tombstones the removed id in that document's `::deleted` set (so an embedded copy does not resurrect on its next open), leaving its other comments intact; deleting the last comment collapses the now-empty list; long quote/note fields render as a bounded snippet with the full text in the element title; and per-comment delete keeps keyboard focus within the same document's list. | `tests/72-storage.spec.js` - `the current document's comments can be browsed lazily and deleted per comment (CMH-STORE-15)`, `another document's comments can be browsed and deleted per comment without touching others (CMH-STORE-15)`, `a long comment note is truncated to a snippet with the full text in its title (CMH-STORE-15)`, `per-comment delete keeps focus within the same document's list (CMH-STORE-15)` |
| CMH-STORE-16 | The dialog has a Close button in a footer at the bottom (in addition to the header close) that closes it and restores focus to the opener. | `tests/72-storage.spec.js` - `the dialog has a footer Close button that closes it and restores focus (CMH-STORE-16)` |
## Section review tracking

| Feature id | Behavior | Covering tests |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CMH-REVIEW-01 | Hovering or keyboard-focusing any section heading (`h1`-`h6`) inside `#commentRoot` reveals a `cm-skip` "Mark reviewed" badge just right of the title; activating it records a marker and renders a green `Reviewed` badge, and activating it again clears the marker. The badge/button are runtime-only and add no commentable text or offsets. | `tests/90-section-review.spec.js` - `marking a heading reviewed shows the badge and toggling clears it (CMH-REVIEW-01)` |
| CMH-REVIEW-02 | Review markers persist in `localStorage` under `<comment-key>::reviews` and restore after reload; the badge and TOC filter are `cm-skip` runtime chrome, so they never enter a Plain HTML export. | `tests/90-section-review.spec.js` - `a review marker survives reload and the chrome is absent from Plain export (CMH-REVIEW-02)` |
| CMH-REVIEW-03 | The section content hash covers the non-`cm-skip` text from the heading to the next same-or-higher heading and ignores transient DOM state (a highlight wrap, collapse state); editing the section's content flips a reviewed badge to amber `Changed - re-review`, while an unchanged reviewed section stays green after a reload. | `tests/90-section-review.spec.js` - `editing a reviewed section flips it to Changed while an unchanged one stays Reviewed (CMH-REVIEW-03)` |
| CMH-REVIEW-04 | An open (un-handled) comment anchored anywhere in a section puts it in the blue `Commented` state (an overlay with precedence over Reviewed/Changed/Unreviewed); deleting the comment reverts to the recomputed underlying state, a handled comment never changes the state, and clicking a Changed/Commented badge re-stamps the current hash (one-click re-review). | `tests/90-section-review.spec.js` - `an open comment marks the section Commented and one-click re-review re-stamps it (CMH-REVIEW-04)` |
| CMH-REVIEW-05 | The side-TOC carries a `cm-skip` segmented filter (All / Reviewed / Unreviewed / Commented / Changed) that collapses every section not in the chosen state and expands the rest; `All` re-expands every section; each TOC entry shows a per-state colored dot. | `tests/90-section-review.spec.js` - `the TOC review filter collapses non-matching sections and All re-expands them (CMH-REVIEW-05)` |
| CMH-REVIEW-06 | `Export as Portable` (both the inline embed and the NonPortable->standalone rebuild) and `Export Offline` bake the current markers into a dedicated `<script id="reviewedSections">` block (inside the EMBEDDED COMMENTS region, not in the Copy-all bundle) so they travel with the file, and `Export to Plain HTML` strips that region so the block is absent from a plain copy. | `tests/90-section-review.spec.js` - `Export as Portable bakes reviewedSections and Plain strips it (CMH-REVIEW-06)`, `Export Offline also bakes the review markers (CMH-REVIEW-06)` |
| CMH-REVIEW-07 | `tools/authoring/mark_reviewed.py` writes/updates/clears the `reviewedSections` block deterministically (computing each marker's hash with the shared `section_hash.py`, refusing unsafe ids/hashes, preserving the dominant newline, and inserting the block after the real embeddedComments block - decoy-safe, case-insensitive close), and `validate.py` accepts the block only as a JSON object whose every marker carries a safe base36 hash. | `tests/test_mark_reviewed.py`; `tests/test_validate_layer.py` - `test_reviewed_sections_block_is_validated` |
| CMH-REVIEW-08 | The JS runtime (`assets/js/84-section-review.js` `cmhSectionHash`) and the Python helper (`section_hash.py` `cmh_section_hash`) produce identical section hashes for a shared committed fixture set, and their DOM/HTML extractors agree over a real document - both exclude cm-skip chrome, script/style/template/noscript, and runtime-transformed blocks (diffs, KQL, mermaid, chart canvases, editable notes) so the hash covers the section's stable prose - so the two implementations cannot drift. | `tests/test_section_hash_golden.py`; `tests/90-section-review.spec.js` - `the runtime section hash matches the JS/Python golden (CMH-REVIEW-08)`, `the runtime and Python extractors agree on a doc with script/style/void and transformed blocks (CMH-REVIEW-08)` |
| CMH-REVIEW-09 | Section review reaches ANY existing document after `tools/authoring/upgrade.py` runs: because the runtime and its CSS live in the swapped CSS/COMMENT UI/JS regions and the runtime is localStorage-first and null-safe, a document produced before the feature existed (the real `dev/upgrade-corpus/v1.117.0.html` snapshot, which has no review chrome) gains a working review badge, mark-reviewed, and (once a section is marked reviewed) the side-TOC review filter after upgrade - with no baked `reviewedSections` block - and the marker persists across reload. | `tests/91-upgrade-review.spec.js` - `a pre-feature document gains a working section-review UI after upgrade.py (CMH-REVIEW-09)` |
| CMH-REVIEW-10 | The section-review chrome stays DORMANT until the reviewer starts: on a document with no review markers and no comments, no persistent state badge is shown (only the hover-only unreviewed "Mark reviewed" affordance) and the side-TOC review filter is hidden. It activates - the filter appears and per-entry status marks render - as soon as any section is marked reviewed OR the first comment is added (`window.__cmhReview.active()` reflects this). | `tests/90-section-review.spec.js` - `the review filter and TOC status marks stay dormant until the first review or comment (CMH-REVIEW-10)`, `adding the first comment activates the dormant review UI (CMH-REVIEW-10)` |
| CMH-REVIEW-11 | While active, each side-TOC entry carries a single-character status badge reflecting its section state - `R` reviewed (green), `C` commented (blue), `!` changed (amber), and a hollow badge for unreviewed - rendered as a CSS pseudo-element (`data-cmh-mark`) so the letter never enters the TOC link text that search and deep-links read; each non-unreviewed mark carries an accessible `role="img"` label. | `tests/90-section-review.spec.js` - `each TOC entry shows a single-character status badge reflecting its state (CMH-REVIEW-11)` |
| CMH-REVIEW-12 | The reviewed state travels with the file: marking a section reviewed then Export as Portable or Export Offline bakes the marker into the `reviewedSections` block, and reopening the exported file with no network loads the marker, activates the review UI (filter + TOC mark), and shows the reviewed badge. | `tests/90-section-review.spec.js` - `the reviewed state persists through Portable/Offline export and reopening activates the review UI (CMH-REVIEW-12)` |
| CMH-REVIEW-13 | Export bakes only markers whose heading still exists in the current document: a marker orphaned because its heading was removed is pruned from the `reviewedSections` block, so a shared Portable/Offline copy never leaks stale review metadata (headingText, reviewedAt, hash) for a section that no longer exists. | `tests/90-section-review.spec.js` - `export prunes orphan markers whose heading no longer exists (CMH-REVIEW-13)` |
| CMH-REVIEW-14 | Each side-TOC review-filter button carries a live per-state count rendered as an inline `(N)` beside its label (`All` shows the total section count; the four states partition it, so reviewed+unreviewed+commented+changed equals All); the count updates on every `refreshReviewUI` as sections are marked reviewed, commented, or changed. The count span is `aria-hidden` and the number is folded into each button's `aria-label` so it is announced once. The label and count stay on one line per button (`white-space: nowrap`) and the group wraps into rows without horizontal overflow even with two-digit counts. | `tests/90-section-review.spec.js` - `each review-filter button shows a live per-state count that stays on one line (CMH-REVIEW-14)` |
| CMH-REVIEW-15 | Marking a section reviewed checks that the marker actually persisted; if the write fails (localStorage full or blocked) it warns the reader with an assertive toast ("Could not persist reviewing this section ... Use Export as Portable to keep the change") instead of silently painting `Reviewed` and letting it revert on reload - symmetric with the un-review warning in `clearSectionReviewed()`. | `tests/90-section-review.spec.js` - `marking a section reviewed warns when the mark cannot be persisted (CMH-REVIEW-15)` |
## Collaboration: author attribution and comment threads

| Feature id | Behavior | Covering tests |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CMH-AUTHOR-01 | The reviewer's display name is a per-browser value stored in `localStorage` under `cmh::author` and seedable by the author via `data-cm-author` on `#commentRoot`. A sidebar identity control (`Commenting as`) shows the current name and lets the reader set or change it at any time; changing it applies to NEW comments only and never rewrites the author stamped on past comments. A new comment/reply is stamped with the current name at creation time (unset = unattributed). | `tests/92-threads.spec.js` - `the reviewer name is set via the identity control and stamps new comments only (CMH-AUTHOR-01)` |
| CMH-AUTHOR-02 | Every attributed comment and reply shows an author pill at the start of its note in the sidebar card, colored by a stable hash of the name (same name -> same color); an unattributed comment shows no pill. Author names are sanitized (control characters/newlines stripped) and capped at 60 characters at the load/merge choke point, so a hostile name cannot break out of the pill markup. | `tests/92-threads.spec.js` - `an attributed comment shows a hashed author pill and a hostile name is sanitized and capped (CMH-AUTHOR-02)` |
| CMH-AUTHOR-03 | Copy all labels each note with its author on the `Comment (by NAME):` / `Reply k (by NAME):` line, and the whole agent-facing bundle is hardened against structural-line injection: the author name AND every one-line metadata field (`Where`/`Section`/anchor labels/`Source`/image alt/etc.) fold ASCII newlines/tabs AND the Unicode line/paragraph separators U+0085/U+2028/U+2029 to spaces (so no attacker-authored value can forge a new logical line a `m`-flag regex or `splitlines()` consumer reads), the author's backtick/tilde runs are neutralized, and values are length-capped - so a hostile author name or metadata value can neither forge the untrusted-note fence nor inject a machine-trailer / HANDLED_IDS line. The Markdown export normalizes the same separators inside each note and keeps it inside its adaptive untrusted-data fence, so a forged heading cannot escape into the document. | `tests/92-threads.spec.js` - `Copy all attributes each note and neutralizes a hostile author name (CMH-AUTHOR-03)` |
| CMH-THREAD-01 | A `Reply` action on a comment card opens an EMPTY reply editor INLINE inside that thread card (Word-style, not a floating popup) whose saved note becomes a reply in that thread; replies render stacked under the root card in chronological (oldest-first) order, each with its own edit and delete controls. The sidebar and toolbar counts show the number of THREADS (top-level comments), not the total note count, and the comment search's shown/total total counts threads. | `tests/92-threads.spec.js` - `replying adds a chronological reply under the root and the count stays per-thread (CMH-THREAD-01)` |
| CMH-THREAD-02 | Deleting a thread root removes the entire thread (the root and all its replies) and tombstones every id so the whole thread stays deleted across reload; deleting a reply removes only that reply and tombstones only its id, leaving the root and sibling replies intact. | `tests/92-threads.spec.js` - `deleting a root removes the whole thread while deleting a reply removes only it (CMH-THREAD-02)` |
| CMH-THREAD-03 | Copy all emits each thread as an initial `Comment` followed by clearly-labelled `Reply 1`, `Reply 2`, ... refinements (each note individually wrapped in the untrusted-note fence), an AGENT INSTRUCTIONS line explains threads, and the machine trailer's `HANDLED_IDS_JSON` array covers the root plus every reply id so a thread is marked handled and pruned together. | `tests/92-threads.spec.js` - `Copy all emits threads as comment-plus-refinements and handles all thread ids (CMH-THREAD-03)` |
| CMH-THREAD-04 | A reply is a single-level comment with a `parentId` (a SAFE_ID that differs from its own id) and no independent anchor; replies survive an Export as Portable / embedded-comments round-trip and reconstruct their threads on reload. A reply whose `parentId` does not resolve to a present thread root (root deleted, never embedded, or pointing at another reply) is an orphan and is pruned at load, never rendering, and stays pruned across reload; it is tombstoned only when its parent is genuinely absent from the embedded block (not merely crowded out by the merge cap), so a legitimate reply is not permanently lost. | `tests/92-threads.spec.js` - `threads survive an embedded round-trip and orphan replies are pruned (CMH-THREAD-04)`, `a live thread survives a real Export as Portable round-trip (CMH-THREAD-04)` |
| CMH-THREAD-05 | Deleting a reply while its inline editor is open drops that editor with no orphaned draft, and Clear all still closes an open ROOT edit composer (top-level comment edits use the floating composer) so an in-progress root edit is not silently lost on bulk delete. | `tests/92-threads.spec.js` - `deleting a reply drops its open inline editor, and Clear all closes an open root edit composer (CMH-THREAD-05)` |
| CMH-THREAD-06 | The `Reply` action opens an EMPTY inline editor inside the thread card (a floating `.cm-composer` is NOT used) - it is never prepopulated with the text of the comment being replied to and shows no "reply to: ..." quote header. Cancel or Escape dismisses the editor without saving anything; Ctrl/Cmd+Enter (or Save) appends the reply to the thread in the sidebar. | `tests/92-threads.spec.js` - `Reply opens an EMPTY inline editor in the sidebar, never prepopulated with the comment text (CMH-THREAD-06)` |
| CMH-THREAD-07 | Editing an existing reply edits it IN PLACE in the sidebar (an inline editor, not a floating composer), prefilled with that reply's OWN current text; saving updates the reply and marks it edited. | `tests/92-threads.spec.js` - `editing a reply edits IN the sidebar, prefilled with that reply's own text (CMH-THREAD-07)` |
| CMH-THREAD-08 | Opening the first reply when no reviewer name is set reveals the sidebar identity editor so the reply can be attributed (a non-blocking prompt tracked separately from the first-comment nudge, so a reply is prompted even if an earlier comment composer already consumed the comment nudge); the reply still saves unattributed if the reviewer declines. | `tests/92-threads.spec.js` - `the first reply without a name prompts for a username, and still saves if declined (CMH-THREAD-08)` |
| CMH-THREAD-09 | An in-progress inline reply/edit draft is preserved across a sidebar re-render (comment sort, a note-typing debounce, a checklist change, etc.) - the editor is re-opened with the same text rather than silently dropped - and still saves normally afterward. | `tests/92-threads.spec.js` - `an in-progress inline reply draft survives a re-render such as sorting (CMH-THREAD-09)` |
## Content, visual conventions, KQL, charts, and optional loaders

| Feature id | Behavior | Covering tests |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CMH-THEME-01 | The template defaults to light theme; `?clawpilotTheme=dark` forces dark; `?clawpilotTheme=auto` follows OS preference. | `tests/01-inline.spec.js` - `renders in a light theme by default`; `tests/10-more.spec.js` - `renders and works in dark theme (clawpilotTheme=dark)`; `tests/33-coverage-gaps.spec.js` - `clawpilotTheme=auto follows the OS color scheme (CMH-THEME-01)`; `tests/23-coverage.spec.js` - `CMH-THEME-01: template defaults to light, supports dark, and follows OS for auto` |
| CMH-THEME-02 | The flat-doc validator evaluates authored `--cp-*` theme overrides for WCAG readability, resolving the light (`:root`) and dark (`[data-theme="dark"]`) environments SEPARATELY (a token overridden only in dark is judged against its dark value, matching custom-property cascade). It runs only on tokens the author changed from the shipped defaults, so the accepted defaults are never flagged. Graduated severity: text/link pairs use a 4.5:1 target - a near-miss in the 3.0-4.49 band is an advisory WARNING, below 3.0:1 is an ERROR; non-text UI pairs use a 3.0:1 bar (below it is an ERROR). A chain that cannot be resolved to two concrete colors is reported as an 'unresolved / not evaluated' advisory. This is a STATIC resolution, NOT a computed-style/cascade simulation (documented non-goal). `--suggest` prints a compliant nudged foreground for a finding when one is reachable (unresolved and mid-tone-unreachable findings get a diagnostic instead). A `<style data-cmh-brand>` palette is deferred to the `--brand` tooling's own contrast advisory (CMH-TOOL-19) and not double-flagged here. The near-miss and unresolved advisories carry a stable prefix so `retrofit.py` keeps them out of its hard-fail path; a bad-contrast ERROR still blocks retrofit. | `tests/test_theme_contrast.py` - `test_cmh_theme_02_environments_resolve_dark_overrides_separately`, `test_cmh_theme_02_nudge_reaches_target`, `test_cmh_theme_02_nudge_returns_none_when_unreachable`, `test_cmh_theme_02_grouped_selector_applies_to_both_envs`, `test_cmh_theme_02_print_media_block_is_ignored`, `test_cmh_theme_02_screen_conditional_groups_are_traversed`, `test_cmh_theme_02_statement_at_rules_do_not_swallow_the_next_rule`, `test_cmh_theme_02_brace_in_declaration_value_does_not_drop_the_rule`, `test_cmh_theme_02_print_style_block_is_ignored`, `test_cmh_theme_02_brand_stamped_palette_is_deferred`, `test_cmh_theme_02_not_screen_media_is_excluded`, `test_cmh_theme_02_compact_at_rule_syntax_is_traversed`, `test_cmh_theme_02_deeply_nested_groups_do_not_crash`, `test_cmh_theme_02_compliant_palette_is_clean`, `test_cmh_theme_02_bad_text_contrast_is_an_error`, `test_cmh_theme_02_near_miss_is_a_warning_not_an_error`, `test_cmh_theme_02_dark_only_override_is_caught_in_dark_env_only`, `test_cmh_theme_02_print_block_does_not_mask_bad_screen_dark_override`, `test_cmh_theme_02_bad_override_in_screen_media_is_evaluated`, `test_cmh_theme_02_partial_override_pairs_against_default`, `test_cmh_theme_02_undefined_tokens_are_not_fabricated`, `test_cmh_theme_02_reformatted_default_is_not_an_override`, `test_cmh_theme_02_unresolved_chain_is_an_advisory`, `test_cmh_theme_02_unresolved_background_is_an_advisory`, `test_cmh_theme_02_non_text_ui_uses_three_to_one`, `test_cmh_theme_02_findings_carry_a_reachable_suggestion`, `test_cmh_theme_02_advisory_is_not_fatal_for_retrofit`, `test_cmh_theme_02_defaults_match_shipped_template`; source: `tools/validate/cmhval/contrast.py` (`theme_environments`, `nudge_to_ratio`), `tools/validate/checks/theme_contrast.py`, wired in `tools/validate/validate.py` |
| CMH-CONTENT-01 | Top-level sections inside `#commentRoot` render as cards with border, radius, padding, and shadow; nested sections are not double-carded. | `tests/16-formatting.spec.js` - `sections render as cards (border + radius + shadow)`, `a nested section does not get a second card (direct-child scoping)`, `section cards and badges are styled in dark theme too` |
| CMH-CONTENT-02 | Plain tables inside `#commentRoot` get collapsed borders, themed headers, and zebra striping. | `tests/16-formatting.spec.js` - `tables use collapsed borders and a themed header` |
| CMH-CONTENT-03 | `.badge`, `.badge.ok`, `.badge.warn`, and `.badge.danger` render as status pills with accessible themed contrast; long badge text wraps inside the pill and never overflows (no `white-space: nowrap`; `max-width: 100%`; `overflow-wrap: anywhere`). | `tests/16-formatting.spec.js` - `status badges are styled pills with a border`, `section cards and badges are styled in dark theme too`; `tests/29-overflow.spec.js` - `a long .badge wraps inside its box instead of overflowing` |
| CMH-CONTENT-04 | Author `nav.cm-toc` markup is styled and its anchor links resolve to stable section ids. | `tests/16-formatting.spec.js` - `a table of contents renders with anchor links that resolve to sections` |
| CMH-CONTENT-05 | ADO repos, files, PRs, work items, builds, and wiki references in authored content should be real links to the source resource. | manual - documented in `SKILL.md`; no automated enforcement found |
| CMH-CONTENT-06 | Cross-references between sections should use stable in-page anchor links instead of prose such as `see above`. | partial - `tests/16-formatting.spec.js` verifies TOC anchor links; manual for prose cross-reference coverage |
| CMH-CONTENT-07 | Rectangular authored tables with a `<thead>` are sortable per column with up/down chevrons; only tables without `colspan`/`rowspan` and with matching cell counts opt in, and each click cycles asc, desc, original. | `tests/24-copy-sort.spec.js` - `every table column header gets sort chevrons`, `a numeric column sorts numerically, cycling asc -> desc -> original`, `a text column sorts lexicographically`; `tests/28-duck-fixes.spec.js` - `a non-rectangular table (colspan) is not made sortable`; source: `assets/js/` - `_sortableTables()` |
| CMH-CONTENT-08 | Table sorting is numeric-aware with empty cells first and no `NaN` comparisons; sort state persists to `localStorage`, re-applies before comment restore, recomputes live text offsets after sort, drops stale offsets when a multi-row highlight becomes discontiguous, reports unavailable offsets in Copy all for that invalidated text anchor, and canonicalizes comments back to original row order for export. | `tests/24-copy-sort.spec.js` - `sorting keeps comment anchors attached, and the sort survives reload`, `a scattered multi-row table highlight does not restore onto unrelated rows after reload (CMH-CONTENT-08)`; `tests/28-duck-fixes.spec.js` - `sorting a numeric column with multiple empty cells does not scramble (NaN guard)`, `a comment on a sorted table cell exports with a correct anchor`; source: `assets/js/` - `_sortRows()`, `_canonicalCommentsForExport()` |
| CMH-CONTENT-09 | The shipped content layout boxes standalone code blocks and `figure.chart`, widens the template content max width to 1480px, and keeps code/chart figures readable without host CSS. | `tests/26-ui-batch2.spec.js` - `standalone code blocks are boxed`; `tests/27-ui-batch3.spec.js` - `chart figures are boxed`; source: `assets/template.shell.html` - `.app { max-width: 1480px; }` |
| CMH-CONTENT-10 | Author text never runs out of its box: long badges, long table cells, and long KQL cluster titles wrap within their container instead of overflowing or being clipped (`overflow-wrap: anywhere`, `max-width: 100%`, and `min-width: 0` on the flex KQL title). | `tests/29-overflow.spec.js` - `a long .badge wraps inside its box instead of overflowing`, `a long unbroken token in a table cell wraps and the table fits its box`, `a long KQL cluster title wraps and the figure is not clipped`; `tests/30-monkey.spec.js` - `monkey: random chrome + render fuzz never crashes on kitchen-sink (seed ${seed})`, `monkey: full-feature fuzz including mermaid + chart never crashes (example over http)` |
| CMH-CONTENT-11 | Boxed asides use theme-aware `cmh-callout` (plus `-info`, `-success`, `-warning`, `-danger`) utilities and a boxed `cmh-lede` lead; all keep readable text (WCAG AA contrast) on soft tinted backgrounds in both light and dark themes, so authors never hardcode colors. | `tests/36-callouts.spec.js` - `callouts and lede stay readable in ${theme} theme (CMH-CALLOUT-01)`, `every callout variant renders as a bordered box (CMH-CALLOUT-01)`, `callout prose is commentable (CMH-CALLOUT-02)`; docs: `dev/skill/references/content-conventions.md` |
| CMH-CALLOUT-03 | Callouts carry a NON-color affordance so meaning survives grayscale printing, color-blindness, and screen readers: each variant renders a distinct leading `::before` glyph (info/success/warning/danger) drawn in `--cp-text` with text (not emoji) presentation and hidden from the accessibility tree via the `content: ".." / ""` alt-text syntax, and a runtime pass stamps `role="note"` plus a variant `aria-label` (`Note`/`Success`/`Warning`/`Danger`). An explicit author `aria-label` is respected, and a leading `<strong>` label (the first meaningful node, robust to a stray empty leading element, but not mid-sentence bold) suppresses the auto aria-label so the variant is not announced twice. The glyph is a pseudo-element and the placement uses logical properties (RTL-safe), so it never enters text selection or comment offsets. | `tests/66-callout-a11y.spec.js` - `each callout variant exposes role=note and the correct variant accessible name (CMH-CALLOUT-03)`, `an explicit author aria-label is respected (CMH-CALLOUT-03)`, `each callout variant renders a distinct non-color glyph (CMH-CALLOUT-03)`, `an authored leading strong label suppresses the aria-label (CMH-CALLOUT-03)`, `mid-sentence bold does NOT suppress the aria-label (CMH-CALLOUT-03)`, `an empty leading element does not defeat strong-label suppression (CMH-CALLOUT-03)`; source: `assets/js/39-callout.js`; `assets/css/` - `.cmh-callout::before` |
| CMH-CONTENT-12 | Each commentable code block shows a small language pill (Python, C#, KQL, and so on) derived from its `language-*` class, sitting next to the Copy button in a shared tools container. | `tests/37-ui-polish.spec.js` - `code blocks show a language pill beside the Copy button (CMH-CODEPILL-01)`; source: `assets/js/` - `setupCodeCopy()`, `_codeLangLabel()` |
| CMH-CONTENT-14 | Top-level prose is not width-capped: ordinary `#commentRoot` paragraphs (and the `cmh-lede` lead) fill the content column, the same full width as tables, figures, code, and callouts (there is no readable-measure cap). | `tests/46-heading-a11y-title.spec.js` - `top-level prose paragraphs are not width-capped (lede and sections both full width)`, `tests/44-ui-parity.spec.js` - `top-level prose paragraphs fill the content width (no measure cap)`; source: `assets/css/` |
| CMH-CONTENT-15 | A document has a themed, visible top-level title: `#commentRoot` top-level `h1` (bare or inside the lede header) is styled larger than body prose, and `new_document.py` prepends a lede header carrying the label as an `<h1>` when the fragment has none (`--no-title` opts out; a fragment with its own `h1`/lede is left untouched). The "already has a title" check parses the fragment (it does not scan raw text), so an `<h1>` inside an HTML comment, `<script>`, or `<style>`, or an `h1`/`cmh-lede` nested below the top level, does not suppress the prepended title. | `tests/46-heading-a11y-title.spec.js` - `the document title h1 is themed and visibly larger than body prose`; `tests/test_new_document.py` - `DocTitleTests` incl. `test_ensure_doc_title_ignores_h1_inside_comment`, `test_ensure_doc_title_ignores_h1_inside_script`, `test_ensure_doc_title_ignores_nested_h1_and_lede`; source: `assets/css/` - `#commentRoot > h1`, `#commentRoot > header > h1`; `tools/authoring/new_document.py` - `ensure_doc_title()`, `_has_active_title()` |
| CMH-CONTENT-16 | The reviewable content region is exactly the `#commentRoot` subtree: `generate_toc.py` and `validate.py` both track the root by identity and mark it closed on the root's (or an ancestor's) end tag, so headings and prose cross-references in a later sibling container (for example a page `<footer>` after `</main id=commentRoot>`) are not collected into the TOC nor validated as content, even though they sit at the same nesting depth. | `tests/test_generate_toc.py` - `test_headings_after_root_close_are_ignored`; `tests/test_validate_references.py` - `test_reference_after_root_close_ignored`; source: `tools/authoring/generate_toc.py` - `_inside_root()`, `root_closed`; `tools/validate/checks/parsing.py` - `_cr_closed` |
| CMH-CONTENT-18 | On wide screens (`min-width: 1700px`) with the comments sidebar open, the content column uses a widened target (1600px, up from 1300px) so the document body fills more of the space between the navigation pane and the sidebar instead of leaving a large centered gutter. When the side table-of-contents navigation pane is present (`body.cm-side-toc-on`, not collapsed), the recipe subtracts that pane's width (`--cm-toc-inset`) so the content still reaches its full target between the nav pane and the sidebar rather than being squeezed by the pane's left inset. The runtime footer tracks the same widened target (including the TOC inset) so it stays flush with the content column (CMH-FOOT-03). | `tests/58-board-ux.spec.js` - `wide screens give the sidebar-open content column extra width (CMH-CONTENT-18)`, `the widened content column reaches its full target with the side TOC pane present (CMH-CONTENT-18)`; source: `assets/css/10-layout.css` - `body.sidebar-open .app`, `--cm-toc-inset`, `assets/css/20-chrome.css` - `body.sidebar-open .cm-footer` |
| CMH-CONTENT-19 | A shipped `.cmh-diagram-gallery` layout helper lays out MULTIPLE diagrams of very different aspect ratios robustly, so authors do not hand-roll a fragile per-document grid. Above the mobile breakpoint it is a centred FLEX-WRAP of UNIFORM DIAGRAM-HEIGHT, content-HUGGING framed cards: every diagram is rendered at one fixed HEIGHT (`svg{height:15rem}`) with its WIDTH derived from the mermaid viewBox aspect ratio (`width:auto`, `!important` overriding mermaid's inline max-width, the `70-kql.css` `max-width:none` reset, and the layer's narrow cap; NO max-width clamp, so the diagram renders at its natural readable width), and each card SHRINKS to hug that width up to a generous `max-width:min(100%,48rem)`. So every diagram fills its card in BOTH dimensions: a tall-narrow diagram gets a narrow full-height card, a wide diagram gets a wide card, and a small diagram is scaled UP to the fixed height (never left tiny, marooned, slivered, or letterboxed). Sizing is pure deterministic CSS anchored on a definite height, so it is pixel-identical across Chromium/Firefox/WebKit and has no JS-measurement race; the layer's own narrow/wide scale-up (CMH-MMD-10, `updateMermaidWidthClass`) is additionally DISABLED inside the gallery - it is measurement-timing dependent and rendered diagrams tiny in a real browser (passing headless). Only diagram CONTAINERS are framed (`> pre.mermaid, > div.mermaid, > figure`), not `> *`, so a stray table/list is left alone; a `<figure>` card is `width:fit-content` so it hugs its diagram for a short caption and grows only to a bounded readable width (`figcaption{max-width:22rem}`) for a longer one, with `margin-inline:auto` centring both the diagram and the caption - so a long caption neither stretches the card to its full length (marooning the diagram) nor collapses to the tiny diagram width (towering into a narrow vertical strip); the `<figcaption>` is centred below, and when a wide diagram makes the figure its own horizontal scroll container the caption stays pinned in view via the RTL-safe logical `inset-inline-start`. Because a captioned figure's TOTAL card height includes its variable-height caption, the uniformity guarantee is on the DIAGRAM height (every card's diagram is the same 15rem), not on a captioned figure's whole card height. A diagram WIDER than the card cap keeps its full readable height and SCROLLS horizontally (`overflow-x:auto`, and `align-items:flex-start` so an over-wide diagram's START edge stays reachable by scrolling rather than being centred to a negative offset and clipped off-screen; start-alignment is visually identical for a diagram that fits, and needs no `safe` keyword so it works in every browser); an overflowing card is made keyboard-focusable (WCAG 2.1.1, `markGalleryCardScrollable` adds `tabindex` plus a scroll hint - a `pre`/`div` card gets an explicit `role=group` and `aria-label`, while a captioned `<figure>` keeps its implicit figure role and gets the hint via `aria-description` (an uncaptioned figure via `aria-label`) - and clears them when it no longer overflows). At `<=480px` the helper is a single-column flow so the layer's natural diagram flow / wide scroll (CMH-RESP-01) is intact; print stacks it single-column and unframed (`92-print.css`). | `tests/53-more-examples.spec.js` - `gallery renders every diagram whole and well-filled in hugging uniform-height cards - no tiny, no sliver, no clip, no marooning` (describe `commentable visuals matrix: diagram gallery layout (CMH-DEMO-06 / CMH-CONTENT-19)`); `tests/76-diagram-gallery.spec.js` - `frames only diagram containers and leaves a stray table alone (CMH-CONTENT-19)`, `every diagram fills its hugging, uniform-height card - no tiny, no sliver, no clip, no marooning (CMH-CONTENT-19)`, `an extreme-wide diagram keeps full height and scrolls horizontally instead of being crushed to a strip (CMH-CONTENT-19)`, `a div.mermaid card is sized and hugs its diagram just like a pre card (CMH-CONTENT-19)`, `a figure wrapping a div.mermaid is sized and hugs like a figure wrapping a pre (CMH-CONTENT-19)`, `just above the mobile breakpoint the gallery is framed and the layer gate agrees (CMH-CONTENT-19)`, `diverse mermaid diagram types each emit a positive viewBox and fill their hugging card (CMH-CONTENT-19)`, `an overflowing (extreme-wide) gallery card is keyboard-focusable but a fitting one is not (CMH-CONTENT-19)`, `a figure card is framed and its diagram is sized whole (CMH-CONTENT-19)`, `an extreme-wide diagram in a FIGURE card scrolls at full width with the caption preserved (CMH-CONTENT-19)`, `a wide figure's caption stays pinned in view while the diagram scrolls horizontally (CMH-CONTENT-19)`, `a short caption under a wide figure is centred, not pinned to the card's start edge (CMH-CONTENT-19)`, `the figure caption pins with a LOGICAL inline-start edge (RTL-safe), not physical left (CMH-CONTENT-19)`, `a long caption on a narrow diagram wraps to a bounded readable width - no tower, no marooning, diagram centred (CMH-CONTENT-19)`, `a nested caption icon in a figure card is not distorted by the diagram-sizing rule (CMH-CONTENT-19)`, `the desktop gallery disables the layer scale-up but mobile keeps it (CMH-CONTENT-19)`, `on a phone the gallery is a frameless single-column flow (CMH-CONTENT-19)`, `print stacks the gallery single-column and unframes each card (CMH-CONTENT-19)`, `a gallery diagram is commentable and the whole-diagram button stays inside its card (CMH-CONTENT-19 / CMH-MMD-06)`; source: `assets/css/50-content.css` - `.cmh-diagram-gallery`, `assets/css/92-print.css`, `assets/js/20-mermaid.js` - `updateMermaidWidthClass()` gallery early-return, `markGalleryCardScrollable()`, `positionMermaidWhole()`; docs: `skill/references/mermaid-diagrams.md` |
| CMH-STATS-01 | A report/plan opens with a `cm-skip` document-overview strip (`div.cmh-doc-stats[data-cmh-doc-stats]`) placed directly under the `<h1>` title, reporting the section count (`<h2>` count), word count, and approximate reading time (words / 200 wpm, rounded up, floor of one minute). `tools/authoring/doc_stats.py` computes and injects it: idempotent (refreshes stale counts in place), excluded from its own word count (it is `cm-skip` and the counter also skips `nav.cm-toc`/`script`/`style`/`template`), and baked into the content so it survives Plain/Standalone export. `new_document.py`, `finalize.py`, and `retrofit.py` bake it by DEFAULT for report/plan; opt out with `--no-stats`. | `tests/test_doc_stats.py` - `test_count_sections_counts_h2_inside_root`, `test_count_sections_ignores_cm_skip_and_chrome_h2`, `test_reading_minutes_rounds_up_and_has_floor_of_one`, `test_block_is_inserted_once_under_the_title`, `test_rewrite_is_idempotent`, `test_rewrite_refreshes_stale_counts`, `test_block_is_excluded_from_its_own_word_count`, `test_in_place_rewrites_file`, `test_main_missing_file_reports_error`; `tests/test_finalize.py` - `test_bakes_doc_stats_for_report_by_default`, `test_no_stats_flag_skips_the_overview_strip`, `test_stats_skipped_for_non_card_kind`; `tests/test_new_document.py` - `test_report_gets_doc_stats_by_default`, `test_no_stats_flag_skips_the_overview_strip`; `tests/test_retrofit.py` - `test_report_gets_doc_stats_by_default`, `test_no_stats_flag_skips_the_overview_strip`; source: `tools/authoring/doc_stats.py`, `assets/css/50-content.css` |
| CMH-CONTENT-13 | Content `<pre>` and `<pre><code>` blocks use `white-space: pre-wrap` so long lines wrap within their container rather than overflowing horizontally; diff (`pre.cmh-diff`) and mermaid (`pre.mermaid`) blocks use their own whitespace handling and are unaffected. | `tests/38-spec-gaps.spec.js` - `content pre blocks wrap while diff and mermaid pre blocks keep their own whitespace (CMH-CONTENT-13)`; source: `assets/css/` |
| CMH-HL-01 | The runtime highlights a commentable `<pre><code class="language-XXX">` block that shipped without highlight spans, on load, using the in-page tokenizer (the same `cmh-code-*` classes as the author-time highlighter), so a labelled block never renders as plain monochrome text even when the author never baked highlighting. It runs before the line-number gutter and before comment restoration, so line numbers and text-offset anchoring stay consistent (a comment on a now-highlighted block round-trips through reload). It is idempotent (an already-highlighted block is left alone) and only fires for a language the tokenizer knows (`diffLangKnown`); the known set includes the markup family, so a raw `language-html`/`language-xml` block also self-heals (tag names, attribute-value strings, and `<!-- -->` comments), while an unknown/non-tokenizable label (`language-text`, `language-kusto`) stays plain. | `tests/61-code-highlight-fallback.spec.js` - `a language-labelled block with no spans is highlighted on load; unknown labels stay plain`, `an unbaked language-html and language-xml block is highlighted on load (markup family) (CMH-HL-01)`, `an already-highlighted (baked) block is not re-highlighted`, `a comment on a runtime-highlighted code block survives reload`; source: `assets/js/` - `highlightCodeBlocks()` |
| CMH-HL-03 | The runtime tokenizer (`diffLangKnown` / `_HL_FAMILY` in `assets/js/26-highlight.js`) knows every language the author-time highlighter supports (`highlight_code.LANGUAGE_CONFIGS`), so a supported language authored raw is always highlightable at runtime (via CMH-HL-01 and the diff highlighter) and can never silently render monochrome. A drift guard fails the moment an author-time language is added without runtime coverage. | `tests/test_highlight_parity.py` - `test_runtime_knows_every_author_time_language`; source: `assets/js/26-highlight.js` - `_HL_FAMILY` |

| CMH-KQL-07 | A framed KQL figure (`figure.cmh-kql`) MUST carry a real `cmh-kql-run` "Run in Azure Data Explorer" `<a>` link built with `tools/kusto/kusto_link.py`. The validator errors (non-zero exit in the default, non-strict mode) when a framed figure has no such link - detected by an actual `<a>` element carrying the `cmh-kql-run` class token, so query text that merely mentions `cmh-kql-run` does not satisfy it - or when a present run link's href, after HTML-entity decoding and URL parsing, is not `https` with host exactly `dataexplorer.azure.com` (a `javascript:`/`data:`/non-ADX or look-alike-host href is a hard error, not a warning). Bare (unframed) KQL blocks are governed separately by CMH-KQL-08. | `tests/test_validate_kql.py` - `test_kql_figure_without_run_link_errors`, `test_kql_figure_run_link_only_in_query_text_errors_missing`, `test_kql_figure_javascript_run_link_errors`, `test_kql_figure_data_run_link_errors`, `test_kql_figure_http_non_adx_run_link_errors`, `test_kql_figure_lookalike_host_run_link_errors`, `test_kql_figure_entity_encoded_javascript_run_link_errors`, `test_kql_figure_with_run_link_is_clean`; `tests/test_validate_cli.py` - `test_kql_figure_missing_run_link_exit_1` (CLI exit 1 in default mode) |
| CMH-KQL-08 | Every KQL code block must be runnable OR explicitly marked clusterless. A bare `<pre><code class="language-kusto">` (or `language-kql`) that is NOT inside a `figure.cmh-kql` carrying a Run in Azure Data Explorer link (CMH-KQL-07) is a hard validator error UNLESS the `<pre>` carries the explicit `data-cmh-kql-no-cluster` metadata marker, declaring there is genuinely no cluster to run it on. Authors should PREFER providing a cluster (`tools/kusto/kql_highlight.py <cluster> <database> <title>` builds the runnable figure); the marker is the rare escape hatch (`tools/kusto/kql_highlight.py --code-only` stamps it). The `<pre>` inside a valid figure is not double-flagged. | `tests/test_validate_kql.py` - `test_bare_kusto_without_no_cluster_marker_errors`, `test_bare_kusto_with_no_cluster_marker_is_clean`, `test_bare_kusto_inside_kql_figure_is_not_double_flagged`; `tests/test_kql_highlight.py` - `test_render_code_no_cluster_marks_pre`, `test_cli_code_only`, `test_main_code_only_arg`; source: `tools/validate/checks/layer.py` - `_check_kql_blocks`, `tools/kusto/kql_highlight.py` - `render_code` |

| CMH-CHART-09 | A built-in canvas chart (`canvas.cmh-chart` / `figure.chart canvas` with inline `data-cmh-chart-points`/`-source`) authored inside a collapsible `<section>` that is collapsed (`display:none`) at load reads `clientWidth` 0 and draws its bitmap to the width fallback (the `width` attribute, else 760), so it is sized wrong for the real column width and looks blurry once revealed - and previously only a window `resize` re-drew it. A reveal `ResizeObserver` on each chart canvas re-renders it once at its real width the moment its box goes from zero-size to a real size on reveal (mirroring the Mermaid width-class observer for CMH-MMD-07). It is a one-shot per reveal (armed while the box is zero, fires on the transition to visible), NOT a perpetual size mirror: re-rendering on every size change would let a standalone `canvas.cmh-chart` in a shrink-to-fit container on a HiDPI screen keep enlarging its own bitmap and never settle (the bitmap feeds the intrinsic width, which feeds `clientWidth`). Genuine window resizes of an already-visible chart stay handled by the existing resize listener. (Unlike the canvas chart, code/KQL line gutters do NOT need a reveal hook: `getComputedStyle(target).lineHeight` resolves to a px value even while the block is `display:none`, so the gutter is already aligned when a collapsed section is revealed - see CMH-CODE-06.) | `tests/76-render-while-hidden.spec.js` - `CMH-CHART-09: a canvas chart in a collapsed section re-renders at its real width on reveal`; source: `assets/js/` - `setupInteractiveCharts()` reveal `ResizeObserver` |
| CMH-CHART-10 | A standalone `canvas.cmh-chart` (with inline `data-cmh-chart-points`/`-source`) placed directly in a shrink-to-fit container (`width: max-content`, an `inline-block`, a float, or an auto-sized `inline-flex`/grid item), rather than inside the shipped definite-width `figure.chart > .chart-wrap`, renders at its intended size on a HiDPI screen (`devicePixelRatio > 1`) instead of stretched `dpr x`. The renderer measures the logical CSS size against a bitmap reset to the AUTHORED size (which is devicePixelRatio-independent, so the canvas's own dpr-scaled bitmap cannot drive a shrink-to-fit ancestor's width - the feedback loop - and which preserves the intrinsic aspect ratio so a definite-width auto-height canvas is not squared), falling back to the authored `width`/`height` attributes when the container collapses without the canvas; the authored attributes are captured once before any bitmap write (canvas `width`/`height` reflect onto the content attributes). If a shrink-to-fit container still stretches the box past that logical size, the canvas box is pinned (`width`/`height` in px, `!important`) so it displays at its intended size; a definite-width ancestor (the shipped `.chart-wrap`) is unaffected and is never pinned, so the backing bitmap stays exactly `dpr x` the displayed CSS box and stable across re-renders. Only a pin the runtime itself set is cleared before re-measuring (author inline sizing is preserved), and a hover redraw reuses the cached logical size rather than re-measuring. | `tests/77-chart-hidpi.spec.js` - `CMH-CHART-10: a standalone chart in a ${label} container renders at its authored size on HiDPI (dpr 2)`, `CMH-CHART-10: a definite-width auto-height standalone chart keeps its aspect ratio on HiDPI (dpr 2)`, `CMH-CHART-10: pinning one axis preserves the author's inline sizing on the other axis`, `CMH-CHART-10: a standalone shrink-to-fit chart revealed from a collapsed section pins to its authored size (dpr 2)`, `CMH-CHART-10: hovering a chart updates the active point without resizing the bitmap`; source: `assets/js/` - `_sizeChartCanvas()` |
| CMH-CHART-11 | The interactive chart's y-axis tick list is derived by a BOUNDED integer index (capped at `MAX_CHART_TICKS`), not by repeatedly adding the attacker-controllable `data-cmh-chart-step`, so a tiny or zero step against a large `data-cmh-chart-max` can no longer drive an effectively unbounded synchronous loop that freezes the tab on startup; a normal step still renders the expected gridlines. The rendered tick count is exposed on `canvas._cmhChart.tickCount`. | `tests/78-chart-tick-cap.spec.js` - `CMH-CHART-11: a tiny step against a large max cannot freeze the tab`, `CMH-CHART-11: a normal step renders the expected gridlines`; source: `assets/js/30-images.js` - `renderInteractiveChart()` |
| CMH-RESP-01 | On narrow screens, mermaid blocks and chart figures stay inside the content column and expose horizontal scrolling inside their own boxes, so wide diagrams and chart labels remain readable without overflowing the viewport; the test fixture must include at least one block whose `scrollWidth` strictly exceeds its `clientWidth` and can scroll horizontally. | `tests/51-charts-mobile.spec.js` - `charts and mermaid blocks are contained in the mobile content column (CMH-RESP-01)` |
| CMH-RESP-02 | Floating Add Comment and highlight bubbles respect the visible bounds of clipped rich-content containers (`pre.mermaid`, `figure.chart`, tables, and raw diffs), horizontally AND vertically: a fully clipped target hides the bubble, and a partly visible target clamps the bubble inside the container instead of floating over unrelated content. This includes the whole-diagram `Comment on diagram` button, so inside a bounded `.cmh-diagram-gallery` card (`overflow:hidden`) the button stays clamped to the card, not the viewport. | `tests/51-charts-mobile.spec.js` - `chart add buttons stay inside the chart scroll container on mobile (CMH-RESP-02)`, `the highlight bubble hides and clamps to a horizontal-overflow clip container across container types (CMH-RESP-02)`, `the chart Add button clamps inside a narrow chart container on horizontal overflow (CMH-RESP-02)`; `tests/76-diagram-gallery.spec.js` - `a gallery diagram is commentable and the whole-diagram button stays inside its card (CMH-CONTENT-19 / CMH-MMD-06)` |
| CMH-MMDLOAD-01 | The skill does not load mermaid itself; host pages should vendor/self-host mermaid for self-contained reports or consciously opt into a CDN that renders by default. The comment layer no-ops if mermaid never renders. | `tests/23-coverage.spec.js` - `CMH-MMDLOAD-01: mermaid renders by default when served over http` (CDN path now has E2E coverage); `tests/12-features.spec.js` verifies comment flows stay usable under denied external requests; `tests/04-comments-code-mermaid.spec.js` exercises locally vendored mermaid; manual for CDN opt-in guidance |
| CMH-MMDLOAD-02 | The mermaid CDN import is gated on the presence of a `pre.mermaid` / `div.mermaid` element, so a diagram-free document (including a deck) makes no external network request at all; a document that contains a diagram still loads mermaid and renders it. | `tests/40-widget-comments.spec.js` - `CMH-MMDLOAD-02: a diagram-free document makes no mermaid CDN request; a diagram loads it` |
| CMH-NET-01 | The commentable review layer is bundled inside the document, while optional rich content is an explicit CDN choice: mermaid, Chart.js, and any tooltip library may fetch from a CDN by default and should be self-hosted or inlined for fully self-contained sharing. When CDN assets are left remote, that content depends on the network and may not render identically when the file is shared. | source/docs: `assets/js/` core runtime; `dev/skill/references/mermaid-diagrams.md`; `dev/skill/references/charts-embedding.md` |
| CMH-OFFLINE-01 | `Export Offline` is available in both the overflow menu and sidebar. It builds the Portable export with current unhandled embedded comments, snapshots rendered mermaid blocks as inline SVG, snapshots chart canvases as PNG data images, removes automatic remote loaders and remote media beacons, declares the real `commentableHtmlLayer.mode` as `offline`, preserves the CMH-FWDCOMPAT-01 region markers and `data-cmh-content-root`, keeps chart/mermaid anchors commentable, and produces a strict-valid file that renders with external network blocked. | `tests/49-offline-export.spec.js` - `Export Offline embeds vendored mermaid and Chart.js for zero-network reopen (CMH-OFFLINE-01, CMH-OFFLINE-02)`; `tests/06-save-export.spec.js` - `Save, Portable, and Offline exports exclude comments already listed as handled` |
| CMH-OFFLINE-02 | `currentDocState()` treats CMH-FWDCOMPAT-01 descriptor mode `offline`, or legacy offline chart snapshots without that descriptor, as the first-class Offline state when no unsaved comments or widget moves are present; the sidebar and toolbar badges show Offline after a zero-network reopen. | `tests/49-offline-export.spec.js` - reloaded offline export badge assertion in `Export Offline embeds vendored mermaid and Chart.js for zero-network reopen (CMH-OFFLINE-01, CMH-OFFLINE-02)` |
| CMH-OFFLINE-03 | Editing and re-saving an already-offline document preserves CMH-FWDCOMPAT-01 descriptor mode `offline`, does not introduce remote refs, and re-running Export Offline keeps the offline chart snapshots stable. | `tests/49-offline-export.spec.js` - `editing an already-offline document preserves offline mode and offline export is idempotent (CMH-OFFLINE-03)` |
| CMH-OFFLINE-04 | Offline export stripping is data-safe and egress-safe: it skips comment-data and layer-descriptor JSON scripts even when comment text mentions `import('https://...')`, strips runnable inline scripts with remote dynamic imports, bare remote imports, remote `from` imports, or network dynamic-import targets, ignores `data-id="commentableHtmlLayer"` descriptor decoys when retargeting mode, neutralizes every network-loading media path (`img`, `iframe`, `video`, `audio`, `source`, `track`, SVG `image`/`use`, `input[type=image]`, `object`, `embed`, and legacy `background`), removes remote form `action` and `formaction` targets, removes refresh meta tags and remote preload/prefetch/prerender links, strips network CSS `@import` and `url(...)`, preserves benign inline scripts whose dynamic import comments contain `//` but no network literal, preserves custom canvas scripts that are not chart snapshots, and preserves only data and relative media references. Same-origin absolute URLs are stripped because an offline file must not beacon to its original host. | `tests/49-offline-export.spec.js` - `Export Offline embeds vendored mermaid and Chart.js for zero-network reopen (CMH-OFFLINE-01, CMH-OFFLINE-02)`, `Export Offline adds a zero-network CSP and strips loader, media, CSS, and event-handler egress (CMH-OFFLINE-04, CMH-OFFLINE-05)`, `Export Offline neutralizes form posts and preserves safe canvas scripts (CMH-OFFLINE-04, CMH-OFFLINE-05)` |
| CMH-OFFLINE-05 | Offline exports add a restrictive Content Security Policy meta tag as defense in depth: inline runtime/styles and data images/fonts are allowed, all network egress, form posts, and embedding are blocked through `connect-src`, `form-action`, `frame-src`, `frame-ancestors`, and related directives, event-handler attributes are removed, and a reopened offline file with injected dynamic imports makes zero HTTP(S) requests. | `tests/49-offline-export.spec.js` - `Export Offline adds a zero-network CSP and strips loader, media, CSS, and event-handler egress (CMH-OFFLINE-04, CMH-OFFLINE-05)`, `Export Offline neutralizes form posts and preserves safe canvas scripts (CMH-OFFLINE-04, CMH-OFFLINE-05)`; source: `assets/js/` - `_ensureOfflineCsp()`, `_stripOfflineEventHandlers()` |
