## Robustness

| Feature id | Behavior | Covering tests |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CMH-ROBUST-01 | Random, seeded interaction (add, edit, delete, and clear comments in any order; sort, collapse, expand, copy, scroll, toggle syntax, hover mermaid and chart) never throws an uncaught error, never loses runtime-ready, and holds every invariant: no duplicate ids, count equals stored, DOM marks equal stored comments, and byte-for-byte anchor restore across reload. | `tests/08-noise.spec.js` - seeded add/edit/delete/clear invariants and byte-for-byte reload stability (`noise: random comments anchor to the right text, persist, and prune (seed ${seed})`, `noise: a random sequence of add/edit/delete/clear keeps every invariant`); `tests/30-monkey.spec.js` - full chrome plus render fuzz on kitchen-sink and on the example (mermaid plus chart) asserts no pageerror or console error and comment persistence (`monkey: random chrome + render fuzz never crashes on kitchen-sink (seed ${seed})`, `monkey: full-feature fuzz including mermaid + chart never crashes (example over http)`) |

## Persistence, security, and accessibility

| Feature id | Behavior | Covering tests |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CMH-PERSIST-01 | The document's `localStorage` namespace is `data-comment-key` for the JSON comment array, plus the sidecar keys `<key>::deleted` (embedded-delete tombstones), `<key>::commentSort`, `<key>::diffLayout`, `<key>::diffSyntax`, `<key>::tableSort`, `<key>::cl` (checklist state), and `<key>::note` (editable-notes state). Invalid JSON or storage failures degrade to empty/default state; only a primary comment-save failure surfaces to the reader. | `tests/38-spec-gaps.spec.js` - `persistence sidecar keys are written under the document key (CMH-PERSIST-01)`, `invalid localStorage JSON degrades to defaults without breaking startup (CMH-PERSIST-01)`, `primary comment save failure stays usable but warns that persistence failed (CMH-PERSIST-01)`; source: `assets/js/` - `saveComments()`, per-feature `localStorage` helpers |
| CMH-PERSIST-02 | `data-comment-key` is origin-scoped and must be unique per document; reusing it intentionally shares comments, tombstones, and UI state, and changing it starts a new namespace with no automatic migration. `new_document.py --key auto` derives a stable key from the document IDENTITY (`--key-from-source`, then `--source`, then the `--out` path), never from `--label`, so two documents that merely share a label do not collide; with no identity source it requires an explicit `--key`. | source: `assets/js/`; `tools/authoring/new_document.py` - `resolve_key()`; `tests/test_new_document.py` - `test_resolve_key_auto_same_label_distinct_identity_does_not_collide`, `test_resolve_key_auto_without_identity_requires_explicit_key`, `test_key_auto_derives_stable_key_from_out_path`, `test_key_auto_without_identity_exits_2` |
| CMH-PERSIST-03 | `mergeCommentSets()` de-dupes ids WITHIN the persisted array as well as across the embedded set, keeping the latest by timestamp (`updatedAt` then `createdAt`) regardless of array order, so a corrupted `localStorage` array that repeats an id renders and re-persists the newest version of that comment once, never twice. | `tests/47-hardening.spec.js` - `duplicate persisted comment ids dedupe and the newest-timestamp entry wins (CMH-PERSIST-03)`, `the newest duplicate wins even when it appears first (CMH-PERSIST-03)`; source: `assets/js/` - `mergeCommentSets()` |
| CMH-PERSIST-04 | `mergeCommentSets()` bounds startup work against an untrusted `embeddedComments` array or a poisoned `localStorage` array under a matching `data-comment-key`: the merged result is capped at `CMH_MAX_COMMENTS` (new ids stop being admitted once reached; an id already present may still be replaced by a newer duplicate), and any comment whose `start`/`end` is present but not a finite, non-negative, ordered, in-range number (per `CMH_MAX_OFFSET`) is dropped before it ever reaches `backfillContext()`'s or `restoreHighlights()`'s per-comment document walk. Comments with no offset anchor (document/image/widget/mermaid/diff) are unaffected. `restoreHighlights()` additionally requires a finite `start`/`end` before treating any comment as a text anchor, so a malformed entry with neither a recognized `anchorType` nor offsets (one that `mergeCommentSets()` lets through as trivially sane) cannot still drive a per-comment `rangeFromOffsets()` document walk. Both bounds are far beyond any real document, so normal documents render identically. | `tests/73-startup-bounds.spec.js` - `an oversized embeddedComments array is capped at merge time and startup stays fast (CMH-PERSIST-04)`, `comments with non-finite or absurd start/end offsets are dropped without breaking other comments (CMH-PERSIST-04)`, `a flood of offsetless, non-anchor-typed comments does not drive per-comment full-document work (CMH-PERSIST-04)`; source: `assets/js/` - `mergeCommentSets()`, `_offsetAnchorIsSane()`, `restoreHighlights()` |
| CMH-PERSIST-05 | Deleting an embedded-in-file comment records its `<key>::deleted` tombstone durably even when the first tombstone write hits quota: if the initial write fails, deletion saves the smaller comment array, retries the tombstone after that save can free space, and warns with an assertive toast if the retry still cannot persist. Single-comment delete and Clear both follow this durable-or-warns path, so a deleted embedded comment either stays deleted across reload or the reviewer is told it may reappear. | `tests/28-duck-fixes.spec.js` - `deleting an embedded comment retries a quota-failed tombstone after the smaller save (CMH-PERSIST-05)`, `Clear retries embedded-comment tombstones after saving the empty comments array (CMH-PERSIST-05)`, `deleting an embedded comment warns if the tombstone cannot be persisted (CMH-PERSIST-05)`, `deleting an embedded comment warns if the smaller comments save fails after its tombstone persists (CMH-PERSIST-05)`; source: `assets/js/05-persistence.js`, `assets/js/50-sidebar.js`, `assets/js/62-sortable-tables.js` |
| CMH-PERSIST-06 | Beyond the CMH-PERSIST-04 count/offset caps, startup does not do O(comment_count x document_size) work for a flood of text comments whose finite offsets are sane but unresolvable (past the document): `getTextNodes()` is walked ONCE per restore pass and the text-node index is reused across `rangeFromOffsets()` lookups (via its optional precomputed-nodes argument) rather than re-walked per comment. `restoreHighlights()` rebuilds the index only after a wrap actually mutates the DOM, so a flood of unresolvable comments costs a single scan; `backfillContext()` reuses one index for its whole pass (it never mutates the DOM) and additionally caps context capture at `CMH_MAX_BACKFILL` (400) per load - `captureContext()` runs its own full-document walk each call, so an uncontexted-comment flood is bounded to at most `CMH_MAX_BACKFILL` such walks per load and the rest stay uncontexted for a later load. `getTextNodes()` bumps `window.__cmhPerf.textScans` and `captureContext()` bumps `window.__cmhPerf.ctxCaptures` when those counters are seeded, so both the shared-scan and the backfill-cap behaviors are testable. | `tests/73-startup-bounds.spec.js` - `a flood of finite-but-unresolvable text comments shares ONE text-node scan at restore (CMH-PERSIST-06)`, `a flood of uncontexted comments caps backfill context capture at CMH_MAX_BACKFILL and shares one scan (CMH-PERSIST-06)`; source: `assets/js/10-offsets.js` - `getTextNodes()`, `rangeFromOffsets()`, `assets/js/95-startup.js` - `restoreHighlights()`, `assets/js/15-context.js` - `backfillContext()`, `captureContext()` |
| CMH-SEC-01 | Trust boundary: authored content fragments are trusted HTML and are NOT sanitized by `new_document.py` or the runtime; callers must sanitize untrusted host HTML before wrapping it. The runtime escapes or `textContent`s all user comment text and metadata, validates comment ids against `SAFE_ID_RE`, and escapes `<` as `\u003c` in the `embeddedComments` JSON. Validator coverage is targeted and does not make arbitrary host HTML safe. The rule is documented in `SKILL.md` (the "Trust boundary (MUST)" note) and in `new_document.py --help` (the epilog). **[partial - the "callers must sanitize untrusted host HTML" rule is documented and enforced only by convention; there is no automated sanitization in the validator or tooling; only the user-data escaping and id sanitization are tested]** | source: `assets/js/`; `tools/authoring/new_document.py`; `tests/test_validate_self_contained.py` - `NewCheckTests.test_embedded_comment_item_bad_id_errors`, `NewCheckTests.test_embedded_comment_item_valid_id_is_clean`; `tests/test_validate_kql.py` - `ValidateDiffAndKqlTests.test_diff_block_raw_html_errors_double_quoted_class`, `ValidateDiffAndKqlTests.test_diff_block_raw_html_errors_single_quoted_class`, `ValidateDiffAndKqlTests.test_diff_block_raw_html_errors_unquoted_class`; `tests/test_new_document.py` - `test_help_documents_the_trust_boundary` |
| CMH-SEC-02 | Defense-in-depth against document-reachable prototype pollution: the checklist and notes state maps (`_clOverrides`, the `::cl` save/load maps in `_clSave`/`_clLoad`, and `_noteOverrides`) are built with `Object.create(null)` at every assignment/reset site; `_clLoad` additionally re-homes the JSON.parse'd outer map and each per-checklist inner map onto a null-prototype copy (`_clNullProto`) before reading them, and the checklist token lookup guards with `Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(CMH_CHECK_TOKEN, ...)`, so a checklist authored with `data-cmh-checklist="__proto__"`/`"constructor"` (cycled by a plain click) or a crafted `<key>::cl` localStorage payload keyed `__proto__`/`constructor` and valued `__proto__`/`constructor` can never write onto or return `Object.prototype`. No reserved-key skip is added: a checklist id or item key of `constructor`/`__proto__` still works as ordinary author data. | `tests/59-checklist.spec.js` - `CMH-SEC-02: cycling checklists authored with id __proto__ or constructor cannot pollute Object.prototype`, `CMH-SEC-02: a crafted ::cl localStorage payload with __proto__ and constructor keys cannot pollute Object.prototype`; source: `assets/js/36-checklist.js`, `assets/js/37-notes.js` |
| CMH-SEC-03 | Source provenance never exposes directories: `new_document.py`, `deck_scaffold.py`, `retrofit.py`, and `upgrade.py` reduce an explicit, default, or legacy source path to its cross-platform basename before stamping or preserving `data-doc-source`, while the raw identity still seeds `data-comment-key` unchanged. Directory-only inputs become the non-sensitive identifier `document`, and URL query/fragment suffixes are dropped. The runtime likewise reduces an existing `data-doc-source` or `location.pathname` fallback to a basename for Copy all, and every Save, Portable, Offline, and Plain export normalizes the serialized active root (`#commentRoot`, or `<body>` fallback) before preserving it. Committed commentable HTML carries no path-bearing source attributes. | `tests/test_doc_stamp.py` - `test_source_basename_strips_posix_and_windows_directories`, `test_source_basename_never_falls_back_to_sensitive_path_parts`, `test_committed_html_source_provenance_is_basename_only`; `tests/test_new_document.py` - `test_source_provenance_is_basename_only_cmh_sec_03`; `tests/test_retrofit.py` - `test_wraps_body_children_and_strict_validates`, `test_explicit_source_provenance_is_basename_only_cmh_sec_03`; `tests/test_upgrade.py` - `test_upgrade_reduces_legacy_source_path_to_basename_cmh_sec_03`, `test_upgrade_ignores_source_like_text_inside_another_attribute_cmh_sec_03`; `tests/06-save-export.spec.js` - `Copy all and Portable export expose only the source basename (CMH-SEC-03)`; `tests/38-spec-gaps.spec.js` - `body fallback anchors comments when #commentRoot is absent (CMH-CORE-15)`, `body fallback Portable export strips source directories (CMH-SEC-03)`; source: `tools/authoring/doc_stamp.py`, `tools/authoring/new_document.py`, `tools/authoring/retrofit.py`, `tools/authoring/upgrade.py`, `assets/js/01-config.js`, `assets/js/65-export-portable.js` |
| CMH-SEC-05 | HTML exports remove `commentable-html-session-id` and `commentable-html-agent` provenance meta tags by default so a shared Portable, Offline, or Plain copy cannot disclose a correlatable authoring session, including when a quoted meta value contains `>`. The toolbar and sidebar Export menus provide synchronized, unchecked `Retain authoring session provenance` checkboxes for the deliberate exception; checking either preserves the provenance in every downloaded HTML export. | `tests/06-save-export.spec.js` - `HTML exports strip authoring session provenance by default (CMH-SEC-05)`, `both export menus synchronize explicit provenance retention for every HTML export (CMH-SEC-05)`; `tests/test_doc_stamp.py` - `test_stamp_session_escapes_tag_delimiters`; source: `assets/template.shell.html`, `assets/js/65-export-portable.js`, `assets/js/66-export-plain.js`, `assets/js/67-export-standalone.js`, `assets/js/68-export-offline.js`, `tools/authoring/doc_stamp.py` |
| CMH-SEC-04 | Accepted risk (by design, not a finding) - scoped to ONE thing: the runtime's automatic load of the mermaid diagram library from a version-pinned jsDelivr URL (`https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/mermaid@<version>/dist/mermaid.esm.min.mjs`) on the online render path. `build.py` pins that import to the single-sourced `dev/package.json` version (never a floating tag such as `latest`), so the pin fixes WHICH version loads; because it is a dynamic ESM `import()` it cannot carry a Subresource Integrity hash, so the accepted residual risk is trusting jsDelivr to serve untampered bytes for that one pinned URL, not merely the version. This is accepted because the dependency is on a top-tier, world-wide CDN (so this marketplace is only ever collateral, never a target) and a fully zero-network alternative exists via Export Offline, which inlines the vendored `assets/vendor/mermaid.min.js`; the decision is recorded in #451. NOT part of this accepted risk and still in scope for review: Chart.js, which never auto-loads from a CDN (it is vendored/inlined by default and may load from a CDN only on explicit per-document opt-in that requires a pinned version PLUS SRI/crossorigin, CMH-CHART-03); and the security-control rows CMH-SEC-01..03, whose stated properties staying true is a requirement, not an accepted risk. A security scan should treat only this pinned mermaid import as out of scope and re-raise it if the facts change (a different host or path, an unpinned version, a newly auto-loaded CDN dependency, or removal of the offline fallback). | `tests/test_cdn_bydesign.py` - `test_runtime_mermaid_import_is_pinned_jsdelivr`, `test_security_spec_documents_the_accepted_cdn_risk`, `test_vendored_rich_libraries_are_credited`; source: `assets/template.shell.html`, `tools/build_parts/00-imports-version.py` - `read_mermaid_version`, `assets/vendor/UPSTREAM.md` |
| CMH-LICENSE-01 | The shipped `THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md` reproduces, in full, the upstream MIT license text and copyright for every vendored rich-content library redistributed by the skill (mermaid and Chart.js), single-sourced by `build.py` from the vendored `assets/vendor/*.LICENSE` files. It is copied unzipped into the shipped skill dir beside the plugin LICENSE and is gated by `build.py --check`, so a vendor version bump that changes a license or version fails until the notices are refreshed. | `tests/test_third_party_notices.py` - `test_notices_are_generated_shipped_and_in_sync`, `test_notices_reproduce_the_vendored_license_texts_and_versions`, `test_vendored_licenses_are_mit_and_offline_comment_safe`; source: `tools/build_parts/00-imports-version.py` - `build_third_party_notices`, `assets/vendor/*.LICENSE` |
| CMH-LICENSE-02 | Because an Export Offline artifact inlines the vendored library bytes, it is a self-contained redistribution, so the export embeds each inlined library's verbatim MIT notice as an HTML comment beside it (never executed, safe under the offline CSP); the export first strips any pre-existing library notice comment from the head so exactly one notice per inlined library is emitted (never duplicated). `build.py` carries the license texts in the offline bundle single-sourced from the vendored `*.LICENSE` files. | `tests/49-offline-export.spec.js` - `Export Offline embeds the MIT license notice beside each inlined library (CMH-LICENSE-02)`; `tests/test_third_party_notices.py` - `test_offline_bundle_embeds_each_license_verbatim`; source: `assets/js/68-export-offline.js`, `tools/build_parts/10-sources.py` - `build_vendored_rich_libs_json` |
| CMH-A11Y-01 | Help and confirm dialogs expose `role="dialog"`, `aria-modal="true"`, an accessible name, a Tab focus trap, Escape/backdrop close where applicable, and focus restoration to the trigger. | `tests/20-ui-chrome.spec.js` - Help dialog focus-trap and focus-restore (`Help traps Tab focus inside the modal`, `Help returns focus to the trigger button when closed`, `Help opened from the overflow menu returns focus to the menu button`); `tests/19-clear-confirm.spec.js` - `the confirm dialog is an accessible modal that traps Tab and restores focus (CMH-A11Y-01)` |
| CMH-A11Y-02 | The comment composer has an accessible name, a labelled textarea (not placeholder-only), deterministic initial focus to the textarea, Ctrl/Cmd+Enter save, Escape cancel, and focus restoration to the element that opened it. | `tests/33-coverage-gaps.spec.js` - `the composer exposes group semantics and a labelled textarea (CMH-A11Y-02)`, `saving a blank note marks the textarea invalid and keeps the composer open (CMH-A11Y-02)`, `closing a composer returns focus to the diff line that opened it (CMH-A11Y-02)`; source: `assets/js/` - `createComposerElement()`, `closeComposerElement()` |
| CMH-A11Y-03 | Toasts carry `role="status"` or `role="alert"` and `aria-live` so screen readers announce them. The `#toast` element ships as a live region (baked `role="status"`/`aria-live="polite"` in the template) BEFORE any toast fires, so even the FIRST toast of the session is announced - a live region added in the same tick as its first text change is not announced by most screen readers - and `showToast` sets the role/politeness (upgrading errors to an assertive alert) BEFORE mutating the toast text. | `tests/36-a11y.spec.js` - `toasts carry a live-region role so screen readers announce them`, `the toast is a live region before the first toast so the first one is announced (CMH-A11Y-03)`, `showToast sets the live-region role/politeness before mutating the text (CMH-A11Y-03)` |
| CMH-A11Y-04 | The confirm dialog's Tab handler always calls `preventDefault` and, when focus has escaped the two buttons, pulls it back to the default (Cancel), so Tab can never reach the page behind the modal. | `tests/47-hardening.spec.js` - `the confirm dialog always traps Tab and pulls escaped focus back to Cancel (CMH-A11Y-04)`; source: `assets/js/` - `showConfirm()` |
| CMH-A11Y-05 | The composer textarea placeholder documents the save shortcut as `Ctrl/Cmd+Enter`, matching the handler that accepts both `ctrlKey` and `metaKey`, so Mac users see the correct key. | `tests/47-hardening.spec.js` - `the composer placeholder documents the Ctrl/Cmd+Enter save shortcut (CMH-A11Y-05)`; source: `assets/js/` - `createComposerElement()` |
| CMH-A11Y-06 | The disclosure toggles name the element they control via `aria-controls`: the overflow-menu trigger `#btnToolbarMenu` (`aria-haspopup`) points at `#toolbarMenu`, and the comments-panel toggle `#btnToggleSidebar` (`aria-expanded`) points at `#sidebar`, so assistive tech can associate each toggle with its target. | `tests/20-ui-chrome.spec.js` - `the sidebar/menu toggles declare the element they control via aria-controls (CMH-A11Y-06)`; source: `assets/template.shell.html` |
| CMH-A11Y-07 | The layer honors `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`: a global CSS reset clamps every non-essential animation and transition (duration, delay, and multi-iteration loops) to a near-instant single pass, and every programmatic smooth scroll (jump-to-comment, scroll-to-top/bottom, heading deep-link, widget/checklist/notes jumps) routes through a shared `cmScrollBehavior()` helper that returns `auto` under the preference (because a JS `behavior` option overrides the CSS `scroll-behavior`), so motion-sensitive readers get an instant jump. Without the preference, animations keep their authored duration and scrolls stay smooth. Durations are clamped (not removed) so keyframe animations that set a final state still land. The deck slide stage additionally carries the vendored slide engine's own `prefers-reduced-motion` rule (animations instant, a minimal essential slide transition), which is out of scope for this layer reset. | `tests/67-reduced-motion.spec.js` - `reduced-motion clamps a keyframe animation to near-zero, normal keeps it (CMH-A11Y-07)` (duration, delay, iteration-count), `reduced-motion also clamps transitions (CMH-A11Y-07)` (duration, delay), `programmatic smooth scrolling becomes instant under reduced motion (CMH-A11Y-07)`, `programmatic smooth scrolling stays smooth without the preference (CMH-A11Y-07)`, `no layer JS partial hardcodes behavior:"smooth" - all scroll sites route through cmScrollBehavior() (CMH-A11Y-07)`, `cmScrollBehavior() fails closed to "auto" when the preference cannot be determined (CMH-A11Y-07)`; source: `assets/css/95-reduced-motion.css`, `assets/js/00-preamble.js` - `cmScrollBehavior()` |
| CMH-A11Y-08 | Forward Tab from a keyboard-focused heading moves focus to the visible `#headingAddBtn`, the floating button stays visible while it has focus, Shift+Tab returns to the heading, Enter activates the heading comment composer, and a subsequent Tab continues to the next focusable content after that heading (including heading-owned controls and links inside the heading) instead of looping back. | `tests/46-heading-a11y-title.spec.js` - `Tab from a focused heading reaches and activates the Add Comment button (CMH-A11Y-08)`; source: `assets/js/82-toc.js` |
| CMH-A11Y-09 | The text-selection context menu (`#contextMenu`) is a keyboard-operable ARIA menu: the container carries `role="menu"` and each item button carries `role="menuitem"` plus a roving `tabindex="-1"` (only one item is focusable at a time, driven programmatically, so Tab never steps through the items). Opening the menu moves focus to the first VISIBLE item so a keyboard-only reviewer lands on the primary action; ArrowDown/ArrowUp rove focus among the visible items with wrap-around, and Home/End jump to the first/last visible item; Tab and Shift+Tab close the menu from the keydown handler (clearing the saved opener and letting focus move naturally, so the menu is dismissed even when Tab moves focus to browser chrome where the focusout backstop sees a null relatedTarget); focus moving to a real control outside the menu also dismisses it cleanly (focusout backstop) without saving a restore target; and Escape closes the menu and restores focus to the element that was focused when it opened. | `tests/36-a11y.spec.js` - `the selection context menu exposes menu/menuitem roles and focuses its first item on open (CMH-A11Y-09)`, `Escape closes the selection context menu and restores focus to the opener (CMH-A11Y-09)`, `the menu items use a roving tabindex and Tab dismisses the menu without a stray Escape-restore (CMH-A11Y-09)`, `a Tab keydown dismisses the menu even when focus does not move to a page element (CMH-A11Y-09)`, `a Shift+Tab keydown also dismisses the menu (CMH-A11Y-09)`, `Home and End rove to the first and last visible menuitems (CMH-A11Y-09)`; `tests/52-deck.spec.js` - `Arrow keys rove focus between the deck context-menu items (CMH-A11Y-09)` (Arrow + Home/End over two visible items); source: `assets/template.shell.html`, `assets/js/41-selection.js` - `showMenu()` |
| CMH-A11Y-10 | The floating per-link add-comment affordance (`#linkAddBtn`, class `.cm-link-add`) carries the shared themed `:focus-visible` ring like its four siblings (`.cm-mermaid-add`, `.cm-diff-add`, `.cm-image-add`, `.cm-widget-add`), so keyboard focus on it is clearly visible. | `tests/36-a11y.spec.js` - `the link add-comment affordance shows the shared focus-visible ring (CMH-A11Y-10)`; source: `assets/css/80-focus.css` |
| CMH-A11Y-11 | The collapsible-section caret (`.cmh-sec-caret`) shows a themed `:focus-visible` outline ring, so a keyboard reviewer can see which section-collapse control is focused. | `tests/36-a11y.spec.js` - `the section-collapse caret shows a focus-visible ring (CMH-A11Y-11)`; source: `assets/css/20-chrome.css` |
| CMH-A11Y-12 | A sortable table's header cell reflects the active sort direction via `aria-sort` on the parent `<th>` (`ascending` / `descending`), and the attribute is removed when that column resets to unsorted or another column takes the sort, so assistive tech announces which column is sorted and how. | `tests/24-copy-sort.spec.js` - `the sorted column header reflects direction via aria-sort (CMH-A11Y-12)`; source: `assets/js/62-sortable-tables.js` - `_reflectSortIco()` |
| CMH-TOC-06 | Generated heading ids are ASCII word-character slugs de-duped against existing ids; a heading with no slug content (e.g. non-Latin) falls back to `section`, `section-2`, and so on, so authors should supply explicit ids for stable non-Latin anchors. | `tests/38-spec-gaps.spec.js` - `browser heading ids fall back to ASCII section slugs for non-Latin headings (CMH-TOC-06)`; `tests/test_generate_toc.py`; source: `assets/js/`; `tools/authoring/generate_toc.py` |
| CMH-PRINT-01 | Flat documents have a direct print/PDF contract: print media hides runtime chrome (toolbar, sidebar, composers, add-comment controls, highlight bubbles, copy buttons, side TOC, footer, dialogs, toasts, and the chrome injected inside `#commentRoot` itself - section carets, sortable-table sort controls, and section-review badges), expands collapsed sections and notes, resets fixed or shadowed layout chrome into a readable paper flow using the `--cp-*` theme tokens, and materializes a non-`cm-skip` end-of-document comments appendix from the in-memory comments on `beforeprint` / print media entry (thread-aware: one article per thread carrying the root author pill and its replies in order). With JavaScript disabled, a print-only fallback states that live comments require JavaScript to render. Deck print keeps each `.slide` visible and page-broken one slide per page. | `tests/68-print.spec.js` - `CMH-PRINT-01: flat print hides runtime chrome, expands content, and materializes comments`, `CMH-PRINT-01: the print appendix shows the comment author pill and ordered reply refinements`, `CMH-PRINT-01: deck print keeps one slide per page` |
| CMH-PRINT-02 | A discoverable "Save as PDF" action makes the CMH-PRINT-01 print/PDF contract reachable without knowing a keyboard shortcut: a `#btnPrintTop` item in the toolbar overflow ("More actions") menu and a `#btnPrint` item in the sidebar Export menu each call the browser's native `window.print()`, which renders the print/PDF layout - zero PDF dependencies (no jsPDF/html2canvas). It deliberately does NOT intercept `Ctrl/Cmd+P`, so the browser's own print/PDF shortcut keeps working unmodified. | `tests/68-print.spec.js` - `CMH-PRINT-02: the Save as PDF action fires native window.print() from both menus without intercepting Ctrl/Cmd+P`; source: `assets/js/83-print.js`, `assets/template.shell.html` |
| CMH-PRINT-03 | A deck prints as one landscape 16:9 page per slide: a named `@page cmh-deck-slide` sized to the native 1920x1080 stage lets each fixed-size slide fill its own page, so a slide is never clipped by a portrait paper page (the print output does not need to be A4/Letter). Each slide keeps its own authored grid/flex layout in print - `83-print.js` pins each slide's on-screen computed display inline (an inline `!important` overriding the vendored deck engine's print rule that forces every slide to `display: block`, which would flatten a multi-column slide so its columns stack and overflow the fixed box, clipping content). Exactly one page is emitted per slide - no phantom trailing blank page (the injected `<style>`, corner-logo overlay, and the stray `.mermaidTooltip` are hidden) and no slide split across two pages. A deck also keeps its own designed dark code/KQL/diff block backgrounds and bright syntax tokens in print (the report-only light-paper reset and dark-theme token re-light are scoped away from decks, so they never strand a deck's bright tokens on a white background). This 16:9-page fidelity depends on the browser honoring the named `@page` size - it is verified against, and targets, Chromium's native print/PDF (Chrome/Edge, the engine behind the "Save as PDF" action); an engine that ignores a named `@page` size (or a user who forces a specific paper size) may fall back to default paper. | `tests/70-print-pdf.spec.js` - `CMH-PRINT-03: the deck prints one landscape 16:9 page per slide, none clipped or blank`; source: `assets/css/92-print.css`, `assets/js/83-print.js` |
| CMH-PRINT-04 | Reports fall back to clean pagination when a document is too tall for a single page (see CMH-PRINT-06): a heading stays with the content that follows it (so a section title never prints alone on a near-blank page), whole sections flow across pages instead of being forced whole (which reserved an empty page), multi-column galleries and draggable widgets (kanban boards) block-stack so their items flow across pages rather than a grid track jumping whole to the next page, and a tall mermaid diagram is capped to the page height so it scales to fit one page instead of splitting a node across a page break. The board columns and cards survive into the printed PDF, and its chart canvases and mermaid diagrams stay visible in print (never hidden by the print CSS). | `tests/70-print-pdf.spec.js` - `CMH-PRINT-04: reports print with no blank/stranded pages and dense widgets/galleries survive`; source: `assets/css/92-print.css` |
| CMH-PRINT-05 | A flat (non-deck) document prints on light paper regardless of the on-screen theme: when dark theme is active (a common case, since dark-mode readers keep `html[data-theme="dark"]` set), print resets the color-scheme to light (so the browser does not paint the page canvas / `@page` margins dark) and re-lights syntax-highlighted code, KQL, and diff tokens to a light palette, so the keyword/function/string/number tokens stay legible on the white paper background instead of printing as near-invisible dark-mode pastels on a dark page. (A deck instead keeps its own designed dark code/KQL/diff backgrounds and bright tokens in print - the light-paper reset and token re-light are scoped away from decks; see CMH-PRINT-03.) | `tests/70-print-pdf.spec.js` - `CMH-PRINT-05: print re-lights dark-theme code/KQL tokens so they stay legible on the paper background`; source: `assets/css/92-print.css` |
| CMH-PRINT-06 | An eligible flat (non-deck) document prints / Save-as-PDF as a SINGLE continuous no-break page rather than paginating onto A4/Letter sheets, so no page break cuts through a section, table, chart, diagram, or code block. On print entry (`beforeprint` / print-media) `83-print.js` measures the full content at the on-screen reading width - mirroring the synchronous height-affecting print rules (revealed appendix, expanded collapsed sections, wrapped code, reflowed tables, capped media) and growing the width to cover a wider-than-column table - then injects a dynamic `@page { size: W H; margin: 0 }`. Because Chromium LOCKS the `@page` to the measurement taken at `beforeprint` (screen media) and ignores later updates, the height is measured proactively under stable screen media (a ResizeObserver on chart canvases plus a short settle) and cached for print. Documents that contain a block-stacking container - a multi-column gallery (`.visual-grid`) or a grid/flex widget such as a kanban board - are intentionally LEFT ON NORMAL PAGINATION (CMH-PRINT-04): their print-time grid->block reflow and async chart resize cannot be measured before the `@page` lock, so they page normally (content never clipped) instead of risking a bad single page. The injection never leaks into an export: the final rules are `@media print` (inert on screen), emptied on `afterprint`, and exports rebuild from the pristine on-disk/snapshot HTML. Content taller/wider than Chromium's ~200in page clamp also falls back to pagination. Depends on the browser honoring a CSS `@page` size (Chromium's native print/PDF); decks keep their per-slide pages. | `tests/70-print-pdf.spec.js` - `CMH-PRINT-06: an eligible flat document prints as a single continuous no-break page`; source: `assets/js/83-print.js` |
## Build pipeline, validator invariants, and tools

| Feature id | Behavior | Covering tests |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CMH-BUILD-01 | The canonical sources are the numbered topic partials under `assets/css/NN-topic.css` and `assets/js/NN-topic.js`, plus `assets/template.shell.html`. `build.py` assembles the layer CSS and runtime JS by DIRECTORY SORT of those partials (no hand-maintained order list; adding a partial does not edit build.py), concatenating them byte-for-byte and rstripping the final newline once; the sort order is load-bearing (the JS single-IIFE statement order, the CSS cascade). Sources must not be recombined into a `commentable-html.js`/`.css` monolith, and `dist/PORTABLE.html` and `dist/` are generated and should not be hand-edited. | `tests/test_build.py` - `BuildTests.test_generated_files_match_disk`, `BuildTests.test_build_is_idempotent`, `BuildTests.test_inline_template_round_trips_from_shell_and_assets`; `tests/test_assets_split.py` - `test_assembled_js_is_one_arrow_iife`, `test_preamble_partial_is_first_and_captures_the_snapshot`, `test_ordered_parts_are_sorted_nonempty_named_and_numeric`, `test_every_partial_ends_with_a_single_newline`, `test_ordered_parts_rejects_a_stray_unnumbered_file`, `test_exactly_one_js_partial_declares_cmh_version`, `test_build_all_reads_the_passed_assets_dir_not_the_default`, `test_the_monolith_sources_do_not_exist`; `README.md` |
| CMH-BUILD-07 | Every source partial is documented in a sibling `MODULES.md` that maps it to the SPEC feature-id areas it implements, so per-module coverage is auditable. The map is an enforced gate: every partial on disk has exactly one row (no undocumented or stale row), and every SPEC area a module claims is real and test-backed (a matching `AREA-NN` row exists in this spec and names a covering test). | `tests/test_module_coverage.py` - `test_js_modules_map_to_real_tested_spec_areas`, `test_css_modules_map_to_real_tested_spec_areas`; sources: `assets/js/MODULES.md`, `assets/css/MODULES.md` |
| CMH-BUILD-08 | `dev/SPEC.md` is a generated artifact assembled from directory-sorted `dev/spec/NN-*.md` topic partials by `dev/tools/build_spec.py`; `--check` compares the checked file to a fresh assembly and fails on drift, so concurrent spec edits land in topic partials instead of the monolith. | `tests/test_build_spec.py` - `BuildSpecTests`; `scripts/test_rebuild_all.py` - `test_check_runs_build_spec_screenshots_and_site_in_order_with_check_flag`; CI: `.github/workflows/validate.yml` runs `build_spec.py --check` |
| CMH-BUILD-13 | `scripts/check_spec_test_refs.py` verifies that every commentable-html and site SPEC row points only at existing test files and that each quoted test title, class, method, or feature id resolves in that file; it additionally requires every automated test-file clause (a test-file reference followed by ` - `) to cite at least one EXACT test - a JS `test`/`it` title or a Python test method/test-case class - so a prose-only clause, a bare feature id, or a `describe` suite title alone fails the validate gate instead of drifting silently. | `scripts/test_check_spec_test_refs.py` - `SpecTestReferenceTests`, `SpecTestReferenceTests.test_flags_automated_clause_missing_cited_name`, `SpecTestReferenceTests.test_bare_feature_id_alone_does_not_satisfy_strict`, `SpecTestReferenceTests.test_describe_suite_title_alone_does_not_satisfy_strict`, `SpecTestReferenceTests.test_non_test_python_helper_alone_does_not_satisfy_strict`, `SpecTestReferenceTests.test_accepts_single_token_exact_js_title` |
| CMH-BUILD-12 | The Playwright suite is split into two projects (`playwright.config.js`) so CI runs them as independent parallel jobs: `heavy` is exactly the browser-subprocess specs listed in `tests/_projects.mjs` (`HEAVY_SPEC_FILES`, e.g. the tutorial-screenshot capture spec), run in its own sharded job with a bounded worker count so its concurrent capture subprocesses do not thrash a shard; `fast` is every other spec, sharded across runners at the default worker count. The two projects are exact complements (`testMatch`/`testIgnore` over the same globs), so every spec runs in exactly one project - a guard test asserts each `HEAVY_SPEC_FILES` name resolves to a real, unique spec file so a typo can never make the heavy job silently run nothing while that spec thrashes in `fast`. | `tests/00-projects.spec.js` - `every heavy-project spec name resolves to a real, unique spec file (CMH-BUILD-12)`; sources: `plugins/commentable-html/dev/playwright.config.js`, `tests/_projects.mjs`; CI: `.github/workflows/plugin-tests.yml` runs the `fast` and `heavy` projects as separate sharded jobs |
| CMH-BUILD-13 | The `fast` Playwright job is sharded by DURATION, not by Playwright's count-based `--shard`: the CI job passes `PLAYWRIGHT_FAST_SHARD=i/N` and `playwright.config.js` restricts the `fast` project to the specs `tests/_shard.mjs` assigns to shard i by longest-processing-time (LPT) bin-packing over committed per-spec timings (`tests/spec-timings.json`), so every shard's total run time stays within ~1% and no single shard is the bottleneck. Balance is self-maintaining: `npm run shard:timings` regenerates the timings and the assignment rebalances. A guard test asserts the timings list exactly the fast specs (no spec missing a timing, no orphan entry for a deleted spec) and that, for every plausible shard count, the LPT partition covers each fast spec exactly once and the worst shard stays within 1.5x of an even split (a spec too slow to fit its fair share trips this, signalling it should be split). | `tests/00-projects.spec.js` - `fast-project spec timings are complete and shard-balanced (CMH-BUILD-13)`, `LPT shard assignment is deterministic and lossless (CMH-BUILD-13)`; sources: `plugins/commentable-html/dev/tests/_shard.mjs`, `tests/spec-timings.json`, `playwright.config.js`; CI: `.github/workflows/plugin-tests.yml` `playwright` job sets `PLAYWRIGHT_FAST_SHARD` |
| CMH-BUILD-02 | `dev/VERSION` is the single source of truth for the version; `build.py` reads it and stamps the runtime `CMH_VERSION` const, `pkg/plugin.json`, the marketplace entry, and each document's `<meta name="commentable-html-version">`. Companion filenames are version-agnostic, so a version bump does not rename dist files. `dist/manifest.json` records SHA-256 and byte size per companion and is serialized with sorted keys for a deterministic, diff-stable payload. | `tests/test_build.py` - version single sourced, manifest hashes, `test_manifest_is_deterministically_key_sorted`, single declaration tests |
| CMH-BUILD-03 | NonPortable output is materially smaller than inline output and avoids re-emitting boilerplate while still validating cleanly. | `tests/test_build.py` - `BuildTests.test_nonportable_is_much_smaller_than_inline`, `BuildTests.test_both_templates_validate_clean`; `tests/07-nonportable.spec.js` nonportable tests |
| CMH-BUILD-04 | Generated fixtures and examples are kept fresh against current assets and exercise the feature surface. Each shipped `examples/report-*.html` is a pure build artifact assembled by `build.py` from its INDEPENDENT content source in `dev/examples/src/report-*.html`: `build.py` swaps in the current CSS/COMMENT UI/JS layer and re-stamps the `<meta>` version, and `--check` compares the shipped file to a fresh assembly, so a hand-edit or a stale/clobbered committed example - of its CONTENT as well as its layer - fails the build (closing the self-sourced hole the site pages had before #91; content edits must be made in `dev/examples/src/`). `build.py --check --check-fixtures` additionally runs the Playwright fixtures' `generate.mjs --check` (skipped only when node is absent), so the required `dist-in-sync` gate also owns fixture freshness (a version bump that leaves a fixture stale fails the fast gate, not only the slow `plugin-tests` job). | `tests/10-more.spec.js` - `committed sample fixtures are not stale (generate.mjs --check)`; `tests/test_examples.py` - example exists, validates, inlines images, exercises every feature, `test_examples_embed_current_version`, `test_build_check_catches_example_drift`, `test_build_check_flags_an_orphaned_example_with_no_source`; `tests/test_build.py` - `test_check_subprocess_passes`, `test_check_fixtures_passes_and_is_reported` |

| CMH-BUILD-11 | The vendored rich-libraries blob (the inline gzip+base64 mermaid/Chart.js payload) is reproducible across zlib implementations. `build.py` does NOT recompress the libraries at build time (gzip DEFLATE output differs between stock zlib and zlib-ng, which Python 3.14 ships, so a live recompress makes the dist blob depend on the builder's machine and breaks `dist-in-sync`); it reads a committed `assets/vendor/<lib>.gz` artifact and only base64-encodes it, which is deterministic everywhere. `build.py --regen-vendor-gz` regenerates those `.gz` artifacts when a vendored library changes (the only step that gzips), and `build.py --check` fails when a committed `.gz` is missing, corrupt, or does not DECOMPRESS to the current source (decompression is deterministic across zlib impls, so the guard never recompresses). | `tests/test_vendored_gzip.py` - `VendoredGzipReproTests.test_blob_reads_committed_gz_not_recompressed`, `test_drift_guard_detects_missing_and_stale_gz`, `test_regen_roundtrips_through_decompression` |

| CMH-SYN-01 | `tools/validate/validate.py` fails a mermaid sequenceDiagram whose message contains a `;` that splits it into a broken second statement. Once the first `;`-segment is a real signal, the ONLY valid tails are another full signal, a keyword-led statement, or an empty (trailing `;`) tail; ANY other tail - an arrow-without-colon signal, a bare word, or ordinary prose (e.g. `A->>B: hi; take entities + dict bags as-is`) - is flagged, which mermaid rejects as "Syntax error in text". Calibrated to zero false positives: only a NON-keyword-led, non-comment, non-empty tail that is not itself a valid signal is flagged (so a `participant A as "a->b"` alias, a keyword tail, and a valid multi-signal `A->>B: x; C->>D: y` are never flagged); runs as a content check inside `validate()`. | `tests/test_validate_mermaid.py` - `MermaidSequenceRule` (`test_flags_original_bug`, `test_valid_multi_signal_not_flagged`, `test_arrow_in_message_without_semicolon_not_flagged`, `test_participant_alias_with_arrow_not_flagged`, `test_semicolon_then_keyword_not_flagged`, `test_bare_word_tail_is_flagged`, `test_prose_tail_repro_is_flagged`), `MermaidWiredIntoValidate.test_validate_flags_broken_sequence_in_document` |
| CMH-SYN-02 | The Python mermaid checker deep-checks ONLY `sequenceDiagram`; every other diagram family (flowchart, graph, class, state, er, gantt, pie, ...) is recognized but delegated to the repo-side real-parser oracle, so it is never a Python false positive - including constructs earlier heuristics mis-flagged: a flowchart label containing `%%` or a literal `"` in a slash/round/hex shape, or a `click` callback. Within `sequenceDiagram` it stays zero-false-positive by only inspecting a line whose first `;`-segment is a real signal (never a keyword-led directive such as `accTitle:`/`accDescr:`, matched even with no space after the colon and even when its free text contains a post-arrow colon), only flagging non-keyword-led tails (so a `participant`/`activate`/`Note`/`link`/`accTitle:` tail is fine), and neutralizing `%%{init}%%` directives and mermaid character entities (`#59;`, `#quot;`) before the split while treating a line or `;`-segment that starts with `%%`, a single `%`, or `#` as a comment that consumes the rest of the line (mid-line `%`/`#` is literal, and an entity like `#quot;` is decoded first so an entity-led tail is still inspected). | CMH-SYN-03 | The shipped Python mermaid checker has ZERO false positives, proven against a broad (200+ entry) corpus spanning every mermaid diagram family (`tests/fixtures/mermaid-corpus.json`, bulk sources in `mermaid-corpus.src.json`): each entry's `valid` is stamped by the REAL mermaid parser and its `py_flag` by the REAL Python checker, and `build_mermaid_corpus.mjs` FAILS generation if the checker flags any parser-valid diagram, so the checker never flags a diagram the real parser accepts. | CMH-VAL-15 | The validator emits a non-fatal WARNING when a `report` or `plan` section is a wall of prose: a run of `MAX_CONSECUTIVE_LONG` (4) or more consecutive long paragraphs (past a tunable `MIN_LONG_PARAGRAPH_CHARS` length) inside `#commentRoot` with no table, list, figure, diff, chart, or diagram breaking it up, nudging the author toward a real skimmable layout. It uses a dedicated stack-based HTMLParser density pass (not the single-pass token stream), is scoped to `#commentRoot`, ignores `cm-skip` subtrees and paragraphs nested inside layout blocks (an INLINE `cm-skip` inside a paragraph excludes only its own text and does not split it), tracks sections as a heading stack so nested and headless sections are labeled by their own heading and each distinct wall (a run of consecutive long paragraphs) is reported - two walls in the same section separated by a layout block or a nested section are both reported, anchors the `<section>` run-break to a genuinely matched frame (a stray `</section>` never suppresses a wall), resets the run at any layout block / heading / `<section>` boundary (and at a `cm-skip` block), and runs ONLY for `report` and `plan` (`slides`, `board`, `generic`, and a missing or unknown kind do not emit this advisory). The thresholds are tunable via `check_density(html, min_chars=, max_run=)`. | `tests/test_density.py` - `test_cmh_val_15_prose_wall_warns`, `test_cmh_val_15_layout_bearing_section_is_clean`, `test_cmh_val_15_short_paragraphs_are_clean`, `test_cmh_val_15_heading_breaks_the_run`, `test_cmh_val_15_slides_and_board_are_exempt`, `test_cmh_val_15_cm_skip_is_ignored`, `test_cmh_val_15_content_outside_root_is_ignored`, `test_cmh_val_15_threshold_is_tunable`, `test_cmh_val_15_paragraphs_inside_layout_do_not_count`, `test_cmh_val_15_unclosed_paragraphs_still_warn`, `test_cmh_val_15_generic_and_missing_kind_are_exempt`, `test_cmh_val_15_first_kind_meta_wins`, `test_cmh_val_15_whitespace_does_not_inflate_length`, `test_cmh_val_15_short_paragraph_breaks_consecutiveness`, `test_cmh_val_15_cm_skip_block_breaks_the_run`, `test_cmh_val_15_self_closing_layout_breaks_the_run`, `test_cmh_val_15_headless_section_is_not_mislabeled`, `test_cmh_val_15_inline_cm_skip_does_not_split_a_paragraph`, `test_cmh_val_15_block_cm_skip_breaks_run_after_unclosed_paragraph`, `test_cmh_val_15_phrasing_cm_skip_does_not_split_a_paragraph`, `test_cmh_val_15_two_flat_top_level_walls_are_both_reported`, `test_cmh_val_15_section_in_layout_block_does_not_reframe`, `test_cmh_val_15_min_chars_is_tunable`, `test_cmh_val_15_two_headless_walls_are_both_reported`, `test_cmh_val_15_two_walls_in_one_section_are_both_reported`, `test_cmh_val_15_phrasing_cm_skip_between_paragraphs_breaks_run`, `test_cmh_val_15_prose_after_nested_section_uses_outer_heading`, `test_cmh_val_15_stray_close_section_does_not_suppress`, `test_cmh_val_15_wired_into_validate`; source: `tools/validate/checks/density.py`, wired in `tools/validate/validate.py` |
| CMH-VAL-16 | The validator ERRORS in NonPortable mode when a BAKED absolute or `file://` companion reference resolves inside an OS temporary directory that is not the document's own folder, because the OS reaps temp directories and the shared document silently loses its whole layer. Temp detection is cross-platform: the machine's `TMPDIR`/`TEMP`/`TMP` and `tempfile.gettempdir()` roots (symlink-resolved via `realpath`, so macOS `/var`->`/private/var` and Windows 8.3 short names do not diverge) plus root-ANCHORED path fragments (`/tmp`, `/var/tmp`, `/var/folders`, `/windows/temp`, and the per-user `AppData/Local/Temp`), so a durable project folder literally named `tmp` is never mis-flagged. Relative refs bake no absolute location and are never flagged (so the temp-dir-based hermetic tests are unaffected), and a companion sitting BESIDE the document keeps its prior behavior (absolute-path warning / clean `file://`). The message points the author at a Portable single-file export or copying `dist/` to a durable folder. | `tests/test_validate_nonportable.py` - `test_file_url_companion_ref_in_temp_dir_errors`, `test_absolute_companion_path_in_temp_dir_errors`, `test_relative_companion_ref_is_not_temp_flagged`, `test_beside_document_absolute_temp_path_is_not_temp_flagged`, `test_project_tmp_folder_absolute_ref_is_not_temp_flagged`, `test_cross_machine_mac_temp_fragment_errors`, `test_cross_machine_windows_temp_on_posix_form_errors`, `test_temp_absolute_ref_errors_with_base_dir_none`; source: `tools/validate/checks/resources.py` - `_is_temp_path()`, `_same_dir()`, `_canonical()`, `_check_nonportable()` |
| CMH-KIND-01 | Every document declares its kind in a mandatory `<meta name="commentable-html-kind">` whose content is one of `report`, `plan`, `slides`, `board`, or `generic`. The validator errors when the meta is missing or the kind is unknown, and enforces that title-bearing kinds (`report`, `plan`) carry a TOP-LEVEL `<h1>` as a direct child of `#commentRoot` (a lede-wrapped `<header class="cmh-lede"><h1>` counts) while `slides`, `board`, and `generic` do not; an `<h1>` nested only inside a deeper `<section>` does not satisfy the rule and is an error, and a top-level lede whose `<h1>` is empty (no non-empty title text) is also an error rather than passing on the lede wrapper class alone. | `tests/test_validate_kind.py` - `test_missing_kind_meta_errors`, `test_unknown_kind_errors`, `test_report_without_h1_errors`, `test_plan_without_h1_errors`, `test_report_with_h1_is_clean`, `test_report_with_nested_only_h1_errors`, `test_report_with_lede_wrapped_h1_is_clean`, `test_report_with_empty_lede_errors`, `test_slides_without_h1_is_clean`, `test_board_without_h1_is_clean`, `test_generic_without_h1_is_clean`, `test_kind_is_case_insensitive` |
| CMH-KIND-02 | `new_document.py` and `retrofit.py` require `--kind`, stamp the `commentable-html-kind` meta, and only auto-add a document title from `--label` for kinds that carry one (`report`/`plan` add it when the fragment has none; `slides`/`board` do not). `retrofit.py` detects an existing kind meta by parsing head metadata order-independently (including a non-canonical `content`/`name` attribute order) and REPLACES it in place rather than appending a duplicate, so retrofitting `--kind` onto a host that already declares a kind yields exactly one meta with the requested kind. `new_document.DOC_KINDS` stays in sync with `validate._DOC_KINDS`. | `tests/test_new_document.py` - `KindTests` (`test_doc_kinds_match_validate`, `test_kind_is_required`, `test_kind_stamps_meta`, `test_slides_kind_does_not_add_title`, `test_report_kind_adds_title_and_validates`, `test_make_document_stamps_kind`); `tests/test_retrofit.py` - `test_kind_is_stamped_and_required`, `test_existing_kind_meta_is_replaced_not_duplicated`, `test_reordered_existing_kind_meta_is_replaced` |
| CMH-KIND-03 | `upgrade.py` migrates a document that predates the kind meta by adding a default `generic` kind, so upgrading an older document yields one that declares a kind and validates. | CMH-KIND-05 | Every document should declare a favicon so a browser tab (especially a full-screen one) shows the commentable-html mark instead of the generic globe. The template shell bakes a `<link rel="icon" href="data:image/svg+xml,...">` into the head, and the validator emits a non-fatal WARNING (enforced under `--strict`, the mandatory finalize path) when a document has no favicon in its head. A favicon is a head-level `<link>` whose `rel`, split on whitespace, contains the exact token `icon` (so `icon` and `shortcut icon` count, but `apple-touch-icon` / `mask-icon` do not) with a non-empty `href`; an icon link in the body or with an empty `href` does not satisfy the check. The authoring tools inject the CMH favicon when the host lacks one: `retrofit.py` adds it to a host head with no favicon (and never duplicates an existing one), and `upgrade.py` migrates a pre-favicon document by adding the template's favicon. Detection is shared (`tools/authoring/_favicon.py`, an `HTMLParser`-based helper matching the validator) so the tools inject exactly when the validator would warn: it is token-exact, head-scoped, ignores links inside `<script>` / `<style>` / comments, does not read `data-rel` / `data-href` as `rel` / `href`, and is not vulnerable to the quadratic blowup a raw-regex scan would have. A host declaring only `apple-touch-icon` still gets a real favicon. | `tests/test_validate_kind.py` - `FaviconTests` (`test_favicon_present_is_clean`, `test_missing_favicon_warns`, `test_empty_href_favicon_warns`, `test_shortcut_icon_rel_is_accepted`, `test_apple_touch_icon_does_not_count`, `test_body_only_favicon_warns`); `tests/test_retrofit.py` - `FaviconHelperTests` (rel-token, head-scoping, data-attribute, script/style/comment, and no-quadratic-blowup cases), `test_favicon_is_added_when_host_head_has_none`, `test_existing_host_favicon_is_not_duplicated`, `test_apple_touch_icon_only_host_still_gets_a_real_favicon`; `tests/test_upgrade.py` - `test_missing_favicon_is_added_on_upgrade`, `test_existing_favicon_is_not_duplicated_on_upgrade`, `test_apple_touch_icon_only_document_gets_a_real_favicon_on_upgrade` |
| CMH-TOOL-01 | `tools/validate/validate.py` is a standard-library CLI that validates one or more HTML files, supports `--charts-only` and `--layer-only`, prints usage on `-h`/`--help`, treats a bare `--` as an end-of-options separator (a following dash-led token is a path, not a flag), treats warnings as exit 0 by default, exits non-zero on any warning under `--strict`, and continues through batches. | `tests/test_validate_cli.py` - `ValidateCliTests`, `ValidateMainTests` (`test_help_flag_prints_usage`, `test_double_dash_separator_treats_following_token_as_path`, ...); `tests/test_validate_self_contained.py` - `NewCheckTests` `--strict`; `tests/test_validate_charts.py` - `ChartValidatorTests.test_cli_ok_exit_zero`, `ChartValidatorTests.test_cli_warnings_only_exit_zero`, `ChartValidatorTests.test_cli_error_exit_one`, `ChartValidatorTests.test_cli_batch_mixed_exits_one`, `ChartValidatorTests.test_cli_usage_exit_two` |
| CMH-HL-02 | `tools/blocks/highlight_document.py` bakes syntax highlighting into every raw, language-labelled `<pre><code class="language-XXX">` block of a file in one pass (aliases resolved, e.g. `cs` -> `csharp`), rewriting only blocks whose language the highlighter supports and whose inner is still raw (no `cmh-code-*` spans, no markup); an already-highlighted block, an inline `<code>`, a non-highlightable label, and an empty block are left untouched, and re-running is a no-op. It exposes a `--check` mode (exit 1 if any block needs highlighting) and a `-` stdin/stdout mode. `tools/authoring/finalize.py` runs this step by DEFAULT (opt out with `--no-highlight`) before validation, so the standard finalization bakes highlighting. | `tests/test_highlight_document.py` - full unit suite (`HighlightDocumentTests`); `tests/test_finalize.py` - `test_highlights_code_blocks_by_default`, `test_no_highlight_flag_skips_highlighting`; source: `tools/blocks/highlight_document.py`, `tools/authoring/finalize.py` |
| CMH-HL-04 | The document-producing tools bake syntax highlighting by DEFAULT (opt out with `--no-highlight`) and surface validator warnings instead of discarding them, so a freshly created document is never raw and a skipped-highlight/skip warning is visible at creation. This is the root-cause prevention for a raw `language-XXX` block shipping unbaked because baking used to live only in the separate, manual finalize step. `new_document.py` and `deck_scaffold.py` bake then print warnings (they do not block on warnings); `retrofit.py` bakes then fails closed on any validator warning, so it never writes a raw document. | `tests/test_new_document.py` - `test_cli_bakes_syntax_highlighting_by_default`, `test_cli_no_highlight_flag_leaves_code_raw`, `test_cli_surfaces_validator_warnings`; `tests/test_retrofit.py` - `test_bakes_syntax_highlighting_by_default`, `test_no_highlight_with_raw_code_is_blocked_by_validation`; `tests/test_deck_example.py` - `test_scaffold_bakes_highlighting_by_default`, `test_scaffold_no_highlight_leaves_raw_and_warns`; source: `tools/authoring/new_document.py`, `tools/authoring/retrofit.py`, `tools/deck/deck_scaffold.py` |
| CMH-STAMP-01 | The document-producing tools stamp a `<meta name="commentable-html-created">` provenance timestamp so the runtime can tell a produced document apart from a strict-validated one. `new_document.py` and `deck_scaffold.py` stamp it (idempotently, honoring `--generated` for deterministic metadata); creation never claims validation (no validated stamp is written at creation). | `tests/test_doc_stamp.py` - `DocStampUnitTests` (set/get/insert/idempotent/escape), `test_new_document_stamps_created`, `test_new_document_does_not_claim_validation`; source: `tools/authoring/doc_stamp.py`, `tools/authoring/new_document.py`, `tools/deck/deck_scaffold.py` |
| CMH-STAMP-02 | `validate.py` and `finalize.py` write a `<meta name="commentable-html-validated">` timestamp AND a `<meta name="commentable-html-validated-hash">` content signature into the file ONLY on a STRICT-CLEAN FULL pass (no errors AND no warnings, and both the layer and chart halves ran - a `--charts-only`/`--layer-only` partial run never stamps), so the validated stamp means the document actually passed the whole strict validator. Stamping works when `validate.py` runs as a standalone CLI subprocess (it puts the sibling `authoring/` tools on `sys.path` via the shared bootstrap so `_stamp_validated_file`'s `import doc_stamp` resolves), not only in-process; `validate.py` is not read-only. A stamp-write failure is a visible NOTE, never silent, and never blocks validation. `--no-stamp` keeps a pure/CI run read-only (no write); a doc with a warning is not stamped. | `tests/test_doc_stamp.py` - `test_validate_stamps_validated_on_clean_file`, `test_validate_no_stamp_flag_leaves_file_unstamped`, `test_validate_does_not_stamp_a_doc_with_warnings`, `test_validate_partial_modes_never_stamp`, `test_validate_cli_subprocess_stamps_validated_and_content_hash`, `test_validate_cli_subprocess_no_stamp_flag_is_read_only`, `test_validate_reports_a_visible_note_when_stamping_fails_but_still_passes`; `tests/test_finalize.py` - `test_stamps_validated_on_a_strict_clean_finalize`, `test_no_stamp_flag_skips_the_validated_stamp`; source: `tools/validate/validate.py` - `_stamp_validated_file`, `tools/authoring/finalize.py` |
| CMH-STAMP-03 | On load, the runtime shows a small, dismissible amber fallback banner when a document carries a `commentable-html-created` stamp but no CURRENT `commentable-html-validated` stamp - the stamp is absent, older than the created stamp, OR content-bound (has a `commentable-html-validated-hash`) whose hash no longer matches the live document's authored TEXT (a post-validation text edit). A strict-validated document whose authored text still matches, and any document with no created stamp (older/foreign), show nothing. The banner is `cm-skip` chrome added to `CMH_INJECTED_CHROME`, so it never bakes into a Save/Export snapshot and is re-derived on load. This is a last resort; the skill MUST always finalize and strict-validate. | `tests/64-validation-banner.spec.js` - `an unvalidated document shows the fallback banner`, `a validated document shows no banner`, `a validation older than creation still shows the banner`, `a document with no created stamp shows no banner`, `the banner is dismissible`, `the banner does not leak into a Plain export`; source: `assets/js/38-validation-banner.js`, `assets/css/87-validation-banner.css` |
| CMH-STAMP-04 | The document-producing tools stamp the creating AI agent's session id as `<meta name="commentable-html-session-id">` (and its agent slug as `commentable-html-agent`) so the footer can copy it (CMH-FOOT-04). `new_document.py` and `deck_scaffold.py` stamp it BY DEFAULT from `--session-id` or, when that is absent, an auto-detected environment variable (`COPILOT_AGENT_SESSION_ID` -> copilot, `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID`/`CLAUDE_SESSION_ID` -> claude); `--agent` overrides the slug and `--no-session-id` opts out. When several agents' session ids are visible at once (a nested launch), the agent that is the immediate runtime wins via its marker env var (`CLAUDECODE` vs `COPILOT_CLI`). The session stamp is idempotent (a re-scaffold preserves the original producer) and a blank id is a no-op. | `tests/test_doc_stamp.py` - `DocStampUnitTests` (`test_stamp_session_sets_id_and_agent`, `test_stamp_session_is_idempotent`, `test_stamp_session_blank_is_noop`, `test_detect_session_reads_copilot_env`, `test_detect_session_reads_claude_env`, `test_detect_session_reads_claude_legacy_env`, `test_detect_session_prefers_copilot_when_both_present_and_no_runtime_marker`, `test_detect_session_prefers_the_running_agent_when_both_present`, `test_detect_session_absent_is_none`), `NewDocumentSessionStampTests`; `tests/test_deck_scaffold.py` - `test_scaffold_stamps_session_from_flag`, `test_scaffold_stamps_session_from_environment_by_default`, `test_scaffold_no_session_id_flag_suppresses`; source: `tools/authoring/doc_stamp.py`, `tools/authoring/new_document.py`, `tools/deck/deck_scaffold.py`; local live checks (not CI; drive the real agent CLIs, shared core `tests/_session_e2e.py`): `tests/copilot_e2e_check.py`, `tests/claude_e2e_check.py` |
| CMH-STAMP-05 | The validated stamp is CONTENT-BOUND to the document's authored TEXT: `validate.py`/`finalize.py` write `commentable-html-validated-hash`, a whole content-root text hash computed with the shared section-hash contract (`section_hash.document_content_hash` - the same FNV-1a and cm-skip/script/style/template/noscript/diff/KQL/mermaid/canvas/note exclusion the section-review hashes use, over the first `id=commentRoot` subtree only, matching the runtime `getElementById`), and the runtime (`cmhDocContentHash` in `84-section-review.js`, exposed as `window.__cmhReview.docHash()`) reproduces it byte for byte. It is a stable-text fingerprint (a strong nudge, not a cryptographic seal): a post-validation edit to the visible authored TEXT re-shows the banner and re-validation clears it, but attribute-only edits and edits inside the excluded rendered blocks are not tracked. A document with no content root gets a timestamp-only stamp (no hash) and the runtime falls back to the timestamp signal so it never false-positives; a reader's persisted table sort is canonicalized (unsorted before hashing) so it does not falsely invalidate, while a genuine content rearrangement (widget/board drag baked into an export) legitimately does. `finalize.py` prints a guardrail reminder on a strict-fail that the fix must end with a clean strict pass to re-stamp. | `tests/test_section_hash_golden.py` - `DocumentContentHashTests`; `tests/test_doc_stamp.py` - `ValidatedHashStampTests`, `test_validate_stamps_content_hash_matching_the_document`, `test_validate_cli_subprocess_stamps_validated_and_content_hash`, `test_validate_partial_modes_never_stamp`; `tests/test_finalize.py` - `test_warnings_fail_with_strict`; `tests/64-validation-banner.spec.js` - `the runtime exposes a stable whole-document content hash`, `a validated document whose content hash matches shows no banner`, `a validated document whose content hash no longer matches shows the banner`, `validate.py content-binds the stamp end to end (Python/JS hash parity, edit re-shows, re-validate clears)`, `the Python and runtime document hashes agree on rich content (kitchen-sink parity)`, `in-root noscript is excluded from the hash on both sides (JS/Python parity)`, `a persisted table sort does not falsely invalidate the stamp (canonical hash)`; source: `tools/authoring/section_hash.py` - `document_content_hash`, `tools/authoring/doc_stamp.py` - `stamp_validated_html`, `assets/js/84-section-review.js` - `cmhDocContentHash`, `assets/js/38-validation-banner.js` |

| CMH-TOOL-06 | `tools/build.py` regenerates inline and nonportable outputs, re-stamps the shipped examples' embedded layer regions and `<meta>` version from the fresh `dist/PORTABLE.html`, re-stamps the version into the Copilot AND Claude manifests (`plugin.json` and marketplace entries under `.github/plugin/` and `.claude-plugin/`) so a bump never leaves the Claude mirror behind, removes stale dist companions (including legacy versioned files left by older builds), prints a size report, and `--check` fails on drift (including a stale example). | `tests/test_build.py` - check subprocess, stale dist, generated output, idempotence, manifest tests, and `test_source_stamps_include_claude_manifests`; `tests/test_examples.py` - `test_build_check_catches_example_drift` |
| CMH-TOOL-07 | `tools/authoring/new_document.py` produces a **NonPortable** document by default (companion refs repointed to the skill's `dist/` by a relative path, or `--copy-assets` to copy them next to the file, or `--assets-href` for a custom prefix) and a single self-contained file with `--portable`; it replaces only the CONTENT fragment and the `#commentRoot` data attributes, refuses demo keys (except when re-stamping an existing document via `allow_reserved_key`), updates the `<title>`, reads a fragment from a file or UTF-8 stdin, and self-validates before writing. NonPortable output to stdout (no `--out`) uses absolute `file://` companion refs by default, while `--assets-href` and `--portable` keep their explicit modes. | `tests/test_new_document.py` - clone, content swap, demo-key rejection, allow-reserved-key, active-root-attrs, stdin, title, NonPortable default/`--copy-assets`/`--assets-href`, `test_nonportable_stdout_uses_absolute_file_urls`, `test_nonportable_stdout_ok_with_assets_href`, `test_nonportable_stdout_ok_when_portable`, `--portable`, mode mutual-exclusion, and validation tests |
| CMH-TOOL-08 | `tools/authoring/upgrade.py` upgrades a standalone document by replacing the CSS, COMMENT UI, and JS regions (preserving handled ids, embedded comments, content, and the root) and re-emitting the shell-baked mermaid loader bootstrap (see CMH-MMD-09), validates the result in a temp file, and leaves the target unchanged if validation fails or the validator crashes. It also restamps the `<head>` `<meta name="commentable-html-version">` to the template's runtime version (inserting it when a pre-version legacy document lacks it) so an upgraded file no longer self-reports the old version; the restamp is scoped to `<head>` so the marker-like JS export regex literal (`content="[^"]+"`) is never matched or rewritten. Post-upgrade validator warnings are surfaced (not discarded) and the upgrade still commits by default so a version-only upgrade is never blocked by a pre-existing content warning; the opt-in `--strict` flag treats those warnings as a failure, leaving the target unchanged and exiting non-zero. A region boundary is matched only on a COMPLETE marker line (the keyword phrase alone on its line, optionally wrapped by its comment delimiter and/or a `====` banner, with nothing else on the line), so an inline mention or a line-leading marker phrase carrying trailing authored text cannot corrupt a swap. The shipped upgrader, maintainer build tool, and runtime export slicers reject duplicate BEGIN or END markers for every layer region instead of silently slicing through authored content. It also preserves the input file's DOMINANT newline (detected from the raw bytes) through the transform, so a Windows-authored (CRLF) document is not silently normalized to LF on upgrade. | `tests/test_upgrade.py` - region swap, preservation, stale `--check`, validation-failure-leaves-target-unchanged, validator-crash-surfaced, marker-line tests, duplicate BEGIN/END tests, `test_version_meta_is_bumped_to_template_version`, `test_version_meta_is_inserted_when_missing`, `test_version_meta_noop_when_already_current`, `test_version_meta_bump_ignores_js_regex_literal`, `test_cli_surfaces_warnings_without_strict`, `test_cli_strict_aborts_on_warning_and_leaves_target_unchanged`, `test_cli_strict_commits_when_clean`, `test_cli_preserves_crlf_line_endings`, `test_cli_keeps_lf_when_lf_is_dominant_despite_a_stray_crlf`; `tests/test_build.py` - `BuildTests.test_region_inner_rejects_duplicate_begin_marker`, `BuildTests.test_region_inner_rejects_duplicate_end_marker`, `BuildTests.test_region_inner_ignores_marker_text_inside_pre_content`; `tests/06-save-export.spec.js` - `Export plain strips the layer but keeps the content`; `tests/52-region-guard.spec.js` - `Export Plain aborts on a duplicated EMBEDDED COMMENTS BEGIN marker (F28)`, `Export Plain aborts on a duplicated EMBEDDED COMMENTS END marker (F37)`, `Export Offline aborts on a duplicated EMBEDDED COMMENTS BEGIN marker in a nonportable doc (F28)`, `Export Offline aborts on a duplicated EMBEDDED COMMENTS END marker in a nonportable doc (F37)` |
