| CMH-ASCII-01 | The document producers rewrite AI "smart" typography - em/en dashes, an ellipsis glyph, curly single/double quotes, and non-breaking / zero-width spaces - to their plain-ASCII equivalents in visible PROSE, leaving VERBATIM regions (`<script>`, `<style>`, `<pre>`, inline `<code>`, HTML comments, CDATA, declarations/PIs) and tag markup (element names AND attribute values) untouched, so no report, plan, or deck ships off-style typography while machine data and code samples are never corrupted. `tools/authoring/normalize_typography.py` is the shared, idempotent, HTML-aware normalizer (a quote-aware `html.parser` tokenizer with a verbatim-element stack that fails closed on unterminated code and is linear on malformed input), with a `--check`/`--out`/in-place CLI and a plain-text `normalize_text()` helper for literal strings; `finalize.py` runs it by default (opt out `--no-normalize`) for reports/plans and `deck_scaffold.py` runs it by default on slide prose and on the plain-text `--label`/`--source`. Because the rewrite is length-changing it is a PRODUCER step meant to run BEFORE a document is first reviewed (normalizing prose after comments exist could shift their stored offsets), and it also flattens a legitimately intentional non-breaking space. | `tests/test_normalize_typography.py` - `NormalizeProseTests.test_replaces_each_ai_character_in_prose`, `VerbatimRegionsTests.test_pre_and_code_are_untouched`, `VerbatimRegionsTests.test_script_and_style_are_untouched`, `VerbatimRegionsTests.test_html_comment_is_untouched`, `HtmlAwarenessTests.test_quoted_gt_in_attribute_does_not_leak_into_prose`, `HtmlAwarenessTests.test_doctype_and_pi_with_quoted_gt_are_untouched`, `HtmlAwarenessTests.test_self_closing_verbatim_protects_tail`, `HtmlAwarenessTests.test_misnested_verbatim_stays_protected`, `HtmlAwarenessTests.test_many_unclosed_tags_are_linear`, `HtmlAwarenessTests.test_many_unmatched_end_tags_are_linear`, `NormalizeProseTests.test_idempotent`, `PlainTextTests.test_normalize_text_treats_input_as_literal`, `CliTests.test_check_reports_and_leaves_file`, `CliTests.test_in_place_rewrites`; `tests/test_finalize.py` - `test_normalizes_ai_typography_in_prose_by_default`, `test_no_normalize_flag_preserves_ai_typography`; `tests/test_deck_scaffold.py` - `test_scaffold_normalizes_ai_typography_in_slide_prose`, `test_scaffold_normalizes_ai_typography_in_label`, `test_no_normalize_flag_preserves_ai_typography` |
| CMH-TOOL-15 | `tools/authoring/retrofit.py` injects the full commentable layer into an existing unlayered HTML file, using parsed `<head>` and `<body>` insertion points, a CSS-only theme-vars block, a unique non-demo key, the content-root descriptor contract, optional `--root-selector "#id"`, matching NonPortable asset options, `--portable`, and `--skip-selectors`; it accepts valid HTML5 optional-end-tag closures while rejecting malformed non-optional closures, keeps JS outside the CONTENT markers, avoids nested `<main>` by wrapping with `<div>` when the body already has a main element, refuses already-layered or ambiguous documents, self-closing non-void roots, and implicitly closed roots, ignores fake container tags inside comments or scripts, warns on likely CSS collisions, validates before writing, and leaves the target unchanged when validation fails. It also bakes the report/plan document-overview strip (CMH-STATS-01, opt out `--no-stats`) and de-dups an author-numbered ordered-list `.cm-toc` (CMH-TOC-10) before validating, and preserves the input file's DOMINANT newline (detected from the raw bytes) so a Windows-authored (CRLF) host is not silently normalized to LF on retrofit. | `tests/test_retrofit.py` - happy path and direct output contract, root-selector including empty and closed elements, portable output, CSS-only theme block, implicit HTML5 closures, malformed non-optional closure rejection, existing-main wrapping, self-closing and implicitly closed root refusal, fake comment/script tags, demo-key and duplicate-root guards, already-layered refusal, malformed/ambiguous head/body, selector miss/multi-match, non-UTF-8 input, validation failure atomicity, skip-selectors, CSS collision warnings, strict validation, doc-stats default and opt-out, ordered-list TOC number de-dup, `test_preserves_crlf_line_endings`, and `test_keeps_lf_when_lf_is_dominant_despite_a_stray_crlf` tests |
| CMH-TOOL-HELP-01 | Every shipped CLI tool under `tools/<topic>/` (discovered recursively across the topic buckets; the `deck_common` shared library has no CLI and is excluded) answers `--help` and `-h` with exit 0 and prints usage text to stdout, so discovering a tool never treats the flag as a filename or fails. A tool that parses argv itself honors a `--` end-of-options separator: `-h`/`--help` is treated as a request for help only before a bare `--`, and any `-h`/`--help` after it is a positional data value (filename/query/id), not help. | `tests/test_cli_help.py` - `test_there_are_tools_to_check`, `test_every_tool_answers_help_with_exit_zero`, `test_help_flag_before_end_of_options_triggers_help`, `test_help_flag_after_end_of_options_is_data`, `test_dash_h_after_separator_is_treated_as_data_end_to_end` |
| CMH-TOOL-LAYOUT-01 | The shipped tools are grouped into per-topic buckets under `tools/<topic>/` (`deck`, `kusto`, `checklist`, `blocks`, `authoring`, `validate`); the `tools/` root holds only the `_toolpath.py` sys.path bootstrap. Each tool imports its siblings by bare name and resolves shipped resources (`dist/`, `vendor/`) through `tools/_toolpath.py`, which puts the tools root and every topic subdirectory on `sys.path` and exposes `SKILL_ROOT`, so a tool keeps working regardless of which bucket it lives in. Every expected tool exists at its bucket path, no tool is left flat at the `tools/` root or is unlisted, and every tool imports cleanly by bare name. | `tests/test_tools_layout.py` - `test_every_expected_tool_exists_at_its_bucket_path`, `test_tools_root_holds_only_the_bootstrap_and_buckets`, `test_no_shipped_tool_is_unlisted`, `test_every_tool_imports_by_bare_name` |
| CMH-TOOL-IMPORTS-01 | Guarded sibling-tool imports in the shipped tools are never SILENT (the #584 root-cause class was a deferred `import doc_stamp` swallowed by `except (OSError, ImportError): pass`). Across the WHOLE shipped tools tree: (1) every guarded sibling import RESOLVES in the shipped layout, so a missing/renamed sibling fails loudly here instead of silently degrading a feature at runtime; (2) any handler that would catch an import failure for a sibling-tool import - `except ImportError`/`ModuleNotFoundError`, or a broad `except Exception`/`BaseException`/bare except that would swallow one - makes the failure VISIBLE: it calls `_toolpath.warn_missing_tool`, unconditionally re-raises, or recovers by re-importing. Merely `pass`ing / assigning a sentinel / returning is forbidden (a later broad `except Exception` after an `except ImportError` that already consumed the import failure is exempt; the `_toolpath` bootstrap and stdlib imports are not sibling tools). The structural check enforces this for future code too. | `tests/test_tool_imports.py` - `test_no_shipped_import_fallback_is_silent`, `test_every_guarded_sibling_import_resolves_in_the_shipped_layout`, `test_warn_missing_tool_writes_a_visible_stderr_warning`, `test_a_module_level_fallback_warns_when_a_sibling_is_missing`; source: `tools/_toolpath.py` - `warn_missing_tool` |

## Claude Code compatibility

commentable-html output is a portable HTML artifact that works with any agent, and the plugin is installable in Claude Code as well as the GitHub Copilot CLI. It ships a `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` beside the Copilot `plugin.json` and is listed in the repo-root `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` (a subset mirror of `.github/plugin/marketplace.json`). The auto-updater is dual-agent too (its own `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` and Claude `hooks/hooks.json`), so both published plugins install in either agent.

| Feature id | Behavior | Covering tests |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CMH-CLAUDE-01 | The plugin is installable in Claude Code: it ships `pkg/.claude-plugin/plugin.json` whose identity fields (name, version, description, author, license, keywords) mirror the Copilot `pkg/plugin.json`, declares `"skills": "./skills/"` which resolves to the shipped skill, and appears in the repo-root `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` (whose entries mirror the matching `.github/plugin/marketplace.json` entries by name/version/source). `scripts/validate_claude_compat.py` checks this structurally and, when the `claude` CLI is present, runs `claude plugin validate --strict` on the marketplace and each Claude plugin dir. | `tests/test_claude_manifest.py` - `test_claude_plugin_manifest_ships`, `test_identity_fields_mirror_the_copilot_manifest`, `test_skills_path_resolves_and_skill_ships`; `scripts/test_validate_claude_compat.py` - `test_compatible_repo_has_no_errors`, the structural-failure tests, and `test_repo_manifests_are_claude_compatible` |
| CMH-HARNESS-01 | `dev/tools/skill_flow_harness.py` is a LOCAL-ONLY regression harness that drives the installed `copilot` executable non-interactively through the skill's create / retrofit / deck / export / validation-handoff routes (copying the shipped `pkg/skills/commentable-html` into `<ws>/.github/skills/` and extracting its `skill-resources.zip` so `copilot -C <ws> --add-dir <ws>` discovers the full shipped skill with file access SCOPED to the workspace, not `--allow-all-paths`) and then RE-VALIDATES each produced document READ-ONLY with the repo's own tools - `validate.py --strict --no-stamp`, `deck_validate.py --strict` for the deck, a self-contained (no companion asset) plus embedded-review-layer check for the portable export, and on EVERY flow the AGENT-written `<meta name="commentable-html-validated">` stamp (the harness never writes it, so a skipped finalize/validate handoff is caught). A timeout is a hard FAIL; a completed non-zero exit with a strict-valid artifact is PASS with a surfaced note. It HARD-refuses to run under CI (in `main()` and again in `run_flow`), is wired into no workflow, clamps every path under the gitignored `tmp/skill-flow-harness/` (leading-separator / `..` / zip-slip / symlink / out-of-root `run_dir` escapes are rejected), and never lets one flow's error abort the batch. The flow corpus is `dev/tools/skill_flow_prompts.py`; usage and limitations are in `dev/tools/skill_flow_harness.md`. Because a live model run is non-hermetic and consumes credits, only the harness PLUMBING is automatically tested; the end-to-end model invocation is an intentional manual coverage gap. | `tests/test_skill_flow_harness.py` - `CiGuardTests`, `ScratchTests`, `CommandTests`, `ContentCheckTests`, `SafePathTests`, `WorkspaceTests`, `CorpusTests`, `PathTests` |
## Deck integration (frontend-slides)

The commentable-html skill incorporates the third-party frontend-slides design system (MIT, Zara Zhang) as NATIVE CMH deck theme presets: curated preset profiles under `dev/skill/tools/deck/themes/` recolour the deck stage, components, and reusable recipe classes with system-font, contrast-safe tokens, so a themed deck needs no per-deck CSS and no per-deck hand-translation. A curated, pristine subset of the upstream skill is still vendored under `dev/skill/vendor/frontend-slides/` (enforced pristine by a required CI gate) as design provenance and the maintainer refresh source, and to supply the inlined `viewport-base.css` and the PPTX extractor. The author-time deck tools live under `dev/skill/tools/deck/`, and the runtime deck profile lives in the layer (activated only by `data-cmh-mode="deck"`, so non-deck documents are unaffected). See `references/deck-contract.md` for the runtime interface contract.

| Feature id | Behavior | Covering tests |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CMH-DECK-01 | The skill detects a presentation/deck request ("slide deck", "presentation", "pitch deck", "convert this ppt") and CONFIRMS with the user before building a real deck rather than silently producing a flat document; if the user declines it falls back to a normal flat document. This is an agent-behavior convention in `SKILL.md` ("Deck capability"), so its coverage is `manual`. | `manual` - documented in `SKILL.md`; agent-behavior convention, not automatable |
| CMH-DECK-02 | `deck/deck_scaffold.py` produces a valid, create-only, commentable-native fixed-stage deck: `#commentRoot` carries `data-cmh-mode="deck"` and a `commentable-html-kind` of `slides`, the body holds `.deck-viewport > .deck-stage` with `viewport-base.css` inlined, every slide is a `<section class="slide">` with a unique stable `data-slide-id` and the first is `.active`, the deck body has no inline editor / remote fonts / host script, ids are deterministic, and it refuses to overwrite an existing output without `--force`. Before writing it runs BOTH the base validator AND the deck contract (`deck_validate.deck_checks`) and fails closed (writing nothing) on any contract violation; its deck-mode injection targets the real content root (never the template decoy) idempotently. | `tests/test_deck_scaffold.py` - `test_scaffold_is_valid_and_commentable_native`, `test_slides_have_stable_ids_first_active`, `test_deck_body_has_no_editor_fonts_or_script`, `test_content_ids_preserved_and_missing_ones_minted`, `test_deterministic_ids`, `test_create_only_then_force`, `test_fragment_without_slides_errors`, `test_existing_ids_and_inject_targets_real_root`, `test_scaffold_fails_closed_on_remote_media`, `test_scaffold_declares_slides_kind`, `test_scaffold_has_legible_presentation_defaults` |
| CMH-DECK-03a | `deck/pptx_to_fragment.py` HTML-escapes extracted slide text (no `<script>` / `</b>` / `-->` injection; RTL and emoji preserved), emits one `<section class="slide">` per slide with a stable id, ignores speaker notes, and the local `--pptx` fallback fails closed without `python-pptx`. It also schema-validates the extracted JSON (each slide/content-block/image is an object; `content`/`images` are lists), rejects an empty slide list and a slide with no title/text/image, and vets every image `path` as local-relative OR a self-contained `data:image/...` URI (rejecting a remote/other scheme, `//host`, absolute, or `..` traversal; normalizing backslashes; skipping an empty path). The `--pptx` fallback inlines each extracted image as a `data:` URI before its temp dir is deleted, so a converted deck never ships a dangling `assets/...` reference. | `tests/test_pptx_to_fragment.py` - `test_hostile_text_is_escaped`, `test_notes_are_ignored`, `test_structure_and_stable_id`, `test_duplicate_slides_get_distinct_ids`, `test_cli_stdin_to_stdout`, `test_non_list_input_errors`, `test_pptx_fallback_fails_closed`, `test_remote_and_unsafe_image_paths_rejected`, `test_data_image_uri_is_accepted`, `test_local_pptx_inlines_extracted_images_as_data_uri`, `test_inline_local_images_leaves_odd_and_missing_paths`, `test_backslash_image_path_normalized`, `test_empty_image_path_skipped_not_error`, `test_empty_slide_list_rejected`, `test_slide_with_no_content_rejected`, `test_non_dict_slide_rejected`, `test_non_list_content_or_images_rejected`, `test_non_dict_content_block_or_image_rejected`, `test_cli_empty_list_exits_nonzero` |

| CMH-DECK-04 | `deck/deck_validate.py` passes a scaffolded deck and fails closed on each deck-contract violation. Active-content and egress checks are HTML-parser based (not regex), so they cannot be bypassed by a solidus attribute separator (`<svg/onload=>`), an entity-encoded scheme (`&#106;avascript:`), an unquoted attribute (`<img src=//evil>`), or an SVG `<image>`/`<use>` `href`. It rejects: missing `data-cmh-mode`, a remote font reference, remote media/resource (`img`/`video`/`audio`/`source`/`track`/`image`/`use`/`iframe`/`embed`/`object`/`link`/`base` fetch attr or CSS `url()` to `http(s):` / `//`), a remote CSS `@import`, an inline event handler, `<iframe>/<object>/<embed>`, an external `<script src/href>` or any `<script>` nested in `<svg>` (an inline chart-init `<script>` is allowed), a `<meta http-equiv=refresh>` redirect, a dangerous URL scheme (`javascript:`/`vbscript:`/`data:text/html`), a `../` asset reference, a `<deck-stage>` component, or a duplicate slide id. It tolerates a local-relative image, a `data:` image URI, an external hyperlink (`<a href>` is not egress), and a local `<link href>`; it does not false-positive on the plain-text token "edit-toggle" in prose. The content region is anchored on the full HTML-comment markers and uses the LAST end marker, so slide text containing the bare marker text cannot truncate validation. It also runs the base validator. | `tests/test_deck_validate.py` - `test_valid_deck_passes`, `test_missing_deck_mode_fails`, `test_remote_font_fails`, `test_event_handler_fails`, `test_iframe_fails`, `test_javascript_url_fails`, `test_parent_traversal_fails`, `test_deckstage_component_fails`, `test_duplicate_slide_id_fails`, `test_cli_passes_on_valid_deck`, `test_remote_media_fails`, `test_remote_css_url_fails`, `test_local_media_and_data_uri_pass`, `test_unquoted_javascript_and_traversal_fail`, `test_edit_toggle_plaintext_is_not_a_false_positive`, `test_solidus_and_whitespace_separated_event_handler_fails`, `test_entity_encoded_javascript_url_fails`, `test_unquoted_remote_media_fails`, `test_svg_image_and_use_remote_href_fails`, `test_html_image_alias_background_and_image_set_egress_fail`, `test_external_hyperlink_is_allowed`, `test_injected_end_marker_does_not_truncate_validation`, `test_begin_marker_without_close_reports_missing_region`, `test_duplicate_active_content_is_deduplicated`, `test_svg_and_external_scripts_fail_inline_chart_script_allowed`, `test_stray_svg_close_and_benign_meta_are_handled`, `test_remote_link_base_and_meta_refresh_fail_local_link_allowed` |

| CMH-DECK-12 | The shipped `tools/validate/cmhval/contrast.py` utility parses CSS colors (`#rgb`, `#rrggbb`, `rgb()`/`rgba()`, simple `var()` values, and common names), computes the WCAG relative-luminance contrast ratio, and flags pairs below the configurable 4.5:1 default. It now also resolves computed descendant text color against the nearest authored background in the DOM (for cases such as a link whose `color` lives on the link but whose dark background lives on its caption container), and checks connector/arrow `stroke` contrast against the visible background with a 3.0:1 floor. `deck/deck_validate.py` applies it to the deck CONTENT region for explicit inline `style` pairs, CSS rules whose same declaration block sets `color` plus the last effective `background` or `background-color`, computed element/background pairs, connector strokes, and deck theme variable pairs (`--slide-fg` with `--slide-bg`/`--stage-bg`); errors name the selector, element, or variable pair and both colors so low-contrast authoring defects fail before publish. The parser rejects non-finite and malformed `rgb()`/`rgba()` colors, ignores colors embedded only inside `url(...)` or quoted strings while preserving real fallback colors, and skips backgrounds whose final backdrop still cannot be resolved. | `tests/test_contrast.py` - `test_cmh_deck_12_black_white_ratio_is_21_to_1`, `test_cmh_deck_12_equal_colors_ratio_is_1_to_1`, `test_cmh_deck_12_parser_supports_hex_rgb_rgba_and_vars`, `test_cmh_deck_12_parser_rejects_non_finite_rgb_channels`, `test_cmh_deck_12_parser_rejects_malformed_rgb_arity`, `test_cmh_deck_12_finds_low_and_good_pairs`, `test_cmh_deck_12_finds_configured_variable_pairs`, `test_cmh_deck_12_skips_semi_transparent_backgrounds`, `test_cmh_deck_12_background_shorthand_uses_declaration_order`, `test_cmh_deck_12_background_url_text_is_not_a_color`, `test_cmh_deck_12_color_in_quoted_string_content_is_not_a_color`, `test_cmh_deck_12_background_url_fallback_color_is_detected`, `test_cmh_deck_12_finds_computed_kql_run_link_contrast`, `test_cmh_deck_12_fixed_kql_run_link_contrast_passes`, `test_cmh_deck_12_finds_low_contrast_connector_stroke`, `test_cmh_deck_12_fixed_connector_stroke_passes`; `tests/test_deck_validate.py` - `test_cmh_deck_12_low_contrast_css_pair_fails_with_selector`, `test_cmh_deck_12_background_shorthand_overrides_background_color`, `test_cmh_deck_12_low_contrast_theme_variables_fail`, `test_cmh_deck_12_non_finite_rgb_does_not_crash`, `test_cmh_deck_12_semi_transparent_background_is_skipped`, `test_cmh_deck_12_good_contrast_inline_pair_passes`, `test_cmh_deck_12_kql_run_link_with_computed_background_fails`, `test_cmh_deck_12_low_contrast_connector_stroke_fails` |

| CMH-TUT-SHOTS-01 | `dev/tools/capture_tutorial.mjs` (dev-only, not shipped) regenerates every tutorial screenshot that `docs/TUTORIAL.md` embeds with one command (`npm run shots`), driving a registry of scenes: the community-garden report at a fixed 1320x900 (2x) viewport into `docs/assets` as `garden-*.png` (sixteen shots, covering the review badge, the side-TOC search/review-filter/expand/scroll navigator, the comment search-and-sort panel, the export menu, a collaboration thread showing the "Commenting as" identity control with per-author pills and a reply, and the comment formatting toolbar plus a rendered rich comment, alongside the original nine), plus one shot each from dedicated examples for content types the garden lacks - `triage-01-board` (kind=board), `checklist-01-checklist`, and `note-01-note`. `npm run shots:check` captures into repo-root `tmp/` and compares against the committed images without rewriting them, failing on missing or stale screenshots; `npm test` and `python scripts/rebuild_all.py --check` run that drift check so stale committed screenshots are gated. The tool pins the capture clock, random seed, viewport, locale, timezone, motion preferences, browser font rendering flags, CSS animation state, and capture fonts, then writes each screenshot to disk raw and full-resolution (crisp, true-color, with no downsampling or color quantization) and judges freshness by applying that determinism normalization (downsample plus color-quantize) to BOTH the committed and the freshly-captured image only at compare time, with a bounded pixel-diff tolerance, so the committed tutorial images stay crisp while repeated captures stay in sync across browser environments even when Chromium text rasterization jitters by a few pixels. The comment-search panel shot (`garden-13-comment-search`) is clipped to the height of its populated content rather than the full fixed sidebar, so it carries no dead whitespace, and the Help/About shot (`garden-07-help`) is clipped to the dialog bounds with the overlay dim/blur neutralized so it is a clean, crisp dialog rather than a dimmed, backdrop-blurred full page. Optional positional args `[example] [outDir] [prefix]` capture or check a single scene by prefix (default `garden`, basename-sanitized), and `--print-paths` resolves the defaults without launching a browser. See `dev/README.md` ("Regenerating tutorial screenshots"). The tool-behavior tests run in a dedicated `heavy` Playwright project (its own sharded CI job) so their browser-launching capture subprocesses do not thrash a shard; the all-scenes `--check` freshness run is not duplicated in the suite (the heavy-job shard 1/3 `shots:check` step already runs it). | `tests/54-tutorial-shots.spec.js` - `--print-paths resolves the shipped tutorial defaults (CMH-TUT-SHOTS-01)`, `regenerates every garden shot into a nested out dir, and --check passes on fresh output (CMH-TUT-SHOTS-01)`, `capturing into an already-populated out dir overwrites without erroring (CMH-TUT-SHOTS-01)`, `garden capture is deterministic across two independent runs (CMH-TUT-SHOTS-01)`, `--check flags a stale garden shot (CMH-TUT-SHOTS-01)`, `regenerates the ${scene.prefix} scene and --check passes on fresh output (CMH-TUT-SHOTS-01)`, `${scene.prefix} capture is deterministic across two independent runs (CMH-TUT-SHOTS-01)`, `--check flags a stale ${scene.prefix} shot (CMH-TUT-SHOTS-01)`, `the no-arg default run's shot registry matches the spec's scene lists (CMH-TUT-SHOTS-01)`; `scripts/test_rebuild_all.py` - `test_check_runs_build_spec_screenshots_and_site_in_order_with_check_flag` |
| CMH-TUT-QUALITY-01 | `dev/tools/check_shot_quality.mjs` (dev-only, not shipped) gates the quality of the committed tutorial screenshots under `docs/assets`. It checks exactly the shots the capture tool declares (via `capture_tutorial.mjs --print-paths`), so a missing declared shot fails and a stray non-shot PNG is ignored. Reading the static committed PNG bytes (never a fresh capture, so the metrics are deterministic across platforms), it fails the build if any shot is color-quantized/faded (fewer than 2% off-grid pixels, the signature of the old downsample+quantize normalization), smooth-blurred (variance-of-Laplacian sharpness below a floor, skipped for sub-3px strips), under-resolved (either side below a 64px minimum-dimension floor; an exact-resolution regression such as a 1x recapture is additionally pinned by `capture_tutorial.mjs --check`), left with excess blank vertical margin (contiguous blank top+bottom rows above a fraction, judged only for shots at least 400px tall so a short element strip's inherent padding is exempt; horizontal blank is not gated because full-width badge/checklist strips legitimately have narrow content), or washed with a load-flash yellow color-cast (fraction of strong-yellow pixels above a threshold that a legitimate small yellow highlight stays under). `npm run shots:quality` runs it standalone, `npm run shots:quality --report` prints the per-shot metrics for calibration, and `npm test` runs the covering spec, so a low-quality shot can never reach the published tutorial again. | `tests/55-shot-quality.spec.js` - `the committed tutorial screenshots pass the quality gate (CMH-TUT-QUALITY-01)`, `the quality gate rejects a degraded (downsampled + color-quantized) screenshot (CMH-TUT-QUALITY-01)`, `the quality gate rejects a smooth-blurred (non-quantized) screenshot (CMH-TUT-QUALITY-01)`, `the quality gate rejects an under-resolved screenshot (CMH-TUT-QUALITY-01)`, `the quality gate rejects a shot with excess blank vertical margin (CMH-TUT-QUALITY-01)`, `the quality gate rejects a yellow-cast (load-flash) screenshot (CMH-TUT-QUALITY-01)` |

| CMH-DECK-31 | In deck mode a plain, trusted, unmodified left-click on EMPTY slide space advances the deck to the next slide in BOTH present mode and the open review panel, so a reviewer can page through by clicking empty space. A click whose point is over rendered text never advances (the reader may be selecting it to comment), nor does a click on an interactive/effect target (links, buttons, form controls, ARIA widgets, focusable custom controls, `[data-cm-part]`, comment anchors, `[data-cmh-no-advance]`, `cm-skip` deck chrome); `elementFromPoint` at click time uses the true release target so a press-on-empty / release-on-control gesture does not advance. ## Coverage gaps

The following features are documented but lack full automated behavior coverage, or have a known mismatch between spec and documentation:

**Remaining manual or design-only gaps:**
- CMH-HARNESS-01: the harness PLUMBING (CI guard, scratch-under-tmp, prompt corpus, copilot command construction, validator wiring) is automated in `tests/test_skill_flow_harness.py`, but the END-TO-END live model invocation (`copilot -p` actually driving each skill flow) is non-hermetic, consumes AI credits, and is non-deterministic, so it is a deliberate manual, local-only check - never a CI gate.
- CMH-DECK-01: deck-intent detection and the "confirm before building a real deck" gate are an agent-behavior convention in `SKILL.md` ("Deck capability"), not an automated test.
- CMH-DECK-03b: the "prefer the Anthropic `pptx` skill when installed, fall back to the local extractor" preference is prompt-level wording in `SKILL.md`; only the deterministic escaping and the local fallback (CMH-DECK-03a) are automated.
- CMH-CONTENT-05: ADO resource links in authored content are a manual authoring convention with no validator or E2E enforcement.
- CMH-CONTENT-06: prose cross-reference links are only partially covered by TOC anchor tests.
- CMH-KQL-01: a framed KQL figure (`figure.cmh-kql`) is now hard-enforced to carry a Run in Azure Data Explorer link (CMH-KQL-07, a validator error). Bare, unframed `<pre>` KQL blocks are now also enforced (CMH-KQL-08): they must be marked `data-cmh-kql-no-cluster` when there is genuinely no cluster to run them on, so a missing cluster is a deliberate choice, not an accidental omission.
- CMH-CHART-05: chart recipe semantics, dark chart palette choices, and data hygiene rules are documentation and review conventions, not automated tests.
- CMH-WIDGET-18: widget drag suppresses text selection inside draggable cards by design; drag behavior is covered, but the selection-suppression decision itself is a manual UX contract.
- CMH-NET-01 / CMH-MMDLOAD-01: CDN opt-in and local vendoring choices are manual. CMH-MMDLOAD-01 now has E2E coverage for the default CDN path; manual guidance for vendor/self-host advice remains.

**Documentation/implementation mismatches (spec is correct; docs or tooling do not reflect it):**
- CMH-COPY-06: `references/limitations.md` implies all copy paths have the `prompt` fallback; only Copy all does. The per-block no-prompt rule now has E2E coverage, but the docs still need alignment outside this tests-only pass.
- CMH-SEC-01: the "callers must sanitize untrusted host HTML before wrapping" rule is stated in the spec and is now documented in `SKILL.md` (the "Trust boundary (MUST)" note) and `new_document.py --help`; it still has no automated enforcement in the validator or tooling.
## Packaging and first-run extraction

The plugin ships a compact `skills/commentable-html/` (only `SKILL.md`, `LICENSE`, `THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md`, and a single `skill-resources.zip`) so the CLI installer writes very few files. This matters on Windows, where Defender briefly locks each file as it is written and the installer aborts the whole install on the first transient lock (the `Access is denied. (os error 5)` failure) - a large file count almost never gets through. The bulky runtime (`tools/`, `references/`, `dist/`, `vendor/`) is assembled by `build.py` into `skill-resources.zip`, and a SessionStart hook unpacks it on first run per version, retrying the same transient lock the installer does not. The large tutorial (`docs/`) and worked examples (`examples/`) are NOT shipped; they live at the plugin top level and online. The editable + built skill STAGE tree lives at `dev/skill/`.

| Feature id | Behavior | Covering tests |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CMH-PKG-01 | The extractor unpacks every member of `skill-resources.zip` into the skill dir and writes a version-stamped `.skill-resources-<version>.ok` marker only after all members succeed; the marker's own final rename retries a transient lock so a fully-successful extraction is never wasted, and a zip carrying no installable runtime directory (truncated/empty/wrong) raises and writes no marker so the next session self-heals. | `tests/test_extract_resources.py` - `test_first_extraction_unpacks_all_members_and_writes_marker`, `test_write_marker_retries_transient_lock_on_final_rename`, `test_zip_without_installable_directories_writes_no_marker` |
| CMH-PKG-02 | A present version marker makes a re-run a no-op: no member is extracted (the hot path does no work). The marker check tests for a real FILE, so a directory that shares the marker name does not short-circuit extraction. | `tests/test_extract_resources.py` - `test_present_marker_makes_rerun_a_noop`, `test_missing_zip_is_a_soft_noop`, `test_marker_named_directory_does_not_skip_extraction` |
| CMH-PKG-03 | A transient file lock (Defender: `PermissionError` / `WinError 5/32/33/145` / `errno EACCES/EBUSY`) is retried per-member with exponential backoff, within time budgets under the hook timeout, until it clears; the zip OPEN itself is retried too (the fresh zip is what Defender scans), and an exhausted budget aborts without hanging. | `tests/test_extract_resources.py` - `test_transient_lock_is_retried_until_success`, `test_retries_winerror_and_errno_lock_variants`, `test_winerror_lock_is_retried`, `test_winerror_dir_not_empty_and_lock_pending_are_retried`, `test_zip_open_is_retried`, `test_budget_exhausted_aborts_without_marker` |
| CMH-PKG-04 | An unrecoverable lock (retries/budget exhausted) leaves NO marker, so the next SessionStart re-extracts (self-heal); a partial extraction never looks complete; a non-lock error is not retried. | `tests/test_extract_resources.py` - `test_unrecoverable_lock_leaves_no_marker`, `test_non_lock_error_is_not_retried` |
| CMH-PKG-05 | Extraction goes to a private staging dir and installs each top-level runtime dir transactionally (rename-aside, move-in, roll back with lock-retry on failure) under one shared time budget, so a stale marker is cleared, files removed/renamed in the new version do not linger, a FAILED upgrade (even mid-swap, even with a transient lock during rollback) leaves the previous version's files fully intact, and a crash that leaves a backup but no live dir is recovered on the next run; a fully-successful swap that then hits a locked orphan backup still writes the marker (the backup is cleaned next run), and read-only/cleared-bit trees are made writable (restoring the directory execute bit on POSIX, not S_IWRITE only) so cleanup can remove them. On an in-place upgrade from a pre-1.132 tree, the obsolete `docs/` and `examples/` dirs the package no longer ships are pruned (only when the new zip does not itself install them), so the runtime converges to the minimal tree. | `tests/test_extract_resources.py` - `test_stale_marker_is_cleared_and_new_one_written`, `test_removes_files_absent_from_new_version`, `test_failed_upgrade_leaves_previous_version_intact`, `test_swap_rollback_restores_previous_version_on_mid_swap_failure`, `test_rollback_survives_transient_lock_during_restore`, `test_cleanup_restores_backup_when_live_dir_is_missing`, `test_make_writable_restores_execute_bit_on_directories`, `test_successful_swap_survives_locked_orphan_backup`, `test_swap_rollback_on_rename_aside_failure`, `test_clear_markers_removes_tmp_leftover`, `test_upgrade_prunes_legacy_docs_and_examples`, `test_legacy_prune_keeps_a_dir_present_in_the_new_zip`, `test_clear_markers_removes_pid_suffixed_tmp` |
| CMH-PKG-06 | `--force` re-extracts even when the current marker is present. | `tests/test_extract_resources.py` - `test_force_reextracts_even_with_marker_present` |
| CMH-PKG-07 | The shipped skill dir is minimal - only `SKILL.md`, `LICENSE`, `THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md`, and `skill-resources.zip`. | `tests/test_session_hooks.py` - `test_shipped_skill_dir_is_minimal` |
| CMH-PKG-08 | `skill-resources.zip` carries exactly the runtime dirs (`tools/`, `references/`, `dist/`, `vendor/`) and neither the tutorial (`docs/`) nor the examples; it carries no `__pycache__`/`*.pyc` junk and unpacks to a working tools tree. A traversing/absolute zip member is rejected (fail closed) on top of stdlib zip-slip sanitisation, and the swap installs only real directories whose names are not reserved (case-insensitively) nor a backup-suffix collision, so a zip cannot overwrite a shipped control file (SKILL.md, LICENSE, THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md, the zip, markers) and a plain file at the staging root is never installed.
